gmw_00001782.name: 4C 12.50
gmw_00001782.overview: 4C 12.50 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1809.07588] and ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1809.07588, arXiv:1609.01291] hosted in a major merger system with a double nucleus [arXiv:1607.06937, arXiv:1609.06539]. The galaxy features long tidal tails [arXiv:1609.06539, arXiv:2311.15009] and hosts a multiphase molecular outflow driven by its radio jet [arXiv:2009.02750, arXiv:2403.08869].

sources.summary: The paper finds PKS B2 1345+125 has extended X-ray emission, strong intrinsic absorption, soft thermal emission, and halo-scale emission aligned with optical structure and the VLBI jet.

quotes.quote: Fig. 1345 shows a smoothed ACIS-S image of the galaxy, PKS B2 1345+125 (z=0.122) with the extended emission on the 10 arcsec scale ( 20 kpc). A detailed analysis of this structure is given in Guainazzi et al (in preparation).

For example PKS B2 1345+125 has a large absorption column (N _H =2.54^ +0.63 _ 0.58 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) and a soft X-ray excess, Q0134+329 is better described by a two component power law model, while Q0740+380 shows a possible absorption line (but in this case the signal to noise is low in this part of the spectrum and calibration uncertainties are large enough that we cannot claim this line detection). We applied a two component model to the PKS B2 1345+125 X-ray spectrum, e.g. an absorbed power law and a bremstrahlung emission, to determine the luminosity of the soft X-ray thermal emission.

The diffuse X-ray emission in PKS B1345+125 may originate as a thermal emission associated with the galaxy halo. The size of the extended X-rays is of order 20 kpc and it agrees with the size of the extended emission line region of < 20 kpc studied in optical holt,axon . identified three kinematically distinct emission line regions in this source. They associated the narrow line component that was the most extended one with the quiescent ISM in the galaxy halo. The X-ray emission is elongated towards the South-West similarly to the optical emission and it also agrees with the VLBI jet axis stan2001 , suggesting that it is somehow related to the expanding GPS source.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 12.50 as a major-merger GPS/narrow-line radio galaxy whose broad H shares the blueshift and width of [O III], indicating a narrow-line rather than BLR origin.

quotes.quote: 4C 12.50 . 4C 12.50 (PKS 1345+12) is hosted in a major merger system which is still ongoing 1986Natur.321..750G . The GPS nuclei was classified as a narrow line radio galaxy (NLRG) by 1977ApJ...215..446G . However, it has very broad [ O iii ] lines. The broad [ O iii ] can be fitted by two Gaussian profiles (Figure fig5 ). Recent analysis of its SDSS spectrum showed a broad H component 2012ApJ...757..140S .

Our results show that the broad H has the same blueshift (about 1549 km s ^ 1 ) with the blueshifted [ O iii ] doublet. And its FWHM (2130 km s ^ 1 ) is also consistent with the blue wing of [ O iii ] . Thus the detected broad H corresponds to the blue shifted narrow line component, but not originates from broad line region (BLR).

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F13451+1232/PKS1345+12 as a radio-loud merging system whose compact nuclear [OIII] emission and multi-phase outflows are likely jet-driven.

quotes.quote: IRAS F13451+1232 (PKS1345+12) IRAS F13451+1232 (PKS1345+12) is a well studied object that shows evidence for outflows in warm, neutral and molecular gas holt03,morganti05,holt11,dasyra12,morganti13 . Deep HST imaging and ground-based spectra for PKS1345+12 are discussed in holt03 , batcheldor07 and . We note that the AGN in this object is radio-loud ( P_ 1.4GHz = 1.9 10^ 26 W Hz ^ 1 m ^ 2 ) with a compact, high-surface-brightness inner radio structure that includes a jet that has a maximum radial extent of 130pc to the S along PA180 morganti13 , as well as a more diffuse extended radio structure with a total extent of 150 kpc in a roughly N-S direction stanghellini05 . Given the similarity between the spatial extent of the inner radio structures and those of the warm, neutral and molecular outflows in this source, there is a strong possibility that the outflows are driven by the relativistic jets (see the discussion in batcheldor07 and morganti13 ). The HST continuum image shown in Figure 1 is dominated by a double nucleus of separation 2.1 arcsec (4.5 kpc) which represents the cores of the two merging galaxies in the system.

The deep HST [ OIII ] image of F13451+1232 has a better resolution and spatial sampling than those of the other objets in the sample, because it was taken with the high resolution camera (HRC) rather than the WFC of the ACS instrument. Even with this better spatial resolution, the nuclear [ OIII ] emission of F13451+1232 is barely resolved: although the emission is slightly elongated roughly in the direction of the radio jets, the first Airy ring is visible in the image. In addition to the compact core, an arc feature is detected 1.2 arsec ( 2.5 kpc) to the NW of the nucleus in the [ OIII ] image.

sources.summary: The paper presents 4C12.50 as an infrared/radio-active gas-rich merger whose ALMA CO emission reveals a rotating molecular disk, merger streams, high-velocity filaments, and a tentative molecular wind.

quotes.quote: The nearby system 4C12.50, also known as IRAS 13451+1217 and PKS 1345+12, is a merger of gas-rich galaxies with infrared and radio activity. It has a perturbed interstellar medium (ISM) and a dense configuration of gas and dust around the nucleus. The radio emission at small ( 100 pc) and large ( 100 kpc) scales, as well as the large X-ray cavity in which the system is embedded, are indicative of a jet that could have affected the ISM.

The CO emission reveals the presence of gaseous streams related to the merger, which result in a small ( 4kpc-wide) disk around the western nucleus. The disk reaches a rotational velocity of 200 , and has a mass of 3.8( 0.4) 10 ^9 . It is truncated at a gaseous ridge north of the nucleus that is bright in . Regions with high-velocity CO emission are seen at signal-to-noise ratios of between 3 and 5 along filaments that radially extend from the nucleus to the ridge and that are bright in \ and stellar emission. A tentative wind detection is also reported in the nucleus and in the disk.

sources.summary: The source 1345+125 has likely WISE contamination, a 24 micron luminosity increase, distorted morphology/interaction signs, Sy/CSO classification, and possible ULIRG classification.

quotes.quote: Contamination in the WISE color classification for the following sources is likely: 0116+319 , 1345+125 , 1358+624 , and 1607+268 , all marked in Table table:1 . These sources have galaxies and/or stars closer than 6.5'' , and as such would contain some level of excess flux in all four WISE bands and result in a shift in the positioning on our diagnostic plot. These four cases can be viewed in Figure fig:contamination , which displays the WISE 12 m image and the respective Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) color composite image of each source.

0710+439, 1345+125, 1607+268, and 1934-638 show an increase of 140-210 in the 24 m band, are all classified as Sy, and are all morphologically classified as CSO. We note these four sources for further investigation in a follow-up paper, as potentially interesting sources to inspect spectroscopically. th WISE Colors and Host Galaxy Morphologies Figure fig:WISE presents the WISE color diagnostic plot, with different symbols reflecting the WISE color identification following Wright10 . Clearly, such an identification does not serve as a rigorous classification, as it only points out the most likely origin of the detected MIR fluxes, corresponding to either the ISM with various levels of the star formation activity (G, SB) or the AGN component (Q/Sy). Note in this context the overlaps between different types of galaxies in the WISE color classification: for example 1345+125, denoted here as ``Sy'', can additionally be classified as Ultraluminous Infrared Galaxy ULIRG; e.g., Rodriguez09 .

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J134733.36+121724.2 as a well-studied GPS merger host with two reddened cores, long tidal tails, dust/gas-rich nucleus, and radio-core association.

quotes.quote: SDSS J134733.36+121724.2 is a well-studied merger host galaxy of a GPS source Evans_1999, Emonts_2016 . Based on and i band images from the Isaac Newton Telescope, Stanghellini_1993 suggested early that this object is a merger between a Seyfert galaxy and an elliptical radio galaxy. The deeper images from the Legacy Surveys clearly indicate an intermediate stage of a major merger with long tidal tails typically produced when at least one disk galaxy is involved. There are two separate bright cores at a projected distance of 5 kpc. The optical colours of both cores indicate strong reddening.

The reddening is stronger for the western core, which is close to the position of the radio core. The HST image (Fig. fig:HST ) reveals more details, such as dust absorption structures and bright knots close to the western core. The detection of the galaxy in both HI absorption, CO emission and as a bright IRAS source suggests the presence of large amounts of dust and cold gas in the nucleus of this GPS source and references therein Stanghellini_1993 .

sources.summary: The paper presents F13451+1232/4C12.50 as a merging ULIRG with young AGN activity, detecting a circumnuclear molecular disk and compact high-velocity outflow likely accelerated by its small-scale jet.

quotes.quote: The ultra luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) F13451+1232 is an excellent example of a galaxy merger in the early stages of active galactic nucleus (AGN) activity, a phase in which AGN-driven outflows are expected to be particularly important. However, previous observations have determined that the mass outflow rates of the warm ionised and neutral gas phases in F13451+1232 are relatively modest, and there has been no robust detection of molecular outflows. Using high spatial resolution ALMA CO(1--0) observations, we detect a kiloparsec-scale circumnuclear disk, as well as extended ( 440 pc), intermediate-velocity (300 |v| 400 km s ^ 1 ) cold molecular gas emission that cannot be explained by rotational disk motions. If interpreted as AGN-driven outflows, the mass outflow rates associated with this intermediate-velocity gas are relatively modest ( M _ out =22 - 27 M _ yr ^ 1 ); however, we also detect a compact ( r_ out 120 pc), high velocity (400 v 680 km s ^ 1 ) cold molecular outflow near the primary nucleus of F13451+1232, which carries an order of magnitude more mass ( M _ out 230 M _ yr ^ 1 ) than (and several times the kinetic power of) the previously-detected warmer phases. Moreover, the similar spatial scales of this compact outflow and the radio structure indicate that it is likely accelerated by the small-scale ( 130 pc) AGN jet in the primary nucleus of F13451+1232.

An excellent object to investigate the impact and launching mechanism(s) of AGN-driven outflows is the ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG; L_ IR 10^ 12 L _ : Sanders1996 ) F13451+1232 --- also known as 4C12.50 --- which is a merger ( Gilmore1986, Heckman1986 ) in a late pre-coalescence phase (nuclear separation 4.5 kpc: Tadhunter2018 ). ULIRGs such as F13451+1232 are among the most rapidly evolving galaxies in the local universe due to merger-induced gas inflows feeding the central AGN and triggering star formation --- the exact situation modelled in some hydrodynamic simulations of galaxy evolution that include AGN-driven outflows as a feedback mechanism (e.g. DiMatteo2005, Hopkins2008 ). As a luminous radio source (L _ 1.4 GHz = 1.9 10^ 26 W Hz ^ 1 ), F13451+1232 contains radio emission on two distinct scales: small-scale emission within a radius of 130 pc of the primary nucleus along position angle PA=151 ^ Stanghellini1997, Lister2003, Morganti2013_4c1250 , and larger-scale, diffuse emission extending to a radial distance of 77 kpc from the primary nucleus along PA 180 ^ Stanghellini2005 .

sources.summary: The paper presents 1345+125 as a GPS ULIRG in an apparent merger, with two nuclei, Seyfert 2 emission, complex radio structure, and near-UV components detected in both nuclei.

quotes.quote: 1345+125 : 4C +12.50 . A long known peaked spectrum radio source e.g., Veron71 . The counterpart of this GPS source is a m _R =15.5 galaxy e.g., Stanghellini93 at z = 0.12174 Holt03 , in a cluster of fainter galaxies Stanghellini93 . IR images of this well known ULIRG show an extremely reddened source with two nuclei separated 1.8 ( 4 kpc) embedded in a common envelope and aligned roughly East-West e.g., Scoville00, Surace00 suggesting an ongoing merger, which may have triggered the AGN e.g., Heckman86, Xiang02 . The western nucleus is the brightest and shows a Seyfert 2 spectrum Gilmore86 .

VLBI imaging shows a complex, distorted 100 mas ( 0.2 kpc) source e.g., Lister03 , roughly oriented North-South (PA 20 ). Older observations of 1345+125 related the radio s ource to the East nucleus but improved astrometry showed that it is related to the western one e.g. Stanghellini97, Axon00, Fanti00 . Evans99 study the molecular gas in 1345+125 and suggest that the molecular gas is fueling the AGN. Our UV image detects both nuclei separated by 1.8 .

sources.summary: PKS 1345+12 is presented as a young radio source with extended ionized halos and very fast blueshifted gas outflows traced in emission and H I absorption.

quotes.quote: The presence of such halos is shown in Figure fig2 and Figure fig3 in the cases of PKS 1549--79 and PKS 1345+12 respectively. Thus, we have used the kinematics of the quiescent halos of ionized gas to derive the systemic velocity of the host galaxy. Deriving a reliable systemic velocity has been a key step in the interpretation of the kinematics of the gas (both warm and cold).
The shifts are up to 2000 \ (see, e.g., PKS 1345+12 in Figure fig3 , Holt et al. 2003). These results show that the presence of fast gas outflows is (almost) ubiquitous in these objects .

These outflows are characterized by the detection of broad, blueshifted \ absorption profiles (see, for example, the case of PKS 1345+12 in Figure fig3 and of 3C 305 in Figure fig4 ). The velocities of these outflows exceed, in the most extreme cases, 1000 .

sources.summary: PKS 1345 + 12 is discussed as a radio-loud Seyfert 2 ULIRG with two nuclei, western CO emission, and infrared evidence that an AGN dominates its power.

quotes.quote: PKS 1345 + 12 (IRAS 13451 + 1232; z = 0.1215) is a ULIRG with L _ IR 10 ^ 12.3 L _ (Table 1). It is classified optically as a Seyfert 2 vei99,yua10 . It has two nuclei with a separation of 2 arcsec along the east-west (EW) direction sco00,kim02,ima14a , and CO J=1--0 emission is detected in the western nucleus eva99 . Infrared 2 m spectroscopy detected an AGN-origin broad (full width at half maximum [ FWHM ] > 2000 km s ^ 1 ) Pa (1.875 m) emission line, due to smaller dust extinction effects compared with the optical vei97 , suggesting that the hidden AGN is only modestly obscured.

Based on the luminosity of the detected broad Pa emission line, vei97 argued that the AGN could be responsible for the bulk of the bolometric luminosity of PKS 1345 + 12. A rising infrared 2.5--5 m spectrum with a non-detectable 3.3 m PAH emission feature also supports the AGN-dominated nature of this ULIRG ima10 . PKS 1345 + 12 shows an excess of the radio-to-infrared luminosity ratio, compared with the majority of LIRGs dra04 , suggesting that the AGN is radio-loud.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 12.50 as a dense-environment, pre-coalescent multiple merger with a double nucleus, long tidal tails, broad fan emission, central absorption, and relic radio emission.

quotes.quote: 4C 12.50 (PKS 1345+12) Figure fig:PKS1345 shows the V-band image of 4C 12.50 (PKS 1345+12). It reveals an apparent multiple merger system. The core of 4C 12.50 consists of a double nucleus, with a separation of 1.6 '' or 3.5 kpc (as previously found by hec86 and eva99 ). At the south-western end of the host galaxy, a double tidal-tail stretches 60 kpc north, encircling roughly 30 of the host galaxy.

On the opposite side of 4C 12.50 there is a broad fan of emission that stretches 85 kpc towards the south-east. 4C 12.50 resides in a dense environment. Two apparent close companion galaxies lie 20 kpc north of the host galaxy and may be involved in the merger (see arrows in Fig. fig:PKS1345 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 1345+125 as a compact radio source with a strong component, bent jet, likely core in component A, and polarized knots affected by depolarization.

quotes.quote: 1345+125 : the VLBA image at 15 GHz shows the same morphology seen at 5 GHz. A strong compact component and a jet leading SE with some knots, then bending to the SW, and widening into a diffuse lobe which, in the 15 GHz image, is mostly resolved out. At 15 GHz, we do not detect the weak emission north of component A, which is present in the 5 GHz image. Here, the image registration is relatively easy thanks to the presence of a number of rather isolated, optically thin, knots in the jet.

Component A is the flattest one with 0.3 , while component B has 0.5 . Component A is therefore likely to contain the core. The northern side is barely visible at 5 GHz, and while we can assume the jet is relatively beamed towards us making the counter-jet undetectable, it is puzzling that we do not see the counter-lobe.

sources.summary: The review describes 4C 12.50 as a gas-rich ULIRG compact radio source with CO and warm H2 detections, multiphase molecular outflow, heated gas, and jet-driven H I acceleration.

quotes.quote: Molecular gas CO has been detected in a few objects in merging/interacting systems -- 3C48 Krips2005 , 3C318 Willott2007 , 4C 12.50 Evans1999,Dasyra2012,Fotopoulou2019 , PKS 1549 79 Oosterloo2019 , and B1740 517 Allison2019 , the possible cool-core CSS PKS 0023 26 Morganti2020 , and two nearby CSOs -- 4C 31.04 Garcia-Burillo2007,Ocana2010 and PKS B1718 649 Maccagni2018 .

Near-IR observations of ro-vibrational transitions of H _2 provide a probe of warm molecular hydrogen in 3C305, OQ208, PKS 1549 79 Guillard2012 , 4C 31.04 Zovaro2019a , 4C 12.50 Dasyra2011, Dasyra2014, Guillard2012 , 3C293 Ogle2010 , and PKS B1718 649 Maccagni2016b . In these sources, the warm H _2 is heated to temperatures of 500-1000 K, likely due to shocks from the radio jet Dasyra2011, Dasyra2014, Guillard2012 . In PKS 1549 79 Guillard2012,Morganti2020 and 4C 12.50 Dasyra2011, Dasyra2014, Guillard2012, Fotopoulou2019 both the warm molecular gas measured with the near-IR lines of H _2 and the colder phase traced by CO are participating in the gaseous outflow.

Outflows of molecular gas are rare so far in compact radio sources, but are detected in PKS 1549 79 Guillard2012,Oosterloo2019 and in the ULIRG 4C 12.50 (PKS 1345+12) Dasyra2011, Dasyra2012, Dasyra2014, Guillard2012,Spoon2013,Fotopoulou2019 . A significant fraction of the H _2 in the outflow in 4C 12.50 has been heated to a temperature of about 400 K Dasyra2014 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 13451+1232 (4C+12.50) as a radio AGN with strong millimeter continuum, applies special CO(2-1) reduction, and reports no significant ALMA CO(2-1) absorption.

quotes.quote: The second target, 13451+1232 (4C+12.50), is a radio AGN with a strong 230 GHz continuum dominated by synchrotron radiation Pereira-Santaella2021 . Given that for this source the continuum is strong enough, we decide to apply self-calibration to these data in order to increase the signal-to-noise. We apply five rounds of phase self-calibration and one round of amplitude self-calibration. Due to the steep slope of its continuum, we use a first-order polynomial to subtract the continuum from the CO(2-1) spectral window in the uv plane.

We inspect the ALMA CO(2-1) spectrum of the west nucleus of 13451+1232 (shown in Fig. fig:absorption ), which is the nucleus dominating the FIR continuum emission and therefore, the nucleus where an absorption could be produced. We do not observe any significant absorption in the spectral channels on the blue-side of the CO(2-1) line in this target.

sources.summary: The paper includes 4C 12.50 among nearby radio galaxies with fast ionized and atomic outflows, warm turbulent H2 emission, and blue-shifted mid-infrared line wings linked to jet feedback.

quotes.quote: We present mid-IR Spitzer spectroscopy of 8 nearby radio-galaxies where fast outflows of ionized and atomic gas were detected by previous optical and radio observations. Our main results and conclusions are the following: In all of the sources, we detect high equivalent width H _2 line emission from warm (100-5000 K) molecular gas, implying warm H _2 masses ranging from 10^8 to 10^ 10 M _ . The observed H _2 to-PAH luminosity ratios are all above the values predicted from photo-ionization models, and we suggest that the H _2 emission is associated with the dissipation of a fraction of the kinetic energy provided by the radio jet.

In 5 sources (3C 236, 3C 293, 3C 459, 4C 12.50 and PKS 1549-79), we securely detect blue-shifted wings (up to 3000 km s ^ 1 ) on the [ Ne ii ] (and [ Ne iii ] for the highest SNR spectra) that matches remarkably well with the blue-shifted, broad H i absorption associated with the outflow. All but one of these sources have resolved and very broad mid-IR rotational H _2 lines with FWHM 500 km s ^ 1 (and up to 900 km s ^ 1 ), compared to only 2 overall amongst the 298 AGN with such spectra in the Spitzer archive Dasyra2011 .

sources.summary: 4C 12.50 is discussed as a young, possibly restarted, infrared-bright radio galaxy whose fast H I and CO outflows are located near a radio hot spot in a lobe.

quotes.quote: A very convincing example for the outflows being off-nucleus is the case of 4C 12.50, a young (possibly restarted, see Stanghellini05 ), IR-bright radio galaxy. In this object, the fast outflow \ has been found to be located at the end of a radio lobe, and is wrapped around a bright hot spot in this lobe ( Morganti13 , see Fig. fig:4C12.50outflow ).

One of the first examples of this was the CO outflow detected in the radio source 4C12.50 (see Fig. \ fig:4C12.50outflow ). Earlier \ observations Morganti05a had shown that a fast \ outflow is present in this object which is located at a bright radio hot spot in one of the radio lobes Morganti13 . The CO data show absorption over the same velocities as where the \ outflow occurs Dasyra12 .

sources.summary: The review describes 4C 12.50 as a radio source where VLBI observations show jet-ISM interaction, outflowing gas near the jet, and evolving outflow structure.

quotes.quote: The best examples are 3C 293 Mahony16 and 4C 12.50 Morganti13 . Other examples can be found in . Figure fig:VLBI shows the case of 4C 12.50, where the interaction jet-ISM is caught in the act. The outflowing \ gas (shown in orange in Fig. fig:VLBI right) is distributed around the head of the jet and on the side of the lobes, very suggestive of an on-going interaction. From the VLBI observations it is also interesting to see that outflowing gas is present very close to the AGN 40 pc, Schulz18 . Furthermore, these studies also suggest that the structure of the outflow changes with time, with an increasing amount of diffuse gas - with respect to gas in clumps - as the radio source grows (Schulz et al. in prep).

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS 13451 + 1232 as the other 2 Jy GPS ULIRG, noting possible [Si VI] emission, 2500 K H2 rotation temperature, and AGN-dominated broad Pa emission.

quotes.quote: This condition is not found in many ULIRGs, with the Parks (PKS) galaxy IRAS 13451 + 1232 being the only other 2 Jy ULIRG identified as a GPS source. This galaxy may also have [ Si 6 ] emission, as reported by vex97 , though the blend with the H _ 2 line at their lower resolution makes it difficult to estimate the relative contribution of [ Si 6 ] . The rest of the H _ 2 lines in IRAS 13451 + 1232, as presented in vex97 , indicate a H _ 2 rotation temperature around 2500 K, in which case the [ Si 6 ] line would account for 30 of the total combined line flux. IRAS 13451 + 1232 does show impressive broadening of the Pa line, clearly standing out as an AGN-dominated source.

sources.summary: The paper finds 4C +12.50 has weak PAH relative to neon, likely AGN effects, and two optical nuclei in a common envelope.

quotes.quote: 4C +12.50, in particular, deviates significantly both from the ULIRG fit and the remainder of the CSO sample (showing almost an order of magnitude less PAH emission than would be expected from the neon strengths).

While emission from AGN-heated gas can contribute to neon emission (which may explain the large disparity of the SFRs for 4C +12.50), PAHs are easily dissociated by hard radiation environments and are almost never detected in AGN.
4C +12.50 has two bright optical nuclei within a common envelope separated by only 1.8 gil86,axo00 , which is several times smaller than the width of the LH slit.

sources.summary: The paper highlights IRAS 13451+1232 as the sample object with the shallowest 30-20 micron slope, a Seyfert/quasar-like low-resolution spectrum, weak PAH emission, and a nearly uniform IRS continuum.

quotes.quote: The shallowest 30-20 slope occurs for IRAS 13451+1232 ( _ 30-20 =1.9 ); the low-resolution spectrum for this object more closely resembles that seen in Seyfert galaxies and PG quasars sch06,hao07 , with weak PAH emission and shallower silicate absorption. The continuum emission for this object is also much closer to being uniform over the entire range of the IRS; the difference between the two spectral indices is only =-0.4 , compared to an average of =2.7 for the entire sample. This behavior is more typical of non-thermal emission that can extend over many decades with the same index.

sources.summary: IRAS 13451+1232 is discussed as a type 2 Seyfert ULIRG with blueshifted neon and [Ne V] emission, indicating a more advanced clearing of its line of sight.

quotes.quote: Only two other sources exhibit such an asymmetry in neon profiles: our target IRAS 12127 1412, and IRAS 13451+1232. The latter is also detected in [ Ne v ] at 14.32 m, which is a straightforward tracer of the AGN radiation field due to the high ionization energy required (97.1 eV). This strongly blueshifted detection indicates a more advanced stage of this source in the process of cleaning its line of sight, which is consistent with its type 2 Seyfert classification in the optical.

sources.summary: The paper cites 4C12.50 as a ULIRG AGN with a 1000 km/s HI and CO molecular outflow co-spatial with a radio jet hot spot.

quotes.quote: An interesting example of a molecular outflow is the ULIRG AGN 4C12.50. It exhibits HI and CO gas outflow with velocity 1000 km s ^ 1 which is co-spatial with the radio jet hot-spot, at 100 pc scales (Morganti et al. 2013, 2014, Dasyra and Combes 2012). This is probably produced by radiative cooling of shocked gas due to jet interaction with the ISM.

gmw_00001783.name: irregular galaxy G
aliases_external: irregular host galaxy G of KSP-SN-2016kf
gmw_00001783.overview: Irregular galaxy G is a small host galaxy with an effective radius of 2.2 kpc [arXiv:1906.09271]. Located at a luminosity distance of 197.4 Mpc, its spectrum exhibits Balmer emission lines including H-alpha and H-beta [arXiv:1906.09271].

sources.summary: The paper identifies irregular galaxy G as the supernova host, measuring its redshift, distance, emission-line spectrum, small size, and negligible host extinction.

quotes.quote: We measure the redshift of the host galaxy of \ to be 0.043 0.002 using the H line in the host galaxy spectrum. The value is consistent with the redshift measured with the SN spectrum, confirming that the irregular galaxy G (Figure fig:host ) is the host galaxy of \ (see 4 and 5 for the details of the spectroscopic analysis and the redshift measurements).

Figure fig:spec (bottom panel) shows the spectrum of the galaxy identified with ``G'' in Figure fig:host . The spectrum exhibits prominent Balmer H emission lines including H and H as well as several other lines such as [ O 3 ] 3727, [ O 3 ] 4959, [ O 3 ] 5007, [ N 2 ] 6584, and [ S 2 ] 6717. The redshift of the galaxy, measured from the H line, is z 0.043 , which is equivalent to the luminosity distance of 197.4 Mpc (see sec:phot .3). This redshift is consistent with that of \ as shown in sec:spec , confirming that the galaxy is the host galaxy of the SN. The host galaxy is 4 \ (or 3.4 kpc at the angular diameter distance of 181.5 Mpc) away from the SN in the eastern direction. It has a small effective radius of 2.2 kpc (or 2 5).

gmw_00001784.name: galaxy 70505
gmw_00001784.overview: Galaxy 70505 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy [arXiv:2311.00511]. Although its split spectral line initially suggested it could be a low-redshift interloper, analysis indicates the split is a genuine Lyman-alpha detection with a dip caused by an over-subtracted skyline [arXiv:2311.00511].

sources.summary: The paper singles out galaxy 70505 as a possible low-redshift interloper but argues its split line is a genuine high-redshift Lyman-alpha detection.

quotes.quote: We would like to highlight a potential candidate for such a low- z interloper in galaxy 70505 and explain why we believe it to be a genuine high-z detection. Firstly, if the two peaks that appear to be present were due to [ O ii ] , we should expect their peaks to be separated by 3 Å rest frame, and the galaxies redshift would be z=1.26 meaning the peaks should be separated by 6.3 Å observed frame. Given that the separation is 5.7 Å, it leads us to conclude that is not the [ O ii ] doublet. Secondly, since one part of the line is over a skyline, there is a part of the 2D spectrum which is over-subtracted. This leads to a dip in the 1D spectrum when collapsed, which can make it appear as if the line is two lines.

For these reasons, we have chosen to set the line as a Lyman- detection and to extrapolate over the missing part of the line. It is possible that we are missing flux from the fit, though this should not affect the determination of the spectroscopic redshift.

gmw_00001785.name: Mrk 477
gmw_00001785.overview: Mrk 477 is a type-2 quasar [arXiv:2212.01258] and Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1505.01166] that exhibits optical microvariability [arXiv:1504.08254] and hosts a compact nuclear starburst [arXiv:2212.01258]. The host galaxy features a bar, a pseudo-bulge [arXiv:2105.12188], and tidal streams connecting it to neighboring galaxies [arXiv:1802.06184, arXiv:astro-ph/0012035].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 477 as the nearest type-2 quasar, hosting a compact nuclear starburst but lacking nuclear PAH emission, likely because quasar radiation or shocks destroy the PAH molecules.

quotes.quote: Mrk 477 is the closest type-2 quasar, at a distance of 163 Mpc. This makes it an ideal laboratory for studying the interplay between nuclear activity and star formation with a great level of detail and signal-to-noise. In this Letter we present new mid-infrared (mid-IR) imaging and spectroscopic data with an angular resolution of 0.4 ( 300 pc) obtained with the Gran Telescopio Canarias instrument CanariCam. The N-band (8-13 m) spectrum of the central 400 pc of the galaxy reveals [ S IV ] 10.51 m emission, but no 8.6 or 11.3 m polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) features, which are commonly used as tracers of recent star formation. This is in stark contrast with the presence of a nuclear starburst of 300 pc in size, an age of 6 Myr, and a mass of 1.1 10 ^ 8 M _ , as constrained from ultraviolet Hubble Space Telescope observations.

Mrk 477 is a perfect laboratory for studying the reliability of PAHs as star formation tracers as a function of distance from the active nucleus. It is a luminous AGN with a high hydrogen column density, co-existing with a nuclear starburst of 300 pc in size, an age of 6 Myr, and a mass of 1.1 10 ^ 8 M _ . This starburst dominates the continuum emission from the UV to the near-IR, but does not show detectable PAH emission in the mid-IR nuclear spectrum ( 400 pc).

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 477 microvariable in both observing nights, with measured amplitudes of 2.7 and 2.3, using ANOVA on optical light curves.

quotes.quote: Mrk 477 was observed on March 29 and March 31, 2011. The gap between the observations on March 29 was caused by a technical maintenance pause. Mrk 477 is the only target that showed microvariable behavior in both observing nights.

The upper limit for variations is 0.027 between 5:55 UT and 7:02 UT and 0.023 mag between 8:46 UT and 10:02 UT (for the first and second observing night, respectively). Computed amplitudes of microvariability are 2.7 and 2.3 for the fisrt and second observing night, respectively. Based on our results, we conclude that Mrk 477 exhibits microvariations.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 477 as a Seyfert 2 nucleus with spatially resolved UV emission, a nuclear starburst, and evidence that its broad HeII emission comes from Wolf-Rayet stars.

quotes.quote: In four of the targets (Mrk 477, NGC 7130, NGC 5135 and IC 3639) the morphology suggests that they harbor a nuclear starburst (Figure 1). In these cases, the UV continuum is spatially resolved, showing sub-arcsec structure. The effective radius (50-200 pc) of the UV emission is similar to the size of the NLR.

Two exceptions to this dual clasification are Mrk 477 and Mrk 463E. Both objects show a very blue continuum, very diluted stellar absorption features and very broad HeII 4686 emission (Figure 6). In the case of Mrk 477, our UV images and spectroscopy indicate that it harbors a powerful red nuclear starburst, and the HeII emission is due to Wolf-Rayet stars.

sources.summary: The paper describes MARK 477 as a type 2 Seyfert with polarized broad lines, nuclear spiral or circumnuclear-ring structure, radio nucleus, and Compton-thick candidate status.

quotes.quote: MARK 477 This object was classified as a type 2 Seyfert veron1997 , and broad lines have been detected in polarized light tran1992,tran1995 . The HST image reveals a structure around the nucleus, that could be a spiral or a circumnuclear ring (see Appendix multiimages ). A nuclear counterpart was found at 6 cm using VLA data ulvestadwilson1984 . It was classified as a Compton thick candidate bassani1999 . The source was observed twice with ASCA in December 1995; variations were not found when fitting a scattered power law plus a narrow line levenson2001 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 477 as a nearby QSO2 with a well-resolved narrow-line region, modeling its mass profile and radiatively accelerated outflow gas.

quotes.quote: We use Mrk 477 and Mrk 34 in order to test our models against recent HST STIS observations of [ O III ] emission-line kinematics, since these AGNs possess more energetic outflows than found in Seyfert galaxies.

For this study, we select Mrk 477 and Mrk 34, two of the targets from our sample of QSO2s from trindadefalcao2020a , to extend our analysis of the dynamics of the [ O III ] gas. These AGNs have a redshift of z =0.038 and z =0.051, and a bolometric luminosity of L_ bol = 1.8 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 and L_ bol = 2.6 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 , respectively (see see section 2.4 trindadefalcao2020a ). Among the targets in the reyes2008a sample, Mrk 34 and Mrk 477 are the two nearest QSO2s with the best-resolved NLRs and, hence, the targets for which the radial mass profile can most readily be constructed.
In the case of Mrk 477 we find two inner components that can be identified with a pseudo-bulge and a bar, with the addition of a third component that corresponds to the disk.

sources.summary: Mrk 477 shows compact [O III] knots plus fainter extended emission, complex broad nuclear components, disturbed gas to 840 pc, and tidal streams to nearby galaxies.

quotes.quote: Mrk 477 The [ O III ] morphology of this target is largely comprised of two emission line knots inside 500 pc, with fainter emission extending to radii of 2.5 kpc. [ O III ] kinematics suggest that the gas is largely in rotation near systemic, with additional emission over the nucleus extending to radii 500 pc that displays multiple emission-line components and excessively large FWHMs 2000 km/s. Relatively moderate FWHM ( > 500 km s ^ 1 ), single-component, near-systemic emission-line kinematics extend to further distances of R _ dist = 840 pc. This target resides in a well-resolved spiral galaxy that shows signs of a recent interaction in tidal streams that bridge Mrk 477 to neighboring galaxies to the northeast DeR87 .

sources.summary: Mrk 477 exemplifies the composite spectra where a buried AGN partially emerges, with soft X-rays consistent with scattered AGN flux and weak thermal evidence.

quotes.quote: The second shows two distinct spectral components: a soft X-ray peak and a hard X-ray peak, consistent with an absorption hump associated with a buried AGN (e.g., Mrk 477).

NGC 7130 and Mrk 477 illustrate the two characteristic continuum shapes of the composite galaxies, depending on whether the intrinsic AGN is completely absorbed, as in the former, or partially emerges to add a high-energy bump, as in the latter.

The soft X-rays in Mrk 477 are consistent with purely scattered flux from an AGN Awa00 , but the spectrum of Mrk 477 can also be fitted with a thermal component added to the best-fitting scattered power law plus line model. The additional component is not statistically significant and the errors are large, but the best thermal model parameters are reasonable: kT =0.9 keV and A3 = 9.3 10^ 6 .

sources.summary: The paper reports Mrk 477 as essentially unresolved in the VLA image, with most flux in the central component and a compact VLBI-dominated structure plus weak halo-like emission.

quotes.quote: Mrk 477 (1439+537) Y : Our image is essentially unresolved, whereas the image of at the same frequency and with similar resolution was slightly resolved. The central component in their map shows weak extensions in several different directions. Our total flux density is consistent with theirs within the errors, with 90 of the total flux density being in the unresolved central component. G : The object is dominated by a compact component containing about 80 of the total flux density on VLBI scales, with some evidence for weak halo-like emission.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes Mark 477, finding its nucleus dominated by starburst light while its fitted line systems are typical of Seyfert 2 gas.

quotes.quote: Mark 477 . This object was discussed in Paper I, where we concluded from the published data and the analy sis of a red spectrum that its nature was unclear. Heckman et al. (1997) have argued that the observed UV through near-IR continuum in the nucleus of Mark 477 is dominated by light from a starburst. The narrow Balmer emission lines would then be excited by ionizing radiation from both the hidden Seyfert 1 nucleus and from the hot stars in the starburst. We have re-analyzed our old red spectrum together with our new blue spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 477 as a galaxy with a tidal-tail companion, low Galactic column density, HRI extension to 15 kpc, and no significant thermal starburst signature.

quotes.quote: Mrk 477 Mrk 477 has a companion 50 \ away, which is connected by tidal tails Zwi71 .
Observed with the HRI, Mrk 477 is extended to a radius of 15 kpc, measured with the constrained technique ( subsec:rosimg ).
Only in one case (Mrk 477) do we fail to detect significantly this starburst signature.

gmw_00001786.name: host galaxy of GRB 050730
gmw_00001786.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 050730 is a young, high-redshift dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:0809.3247, arXiv:0904.2206] with an estimated age of 0.2 to 0.6 Gyr [arXiv:0811.1591, arXiv:1903.01353]. It is characterized by low star-formation efficiency [arXiv:0904.2206, arXiv:0811.1591], low dust content [arXiv:0811.1591, arXiv:1903.01353], and a clumpy interstellar medium [arXiv:astro-ph/0701558].

sources.summary: The GRB 050730 host sightline has saturated high-ion absorption, two narrow components, an excited-line non-detection, a blueshifted wing, a high-velocity absorber, and extremely high column density.

quotes.quote: GRB 050730 =3.9686 High-ion absorption is seen in , , , , , and \ centered at . All of these lines except \ are saturated. The \ profile shows two components separated by 27 5  , each with fairly narrow b values (7 3 and 14 8  ); we fit the \ absorption with one component with b =17 2  . ^* 1072.973 is not detected at ; we measure log N ( ^* ) < 13.70 (3 ) over the velocity range 25 to 20  , in which we measure log N ( )=14.61 0.15, so that N ( ^* )/ N ( ) < 0.12.

The , , , and \ profiles show a clear negative-velocity absorption-line wing extending from 70 to 145  . Finally, a HV absorber at 1550  \ is seen with at least five \ and \ components spread over 200  \ see also Ch07 . This HV absorption-line complex is shown in Figure 5. This GRB shows an extremely high \ column of log N ( )=22.15, and is the highest redshift GRB in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB 050730 host as a young dwarf irregular at z about 4, constraining its low star-formation efficiency, age, specific star-formation rate, and dust ratios from afterglow abundances.

quotes.quote: Fig 1 shows clearly that the most likely progenitor for the host galaxy of GRB 050730 is a dwarf irregular galaxy, and how the elliptical and spiral model appear indadequate to describe the abundance pattern observed by P07 in the GRB 050730 host. In the following, we will use the dwarf irregular galaxy model to constrain the parameters of the SF history and the age of the host galaxy GRB 050730. The next step is to constrain the main star formation history parameter of the host galaxy of GRB 050730, i.e. its star formation efficiency .

The abundances are reproduces at best at an ages of 0.4 Gyr, corresponding to a redshift of formation z_f 5 . At this age, the specific star formation rate is 5 Gyr ^ 1 , the Dust-to-Gas ratio is 10^ 6 , in agreement with the upper limit of 8 10^ 6 as derived observationally by P07. The Dust-to-metals ratio is 0.04, consistent with the upper limit of 0.1 measured by P07. Finally, in Fig. fig4 , we compare the predicted SSFR vs [ Fe/H ] obtained for the best model for the host galaxy of GRB 050730, with SSFR derived for a sample of QSO DLAs by D07. The evolution of the SSFR of the GRB host is compatible with the values found for QSO DLAs, possibly indicating similar chemical evolution paths.

sources.summary: The paper uses GRB 050730 as a GRB-host ISM case study, reporting echelle spectroscopy, excited Fe II fine-structure identifications, and UV-pumped gas within about 100 pc.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present a comprehensive study of the physical properties of the ISM in GRB host galaxies, using absorption lines identified in the echelle spectra of two GRB afterglows, GRB 051111 and GRB 050730 as case studies. We first consider the enhanced radiation field in the circumburst environment due to the progenitor star as well as the optical afterglow, and constrain the distance of the observed neutral gas cloud based on the presence/absence of various atomic lines such as Mg I and Fe I. The dominant species of these elements is in the singly ionized state, because their first ionization potential is less than 1 Ryd.

Echelle spectroscopic observations of GRB 050730 were obtained using the MIKE echelle spectrograph bernstein03 on the Magellan Clay telescope. The data cover observed wavelengths = 3400 - 9000 \ at a FWHM  11 \ dispersion with a typical SNR=12 per resolution element. A detailed description of the observation and data reduction is presented in . In addition to the absorption features presented in Chen et al., here we also present identifications of Fe 2 transitions from excited fine structure states, 3d^6 4s ^6D_ J=5/2,3/2,1/2 (Figure  fig:050730 ).
We draw similar conclusions for GRB 050730 and the other GRB listed in Table  tab:rlim .

sources.summary: The paper models the host galaxy of GRB 050730 as a low-efficiency star-forming system about 0.6 Gyr old, with low dust content and abundances not strongly shaped by depletion.

quotes.quote: In this Section, our aim is to constrain the main parameter of the star formation history of the host galaxy of GRB 050730, i.e. its star formation efficiency . We need to find the model which reproduces at best the observed abundance ratios. In this study, the only parameter allowed to vary is the star formation efficiency.

The GRB host abundances are best reproduced by assuming z_ f = 6 . The difference between z_f and the redshift of the GRB host galaxy z_ GRB provides an estimate of the age of the system. The best model of the ``expected dust'' scenario points towards an age of 0.6 Gyr. With the best model of the ``alternative dust'' scenario, a nearly identical value for the age of the GRB host can be derived.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes GRB050730's host-galaxy absorption, finding fine-structure components with varied densities and using C/Fe at z=3.967 to infer a young, burst-forming galaxy affected by dust depletion.

quotes.quote: We focus here on the study of the local, GRB surrounding medium and on the analysis of the absorption lines produced by the gas in the host galaxy throughout observations of three GRBs: GRB050730, GRB050922C and GRB060418.

For GRB050730, two out of five components show fine structure lines (Fig. CIIetc ). Reliable values for temperature and electron density are T a few 10^3 K and n > 10^4 cm ^ 3 (second component; the components are numbered according to decreasing z) and n 10 100 cm ^ 3 (third component). The other components do not show fine structure features: this is an indication that they refer to a clumpy environment.

For GRB050730, whose redshift is z = 3.967 , we find a value in agreement with that expected for a galaxy younger than 1 Gyr subject to a burst of star-formation. Again, dust depletion may still play an important role. The [ C/Fe ] ratio evaluated component by component in GRB050730 shows that FeII is less abundant in component 3 than in component 2.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 050730 host as a less-dusty irregular galaxy whose abundance ratios are best fit by decreased massive-star dust production and age 0.2 Gyr.

quotes.quote: GRB 050730 The data-models comparison at Figure f:050730 show quite good agreement with the irregular galaxy reference model. In particular, [ S/Fe ] and the [ Ni/Fe ] data are the main drivers of this hypothesis, corroborated by the compatibility with Si and Mg lower and upper limits. From this test, we determined the irregular galaxy model with decreased dust production by massive stars (I ) as the best model among those shown in Table t:models . In this way, adopting the less dusty irregular model as the best one, we estimated the age of the host at the time of the GRB event. We found for it an age of 0.2 Gyr .

gmw_00001787.name: host galaxy of GRB 050820
gmw_00001787.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 050820 is a massive, strongly star-forming elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1903.01353] at a redshift of z=2.6147 [arXiv:0809.2608]. It exhibits a complex, extended morphology with multiple emission components [arXiv:2310.15967], which may represent a dwarf satellite within an interacting system [arXiv:1709.06574].

sources.summary: The paper detects extended host emission for GRB 050820A, derives ISM metallicity, dust and molecular-gas constraints from afterglow spectra, and describes the host as extended in rest-frame UV morphology.

quotes.quote: These two spectra together cover a full spectral range from 3300 \ through 10,000 , allowing accurate estimates of chemical abudances in the ISM of the GRB host galaxy. Based on multiple absorption features from both ground-state and excited-state ions, we determine a source redshift of z=2.6147 and a total neutral hydrogen column density of N( )=21.0 0.1 (Prochaska \ 2007b; Ledoux \ 2005). An absorption-line analysis of various ions shows that [ S / H ] = -0.63 0.11 and [ S / Fe ] = +0.97 0.09 (Prochaska \ 2007b), from which we derive a dust-to-gas ratio that is comparable to what is seen in the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC).

The OT was clearly detected in the first epoch image, but faded in the second epoch image which reveals faint extended emission features of the host galaxy. In addition to the extended low-surface feature seen at the position of the OT, which is identified as the host galaxy, we identify two compact sources at =1.3 north of the OT (Object A ) and 0.4 south of the OT (Object B ) from the afterglow lines of sight. We measure isophotal magnitudes of the host and Objects A and B .

sources.summary: The GRB 050820 host sightline shows a narrow offset component, complex multi-component absorption over 400 km/s, lower-S/N matching velocity structure, and a two-component high-velocity absorber.

quotes.quote: GRB 050820 =2.6147 \ shows a single narrow component at 109  \ relative to \ with b =9 3  . This \ offset is the largest among our seven spectra, and has been noticed before Pr08b , but the formal significance of the 1238.821 and 1242.804 detections is only 3.1 and 2.0 respectively. The \ and \ profiles exhibit at least ten components spread over 400  \ in velocity width, with a / \ ratio that changes dramatically between components. \ 1550.781 is blended so we only display \ 1548.204. A similar velocity structure is observed at lower S/N in \ 1862.789.
Finally a two-component HV absorber at 3 660  \ relative to \ is seen in , but with no detectable , , or \ (see Figure 5).

sources.summary: The paper finds GRB 050820's host abundance pattern best matched by a low star-formation-efficiency model with dust accretion, implying an age lower limit and realistic dust content.

quotes.quote: In fig. 4, we show various observed interstellar abundance ratios vs metallicity for the host galaxy of GRB 050820. The observed abundance ratios are compared with predictions computed by means of three chemical evolution models with SF efficiencies =0.1 Gyr ^ 1 , =1 Gyr ^ 1 and =10 Gyr ^ 1 . In this case, we see how, with a model assuming only dust destruction and no dust accretion, the observed abundance ratios do not allow us to determine which model is the most accurate in reproducing the data.

GRB 050820, we conclude that the model with SF efficiency =0.1 Gyr ^ 1 , ``expected dust'' composition and dust accretion is the best in reproducing the observed abundance ratios vs metallicity. We now use this model to infer the age of the GRB 050820 host galaxy. In this way, we are able to derive a lower limit of 1.5 Gyr for the age of the host galaxy of GRB 050820.

sources.summary: The paper classifies the GRB 050820 host as a massive, strongly star-forming elliptical galaxy with high alpha and zinc abundance ratios and an age of 15 Myr.

quotes.quote: GRB 050820 From Figure f:050820 , we see that the three upper panels for [ /Fe ] ratios indicate for this host an elliptical galaxy. Rather, we can exploit the very high [ Zn/Fe ] value ( >1 dex ) in this host as a further insight for the elliptical galaxy hypothesis. In Figure f:050820_1 we show what happens adopting the model E m for an elliptical galaxy with increased mass ( 10^ 12 M_ ) and SFE ( 25 Gyr^ 1 ), which results the best one from the statistical test performed. Concluding, we identify this host galaxy as a massive, strong star forming elliptical galaxy. For what concerns the age of this host, we found for it a very young one of 15 Myr .

sources.summary: The paper observes the GRB 050820A host with NIRSpec IFS, noting extended multi-component morphology, accurate HST-based astrometry, and emission-line metallicities at diagnostic maxima.

quotes.quote: The host galaxies of GRB 050820A and GRB 150403A showed evidence of spatially extended emission in the available imaging data, and NIRSpec observations were therefore performed using the integral field spectrograph (IFS), which has a 3 3 field of view. In the case of GRB 050820A, previous Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) imaging data showed the host galaxy consists of at least two components separated by < 1 5 chen12 , and the galaxy complex was therefore sufficiently compact to be able to perform a two-point nod to cover both components and additionally sample the sky background.

An image of the G140M/F100LP NIRSpec data cube centered at the redshifted \ line is shown in Fig. fig:grb050820A_IFS , where a complex galaxy morphology made up of numerous emission components can be seen.

For the two galaxies observed with the IFS (host galaxies of GRB 050820A and GRB 150403A) we plot the emission line metallicity of the resolved component closest in projection to the GRB afterglow position, which in both cases is component C in Figs. fig:grb050820A_IFS and fig:grb150403A_IFS . In the case of GRB 050820A the line ratios of all components are larger than the maximum value covered by the , \ and R diagnostics considered, and the emission line metallicity in all cases is thus the maximum possible metallicity given by the diagnostic.

sources.summary: The paper describes a high absorption velocity width relative to the host stellar mass, suggests an interacting dwarf-satellite scenario, and infers an emission metallicity from its velocity measurement.

quotes.quote: The situation is similar in the case of GRB 050820A host with a of 300 and an stellar mass of M_* = 10^ 8.96 M_ (presented in the right panel of Fig. fig:MS ) where the rotational velocity is expected to be 90 based on the sTF relation presented in Ubler17-2017arXiv170304321U .

In the case of GRB 050820A (mentioned above) Chen12-2012MNRAS.419.3039C proposed the broad absorption signatures in the afterglow spectra to be due to the occurrence of the GRB in a dwarf satellite of an interacting system.
For the host galaxy of GRB 050820A, we use the = 300 measurement and infer an emission metallicity of 7.97 0.18 .

gmw_00001788.name: host galaxy of GRB 051111
gmw_00001788.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 051111 contains neutral gas located at least 50 to 80 parsecs away from the afterglow [arXiv:astro-ph/0601057]. This interstellar gas exhibits a very large zinc-to-iron ratio, which is indicative of strong dust depletion [arXiv:0811.1591].

sources.summary: The paper treats the GRB 051111 host as a high-redshift ISM sightline, finding unusually large Mg I absorption and neutral gas at least about 50-80 pc from the afterglow.

quotes.quote: Column density measurements for the atomic species shown in Figure  fig:051111 are presented in Table  tab:051111 . We detect several transitions of Mg 1 but no Fe 1 transition. The Mg ^0 column density for the host galaxy of GRB 051111 may be the largest ever recorded outside the Milky Way.

Based on the kinematics of the Mg 1 profile and its large column density, we argue that this atomic gas is co-spatial with the majority of low-ion absorption observed along the sightline. As such, we will treat the Mg ^0 , Mg ^+ , Fe ^0 , and Fe ^+ gas to arise from a single cloud within the ISM of the galaxy.

The presence of strong Mg 1 absorption transitions at t=1500 s after the burst therefore suggests that the neutral gas is located at least 50 pc away from the afterglow. A stronger constraint can be placed on the minimum distance from the GRB by evaluating time variations in the Mg ^0 column density mhk+02 . In this case, the result is independent of the total Mg ^0 column density prior to the GRB.

sources.summary: The paper says GRB 051111 has too few measured abundance ratios to constrain its host's star-formation history, but its high Zn/Fe suggests strong dust depletion.

quotes.quote: Of the elements studied in this paper, for GRB 051111, only 4 measured abundance ratios are available (see Tab. 1). However, of these 4, 3 are lower limits and only one is a real measure. In fig. 7, we show the observed interstellar abundance ratios vs metallicity for the host galaxy of GRB 051111, compared with predictions computed by means of three chemical evolution models with SF efficiencies =0.1 Gyr ^ 1 , =1 Gyr ^ 1 and =10 Gyr ^ 1 . The predictions are shown both taking into account dust accretion (thick lines) and not (thin lines).
The poor number of abundance ratios measured for this GRB DLA does not allow us to constrain its star formation history. However, the very large [ Zn/Fe ] is indicative of strong dust depletion.

gmw_00001789.name: host galaxy of GRB 060418
gmw_00001789.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060418 is a blue, low-luminosity irregular galaxy at a redshift of approximately 1.5 [arXiv:0811.1591, arXiv:0902.3661]. It exhibits a disturbed morphology with at least three distinct components [arXiv:0902.3661], an estimated age of 0.63 Gyr [arXiv:0811.1591], and subsolar metallicity [arXiv:astro-ph/0701558].

sources.summary: The paper models GRB 060418's host with low star-formation efficiency and dust accretion, deriving a 0.63 Gyr age, high SSFR, and predicted dust ratios.

quotes.quote: For GRB 060418, only 4 measured abundance ratios are available to be compared with our predictions. In fig. 8, we show the observed interstellar abundance ratios vs metallicity for the host galaxy of GRB 060418, compared with predictions computed by means of three chemical evolution models with SF efficiencies =0.1 Gyr ^ 1 , =1 Gyr ^ 1 and =10 Gyr ^ 1 , taking into account dust accretion (thick lines) and not (thin lines). In this case, we have two real measures and two lower limits. Also this system presents a supersolar [ Zn/Fe ] ratio, which is likely indicating the presence of dust depletion.

The two actual measures allow us to derive some constraints on the star formation history and on the age of the host galaxy of GRB 060418. From Fig. 8, we see that the model which best reproduces the observed abundance ratios is the one characterized by =0.1 Gyr ^ 1 and including dust accretion. We use this model to derive a value for the age and for the SSFR of the host galaxy of this GRB. The redshift of the GRB is z_ GRB = 1.5, which, with the cosmology adopted in this paper, implies an age of 0.63 Gyr and a SSFR of 2.8 Gyr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 060418 host as a blue, disturbed multi-component low-luminosity galaxy at z=1.49, using imaging and absorber limits to assess its local environment.

quotes.quote: GRB 060418 occured along a very complex sightline, but by performing photometric and spectroscopic observations of the objects in this field we have disentangled three foreground systems from the complicated host galaxy. The blue host exhibits a disturbed morphology, with at least three components seperated by 2 . The extended system is reminiscent of the z=1.09 host of GRB 980613 dbk00 , the z=2.04 host of GRB 000926 Fynbo:2002 , and the z=2.14 host of GRB 011211 Jakobsson:2003 . The system conforms to our current picture of GRB hosts as blue and irregular galaxies experiencing a mode of ongoing star formation which is different from that found in the massive starbursts typified by mid-IR and sub-mm detections Le-Floch:2003,Fruchter:2006 . Most of the GRB hosts observed to date have 0.1 L_ * luminosities Chen:2009 .

Using the limit of H>25.0 mag calculated from our PANIC data, we conclude that this host's rest-frame absolute B magnitude, M_ B, rest 5 log h , is greater than 18.4 mag. Using M_ B_ * 5 log h =-20.5 for blue galaxies at z_ gal 1.3 Faber:2007 , this limit on M_ B, rest corresponds to a luminosity limit L 0.1 L_ * , consistent with other GRB hosts.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes GRB060418's host-galaxy absorption, reporting four absorption systems, Cr II and Zn II lines, and metallicity estimates from X-ray-derived hydrogen columns that remain below solar.

quotes.quote: We focus here on the study of the local, GRB surrounding medium and on the analysis of the absorption lines produced by the gas in the host galaxy throughout observations of three GRBs: GRB050730, GRB050922C and GRB060418.
The GRB060418 afterglow was observed 10 minutes after the trigger. We find four main absorption systems at z=1.489, 1.106, 0.655 and 0.602.

In GRB060418 we identify CrII and ZnII lines (Fig. met ). Such elements tend to stay in the gas state, minimizing the uncertainty when estimating the metallicity. No H features are present in this GRB spectrum, so we derive the N_ H column from the X-ray data, leading to: Z(Cr) = -1.8 0.3 and Z(Zn) = -1.3 0.2 , a bit higher than for the other two GRBs, but still below the solar values.

gmw_00001790.name: MCG 6-30-15
gmw_00001790.overview: MCG 6-30-15 is a nearby, radio-quiet [arXiv:astro-ph/0011167, arXiv:astro-ph/0604095] Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0610436, arXiv:1301.1957] with an elliptical or lenticular host morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0604095]. It exhibits rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0011167], complex outflow kinematics [arXiv:2206.15074], and a relativistically broadened iron emission line [arXiv:astro-ph/0011167, arXiv:1301.1957].

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG 6-30-15 as a Seyfert broad-iron-line test case, concluding continuum variability and black-body constraints rule out the Compton cloud model.

quotes.quote: However, in the Comptonization model, the continuum photons also pass through the same Comptonizing medium as the iron line photons. Thus, continuum variability can be used to place much tighter constraints on the size of the cloud. Any variability of the central source would be smeared out as the photons random walk through the cloud on a timescale of t_ MS R . Appreciable continuum variability in MCG 6-30-15 is observed on timescales down to t_ obs 100 (Reynolds et al. 1995; Yaqoob et al. 1997).

Since the continuum source is hypothesized to be interior to the Compton cloud, we must have R_ max R_ cloud . The region of the ( L ,T) plane forbidden by this constraint is shown in solid-shade in Fig. 1a. We see that applying these three constraints eliminates all regions of the ( L ,T) plane. One must conclude that the Compton cloud model discussed by Misra Kembhavi (1998) and MS99 is not valid in the case of MCG 6-30-15.

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton RGS spectra of MCG 6-30-15, describing it as a nearby NLS1 AGN with rapid X-ray variability, no soft excess, Compton reflection, and relativistically broadened soft X-ray lines.

quotes.quote: RGS observations of MCG 6-30-15 and Mrk 766 , which are reported here, have forced us to examine alternatives to the warm absorber model, and to propose a new and radically different interpretation of the soft X-ray spectra of active galaxies. MCG 6-30-15 and Mrk 766 (NGC 4253) are classified as Narrow Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxies on the basis of the widths of their Balmer lines ( < 2000 km s ^ 1 ), although they are not of the extreme kind. Both show strong and rapid variability in their X-ray fluxes, as well as variability in the slope of their power-law continua. MCG 6-30-15 is not known to possess a ``soft excess'' (which is one of the dominant characteristics of this class of objects), while Mrk 766 displays a soft excess that varies less than the continuum at higher energies.
Evidence for Compton reflection has been found only in MCG 6-30-15 .

MCG 6-30-15 and Mrk 766 are bright ( L_ 2-10 keV few 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) and nearby AGN (z = 0.00775 0.00005 and 0.01293 0.00005 for MCG 6-30-15 and Mrk 766 , respectively; redshifts based on optical emission line measurements, Fisher et al. \ , Smith et al. \ ), with relatively low Galactic absorption along the lines of sight ( 4.1 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 and 1.8 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 , respectively). MCG 6-30-15 was observed with XMM-Newton in July 2000 for a total of 120 ks; Mrk 766 in May 2000 for 55 ks.

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG 6-30-15 as a well-studied NLS1 with broad iron K emission, warm absorbers, and soft/hard time lags fit by compact-corona models.

quotes.quote: MCG 6-30-15 is a well-studied NLSI galaxy and it was the first source where a broad iron K emission line was detected tanaka95 . Since the initial discovery with ASCA , the source has been observed several times with a number of instruments, and its X--ray spectrum has been well studied guainazzi99,wilms01,fabian03,vaughan04,brenneman06,miller07,miniutti07,turner07,marinucci14 . It shows clear evidence for warm absorbers otani96,lee01,turner03,turner04 . Recent studies, using simultaneous XMM-Newton and NuSTAR data, have shown that the spectral slope of the continuum power-law is 2.1 marinucci14 .

The X-ray time-lags in this source were originally studied using the first 300 ks observation by emmanoulopoulos11 , who found a significant soft-lag at high frequencies and a hard-lag at low frequencies. Modeling of the soft and the iron-line vs. the continuum lags have revealed a height of the lamp-post to be of h=2.9^ +0.4 _ 0.7 ,<3 _ g (E14 and EP16a, respectively) and a BH spin of a =0.98_ 0.26 (E14).

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG 6-30-15 as a radio-quiet Seyfert whose iron-line spin interpretation is uncertain, notes its E/S0 host morphology, and suggests it may harbor a rapidly spinning black hole.

quotes.quote: The interpretation of the very extended and weak red wing of the fluorescent iron line observed in the radio-quiet Seyfert galaxy MCG 6-30-15 in terms of the model involving rapid rotation of the central black hole (Wilms et al. 2001) is not unique, and depends crucially on the details of the continuum production in the vicinity of the black hole. These details are very uncertain (Beckwith Done 2004). Furthermore, broad fluorescent iron lines can be produced also in the wind (Done Gierli nski 2006).

Finally, morphology of the host galaxy is E or SO (Ferruit, Wilson, Mulchaey, 2000) and therefore the object could have undergone a major merger. In addition, McHardy et al (2005) found 0.4 for this object. Hence, MCG 6-30-15 may indeed harbor a rapidly spinning black hole, but as most quasars , it happens to be observed in its radio quiet state.

sources.summary: MCG 6-30-15 has Fe K lines confirmed by other X-ray observatories; weak hard-band absorption is reported, and HETG data corroborate the line detections.

quotes.quote: In MCG 6-30-15 the lines have also been confirmed with other X-ray observatories ( young:2005 ; miller:2009 ) which suggests that the presence of a broad Fe line does not introduce any significant model systematics.
Furthermore, weak hard-band absorption has been noted in the spectra of MCG 6-30-15 by several authors (e.g., fabian:2002, vaughan:2004, nandra:2007, miller:2008 ).

detected a resolved absorption line at E 7.25 keV in the HETG spectrum MR 2251-178 ( 13,000 ) while found variable absorption lines due to and in MCG 6-30-15 with 2,000 . In both cases the outflow velocities are consistent with those found in our analysis, and the detection in the HETG spectrum of MCG 6-30-15 overcomes the ambiguities which remained on the basis of the data and independently corroborates the line detections.

sources.summary: Suzaku spectra of MCG 6-30-15 show broad iron K emission, strong hard reflection, and continuum variability where the reflection component is less variable than the power law.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG 6-30-15 ( z=0.00775 ) was observed 3 times by \ in 2006 January, with a total exposure of 347 ks and a mean 2-10 keV flux of 4.0 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , similar to the \ deep look (Fabian et al. 2002). The broad band \ spectrum of MCG 6-30-15 plotted as a ratio to a power-law continuum of =2.0 , is shown in Figure 4. The familiar signature of the broad iron K line is present between 4--7 keV, while below 3 keV the spectrum is strongly absorbed by a warm absorber as previously known (e.g. Turner et al. 2004). Importantly the HXD/PIN data above 10 keV show a very strong hard excess, the clear signature of a reflection component.

sources.summary: The paper lists MCG 6-30-15 as a paradigmatic Seyfert 1 target with a broadened iron line, high X-ray flux, constrained inclination, and predicted polarization.

quotes.quote: MCG 6-30-15 : The paradigmatic Seyfert-1 source for strong, relativistically broadened iron line emitted from the inner disk. The relatively high 2-10 keV flux makes it feasible for polarization measurements, and the inclination is well-contrained by the blue-shifted edge of the iron line. While this inclination is small ( 30 degrees), the AGN corona models described in schnittman:10 predict at least a few percent polarization in this case.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG 6-30-15 as a complex Seyfert outflow source with two kinematic components, including a high-ionization component and lower-ionization components at different velocities.

quotes.quote: The outflows of NGC 3516 and MCG 6-30-15 have more complex kinematic structure. NGC 3516 requires at least two kinematic components. One between - 650 - 50 and the second at 1650 50 . Both kinematic components have all six of the _i components. The turbulent velocity in each outflow was kept fixed for all _i components. The outflow of MCG 6-30-15 also has two kinematic components; the =4 component is at 1800 80 , while the lower- components are between 350- -130 .

gmw_00001791.name: PKS 1830-211
aliases_local: z=0.89 QAS toward the Quasar PKS 1830-211
aliases_external: z=0.89 foreground absorber/lens galaxy toward PKS 1830-211
PKS 1830-211 sightline / z=0.88582 lens toward PKS 1830-211
gmw_00001791.overview: PKS 1830-211 is a high-redshift, gravitationally lensed flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar [arXiv:0803.1900, arXiv:1904.05139]. The system is lensed by an intervening face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2401.09975], which splits the background source into two compact images connected by an Einstein ring [arXiv:0803.1900, arXiv:1904.05139].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PKS 1830-211 as a z=2.507 lensed blazar with INTEGRAL/COMPTEL detections, hard X-ray variability, persistent gamma-ray emission, and radio structure interpreted as lensed images plus an Einstein ring.

quotes.quote: We report on an analysis of X- and ray observations of PKS 1830-211 , based on the long-term campaigns carried out by INTEGRAL and COMPTEL. The INTEGRAL data currently available present a 33 significance detection in the 20-100 keV band, while the COMPTEL 6 years data provide a 5.2 significance detection in the 1-3 MeV energy band. At hard X-rays, INTEGRAL and supplementary SWIFT observations show flux variability on timescales of months. At rays, the source shows persistent emission over years.

Among them is the high redshift blazar PKS 1830-211 ( z=2.507 ), gravitationally lensed by an intervening galaxy at z=0.89 . The discovery of such gravitational system traces back to radio observations (Pramesh Rao Subrahmanyan 1988). The radio map was composed of two compact components separated by 1'' , supposed to be the split images from the central region of the source and an extended structure which is most probably from the jet and regarded as an unusually strong Einstein ring (see Jauncey et al. 1991; Sunita Nair et al. 1993). Steppe et al. (1993) have shown that PKS 1830-211 is radio variable on timescales of months.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1830-211 as a lensed blazar/quasar, summarizes its radio, X-ray, redshift, lensing, and ring-like morphology, and measures a gamma-ray time delay.

quotes.quote: The AGN PKS 1830-211 is a variable, bright radio source and an X-ray blazar. Its redshift was measured to be z=2.507 1999Lidman . The blazar was detected in the ray wavelengths with EGRET. The association of the EGRET source with the radio source was done by . The classification of PKS 1830-211 as a gravitational lensed quasi-stellar object was first proposed by .

According to 2002Winn , the actual lens is the galaxy at redshift z=0.89. PKS 1830-211 is observed in radio as an elliptical ring-like structure connecting 2 bright sources distant of roughly one arcsecond 1991Jauncey . The compact components were separately observed by the Australia Telescope Compact Array at 8.6 GHz for 18 months.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS B1830 211 as a gravitational lens system with a z = 0.89 spiral lensing galaxy producing molecular absorption used to constrain changes in the proton-to-electron mass ratio.

quotes.quote: We have used the Australia Telescope Compact Array to measure the absorption from the 2_ 0 3_ 1 E 12.2 GHz transition of methanol towards the z =0.89 lensing galaxy in the PKS B1830 211 gravitational lens system. Comparison of the velocity of the main absorption feature with the published absorption spectrum from the 1_ 0 2_ 1 E transition of methanol shows that they differ by 0.6 1.6 . We can use these observations to constrain the changes in the proton-to-electron mass ratio from z =0.89 to the present to 0.8 2.1 10^ 7 .

The lensing galaxy at z =0.89 is an Sb or Sc spiral seen nearly face-on Winn+02 , with the line-of-sight of the south-western component intersecting the galaxy at a galacto-centric radius of approximately 2 kpc, and the north-eastern at a radius of 4 kpc. The strongest molecular absorption is seen towards the south-western component, while the strongest H i absorption is seen towards the north-eastern component Carilli+98,Chengalur+99 . The velocity offset between the absorption from the south-west and north-east components is approximately 150 . The molecular absorption in PKS B1830 211 has been the subject of numerous studies with more than 30 different molecular species detected and multiple complexes spanning a velocity range of nearly 500 see and references therein Muller+11 . To date, no molecular absorption has been detected towards the second line-of-sight galaxy at z =0.19.

sources.summary: The software demonstration fits ALMA data for the lensed blazar PKS 1830 211, separating unresolved NE and SW images and extracting molecular absorption spectra through the foreground lens.

quotes.quote: As real-case examples, we show here the results obtained from the analysis of ALMA cycle-0 data on the lensed blazar PKS 1830 211 Project ADS/JAO.ALMA 2011.0.00405.S and VLBI data of the radio supernova SN 1993J. ALMA observations of PKS 1830 211 The separation between the two lensed images of the blazar (one at the north-east, NE, and the other at the south- west, SW, of the foreground galaxy) is 1 arcsec. However, the restoring beam of the ALMA B3 ( 90 GHz) observations is 1.5 1.9 arcsec, with a position angle of 80 deg. The two images are thus not resolved by the synthesized beam. Molecular absorption from the foreground lensing galaxy is found along both lines of sight to the SW and NE images, although with a slight offset in velocity (due to the galactic rotation, Muller ).

Indeed, we used the same fitting strategy to extract the absorption spectra toward the NE and SW images. The image of PKS 1830 211, obtained using only one spectral channel, is shown in Fig. PKSImage . The location of the NE and SW images (as estimated with uvmultifit ) is marked with crosses.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1830-211 as a strongly lensed high-energy FSRQ whose radio and gamma-ray delays can test whether low-state and flare emission sites coincide.

quotes.quote: Among the FSRQs detected at high energy, PKS 1830-211 and B2 0218+35 are known as a strongly gravitationally lensed systems. The detection of a gravitational-lens induced echo of 27 0.5  days in the light curve of PKS 1830-211 during low state by 2011A&A...528L...3B provided the first evidence for strong gravitational lensing of a ray source (see HujCheung for an alternative view). Radio observations of the two lensed images of PKS 1830-211 provide a magnification ratio of 1.5 and time delay 26 4  days 1998ApJ...508L..51L . Remarkably, the delay for the ray emission in the low state is consistent with the radio delay. Thus, the radiation sources may be coincident.

In contrast, the PKS 1830-211 flares detected in rays 2010ATel.2943....1C do not show delayed counterparts at the radio time delay. The magnification ratio has not yet been measured. A clean measurement of the time delay and the corresponding magnification ratio would determine whether the flare and the emission in the low state originate from the same region.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1830 211 as a radio-loud, Fermi-flaring lensed blazar with two images, model magnifications, measured time delay, and variability affecting magnification ratios.

quotes.quote: Flares in rays from Fermi LAT were also reported for PKS 1830 211, a double-image lens system with a separation of 1 arcsec Donna . This radio-loud blazar is at z=2.51 and is gravitationally lensed by a spiral galaxy at z=0.89 . Based on the SIE model, the magnification values that we obtain for the two images are 3.7 and 1.5. A time delay of 27.1 0.6 days between the images was estimated at radio wavelengths . Evidence for a time delay in the Fermi LAT ray data, consistent with the radio measurement, has also been reported for this source .

Observations with ALMA that overlapped with some of those of Fermi suggested microlensing at sub-mm wavelengths Marti-Vidal:2013vva . Their measurements also revealed chromatic variations in the flux densities of the images at these wavelengths, which may arise from microlensing or from intrinsic variability of the blazar jet. Our estimates for the magnifications of the images of PKS 1830 211 yield a magnification ratio of 2.4 , compared with the estimated magnification ratio of 1.5 at radio wavelengths . Because this source is highly variable (both extrinsically and intrinsically), the estimates of magnification ratios are affected and also variable.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1830-211 as a gravitationally lensed FSRQ/blazar with two radio images, an Einstein ring, gamma-ray flares, and no clear delayed LAT flare counterpart.

quotes.quote: The flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) \ (also known as TXS 1830 210, RX J1833.6 210, MRC 1830 211, 2FGL J1833.6 2104) has met with considerable attention, because it is such a good example of a gravitationally lensed source. The two lines of sight towards \ have been used as a cosmological probe: temperature of the cosmic microwave background, variations in the fundamental constants, the Hubble constant estimation bagdonaite13,blandford92 . This object also offers a unique opportunity to study both the interstellar medium of lens galaxy and the relativistic jet of the background ray blazar.

\ was discovered as a single source in the Parkes catalog, but later radio observations by the Very Large Array (VLA) and Australian Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) clearly revealed two sources, one in the northeast (NE) and one in the southwest (SW), separated by 0.98 and connected by an Einstein ring pramesh88,Jauncey91 . When the source, lensing foreground object, and observer lie along a straight line, the theory of gravitational lensing e.g.

The ``A'' interval contains the first ray brightening seen by the LAT, near the end of 2009. The announcement of a detection by AGILE on 2009 October 12 and 13, MJD 55116-55117 and references therein donnarumma11 occurred already some weeks before the ``A'' interval. To explore the behavior of \ during the main outburst (interval ``B'') and the second brightest flaring period (interval ``C'') in greater detail, we performed power-law fits to the source in 12-hour bins, with both the flux and photon indices ( _ ) left as free parameters.
