In both 4C 09.17 and 7C1354+2552, the blueshifted and redshifted kinematic components appear to move towards the central galaxy group.

sources.summary: The paper models the DLA galaxy associated with Q 1354+258 as a relatively massive system, predicting its mass, formation redshift, optical/near-infrared brightness, and nucleus position relative to the quasar sightline.

quotes.quote: An example is shown in Fig. f:example , for a DLA observed in the spectrum of the quasar Q 1354+258 .
In particular, for Q 1354+258 , we predict relatively massive galaxy, with a baryonic mass M_b 10^ 10 M_ (and so, a total gravitational mass M 10^ 11 M_ ) which formed at a redshift z_ turn 4.5 .

For Q 1354+258 , we predict B 26 and K 20 . We also predict that the nucleus of the DLA galaxy lies about 5 kpc from our line-of-sight to the quasar. We now know where to look on the sky, and in blue and red apparent magnitude space, to try and directly detect the DLA galaxy itself.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 7C1354+2552 B as a southeast companion galaxy detected in emission lines, with star-formation-like line ratios and CGM kinematics.

quotes.quote: In Vayner21b we discovered a companion galaxy (7C1354+2552 B) towards the southeast direction. This galaxy is detected in the , , and emission lines. The line ratios are consistent with a star-formation as the primary source of gas photoionization with a rate of 30 .
The velocity of galaxy 7C1354+2552 B indicates that it is likely associated with the redshifted kinematic component detected in \ and \ towards the south-west.

gmw_00001795.name: Sculptor
gmw_00001795.overview: The Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy is located near the South Galactic Pole [arXiv:astro-ph/0601547]. It exhibits a radially biased dynamical structure [arXiv:1204.5181] and a line-of-sight velocity distribution that is more peaked than a Gaussian [arXiv:1401.6195].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Sculptor dSph velocity data, using two complementary samples and finding positive symmetric deviations, radial orbital bias, and an outwardly increasing velocity-dispersion profile.

quotes.quote: Sculptor The available sample from Wa09data contains 1370 line of sight velocity measurements with membership probability higher than 0.5. We adopt the coordinates (J2000) of the dSph`s centre from Ma98 : ( _0, _0)=(1^h 00^m 09^s, -33^ 42.5') . The photometric ellipticity e=0.26 0.01 and position angle _ MA =-85.3^ 0.9^ are taken from deB11 . As reported in Section attenuation , this kinematic sample comes with a (normalized) level of uncertainty of _m/ 0.325 . We accompany this kinematic sample with the one provided by Ba08 and then recalibrated by St10 (results related with this datasets are displayed in green in all relevant Figures). To avoid misalignment between the catalogs, for this second dataset we use the dSph center as determined in deB11 . Even though the number of line of sight velocity measurements is lower with 629 giants, they cover a significantly more extended radial region (see Fig sculptor2d ), which makes the two datasets complementary. Also, a smaller (normalized) level of uncertainty _m/ 0.17 is achieved.

The symmetric deviations display a marked preference for positive values of s (and h_4 ) for the entire radial range covered by the tracers. This behaviour is confirmed by both datasets, which are found in perfect agreement. Identical profiles (within the uncertainties) are found for the major axis and minor axis regions. Taken at face value, these results support a radially biased dynamical structure, which was also the result of Am12 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Sculptor dwarf spheroidal kinematics with virial shape parameters, using its photometric and velocity data to test whether its dark matter halo is cuspy or cored.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: central RA-Dec coordinates of _C=01^h00^m09^s and _C= -33^ o 42.5^

quotes.quote: We will use rich data from the dwarf spheroidal galaxy Sculptor as there is evidence for a kurtosis which is different from Gaussian at a statistically significant level. We therefore hope that this will make it a good object to see the power of higher order techniques. For the time being we will restrict our fit to consider only solutions of the Jeans equations with constant velocity anisotropy , a fixed Plummer profile (Eq. tracezhao with _ =2 , _ =5 and _ =0 ) for the stars and simple two parameter density profiles so that we can see more clearly how can distinguish between different solutions.

Let us now introduce the variables for the Sculptor data. Using Eqs. zetaestA and zetaestB we found _A = 3.43 and _B = 3.69 . Green likelihood regions in Fig. sculptconstep show the 1 and 2 confidence regions for that are discussed in zetastat . We re-emphasize that these regions are derived purely from the data and therefore remain fixed in both panels. The large observed value of _A 3.5 indicates that Sculptor has a more peaked LOS velocity distribution than a Gaussian _A=3 which is consistent with an analysis made of the same data set with Gauss-Hermite moments.

sources.summary: The paper measures Sculptor's proper motion, reviews its discovery, gives its celestial and Galactic position, and describes it as a dwarf spheroidal near the South Galactic Pole.

quotes.quote: This article presents a measurement of the proper motion of the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy determined from images taken with the Hubble Space Telescope using the Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph in the imaging mode. Each of two distinct fields contains a quasi-stellar object which serves as the ``reference point.'' The measured proper motion of Sculptor, expressed in the equatorial coordinate system, is ( _ , _ )=(9 13,2 13) mas century ^ 1 . Removing the contributions from the motion of the Sun and the motion of the Local Standard of Rest produces the proper motion in the Galactic-rest-frame: ( _ ^ Grf , _ ^ Grf ) = (-23 13, 45 13) mas century ^ 1 .

Shapley (1938) discovered the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy --- the first example of this type of galaxy in the vicinity of the Milky Way --- on a plate with a 3 hour exposure time taken with the Bruce telescope. Shapely notes ``... that systems such as the Sculptor cluster may not be uncommon; their luminosity characteristics would enable them to escape easy discovery.'' Since the detection of Sculptor, astronomers have identified eight other dSphs. Sculptor is at a celestial location of ( , ) = (01^ h 00^ m 09^ s , -33^ 42^ 30^ ) (J2000.0; Mateo 1998), which corresponds to Galactic coordinates of ( , b)=(287 5,-83 2) . Thus, Sculptor lies nearly at the South Galactic Pole.

gmw_00001796.name: IC 711
gmw_00001796.overview: IC 711 is a head-tail radio galaxy located in the Abell 1314 cluster [arXiv:1610.07783, arXiv:2205.01413]. It is recognized as the longest known tailed radio galaxy [arXiv:1709.07421, arXiv:1611.02700], featuring thin, asymmetric radio jets [arXiv:1709.07421, arXiv:1810.01894] and an extended radio tail that exhibits sharp bends [arXiv:1610.07783, arXiv:2205.01413].

sources.summary: The paper presents GMRT observations of IC 711, describing its 700 kpc narrow-angle radio tail, spectral break, sharp bends, and inconsistency with a simple circular-orbit tail model.

quotes.quote: We present low-frequency, Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) observations at 240, 610 and 1300 MHz of IC 711, a narrow angle tail (NAT) radio galaxy. The galaxy lies in Abell cluster 1314 (redshift 0.034 ) and has a long radio tail of total angular extent 17 arcmin, corresponding to a projected linear size of 700 kpc. This makes it the longest head-tail radio galaxy known. The objectives of the GMRT observations were to investigate the diffuse-emission of the long tail structure at low frequencies. The radio structure, especially initial 10 arcmin of tail being a long straight feature, does not seem to be consistent with a simple circular motion around the cluster centre, as previously suggested in the literature.

The GMRT images of IC 711 at 610 MHz is presented in Fig. 1. The 610-MHz image with an angular resolution of 7. ^ 8 5. ^ 2 along a position angle of 71 ^ , shows the radio galaxy IC 711 having a long tail of total angular extent 17 arcmin. This high resolution map shows that the radio tail along its stretch is initially straight for two-thirds ( 10 arcmin) of its length , then there is a sharp (almost a 90^ ) bend after 4 arcmin, and then further along there is an other similar sharp bend pointing away from the cluster center and the diffuse source tail continues for another 3 arcmin.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 711 as the longest known head-tail galaxy, with thin radio jets, extended low-frequency tails, orbital motion around its cluster, and spectral-index variations along the tail.

quotes.quote: IC 711 IC 711 is the longest head tail galaxy known 1987AJ.....94....1V,1988Ap&SS.149..225V,1988AJ.....95.1360V . The high resolution maps of IC 711 at 610 and 240 MHz are presented in Figure ic711 . Thin, unresolved jets are seen to emerge from the radio core. Later on, both of the jets appear to widen into a high surface brightness feature, similar to a hot spot. The low-resolution images reveal much more extended features at the end of the tail.

The galaxy seemed to have moved in an orbital trajectory, about the centroid of the cluster whose position is marked by the compact radio source IC 712 to the east of the radio tail. There is a sudden disruption toward the end of the tail. The low-resolution image at 240 MHz shows much more emission toward the extreme ends of the tails, pointing to comparatively high spectral index in that region.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 711 as a head-tail radio galaxy with a long tail, high RM dispersion, and disturbed diffuse emission possibly shaped by cluster processes.

quotes.quote: The brightest of these, IC 708 and IC 711, are bent tail galaxies, and IC 711 in particular has a very long radio tail extending for almost a mega-parsec in a SE-NW direction across the cluster vallee1976 . Observations from 2016 with the GMRT srivastava2020 showed a spectral break part of the way along the tail of IC 711, and suggested that this may arise due to local environmental factors within the cluster. This was supported by later observations, which showed low-levels of diffuse emission perpendicular to the main direction of the IC 711 tail, again indicating a disturbance within A1314 sebastian2017 .

The head-tail radio galaxy IC 711 (Source 9; Table tab:a1314-sources ) has a similarly high RM dispersion to IC 708 with a value of _ RM = 59.85 rad m ^ 2 . The full extension of the long tail of emission associated with this source seen at longer wavelengths sebastian2017,wilber2019 is not visible in these higher frequency VLA data at this resolution. We note however that the discrete compact source listed as Source 6 in Table tab:a1314-sources and shown in Figure fig:a1314_overlay , is coincident with the location of the northern east-west extension of emission perpendicular to the main tail of IC 711.

This abrupt turn in the direction of the diffuse emission from IC 711 was first noted by and also detected by and . Those works proposed that the turn in emission was potentially due to a disturbance caused by ram pressure or shocks propagating outwards in the intra-cluster medium.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J113446.55+485721.9, also IC 711, as an extended head-tail radio source with bent asymmetric jets and a flat-spectrum core.

quotes.quote: In Fig.  ic711 we present the images obtained as part of our program of VLA observations of SDSS J113446.55+485721.9 which shows a pair of bent asymmetric jets extending for 90 \ from the radio core. This source, also known as IC 711, is actually the longest head-tail radio source known vallee76 , extending over 500 kpc vallee87 . Its extended nature is clearly seen also in the NVSS image (see Fig. NVSS ).
Nonetheless, the case of IC 711 indicates that they can still be used to improve the sources morphological classification.

Regarding IC 711, the core flux densities measured from the VLA observations are 25.92 0.03 at 1.5 GHz, 24.30 0.02 at 4.5 GHz, and 23.23 0.01 at 7.5 GHz, respectively, showing a flat spectrum with = - 0.07. In this source the jets are highly asymmetric in the innermost regions, but their brightness ratio decreases at larger distances, possibly due to a deceleration from relativistic to subsonic bicknell95 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 711 as a spectacular one-megaparsec tailed radio galaxy whose sensitive LoTSS observations can map tail extent and spectral-index variations.

quotes.quote: However, few tailed radio galaxies are more spectacular than IC 711 ( z =0.034) which, at a length of 1 Mpc, is one of the longest known tailed radio galaxies (see e.g. Vallee_1976 ). Detailed studies of tailed radio galaxies like this provide a history of their motion and of the interaction between the tails and the ICM. For example, the oldest region of the tails of IC 711 is thought to be 2 Gyrs old, and the multiple intensity variations along the structure are thought to be caused by in situ reaccelaration, whereas the abrupt increase in the width of the tail close to its northern edge may reflect a sudden change in the jets physical conditions within the optical nucleus 1.6 Gyrs ago or the properties of the surrounding ICM ( Vallee_1988 ). The LoTSS observations of IC 711 that are presented in Figure fig:science-examples are by far the most sensitive low-frequency observations of this object. A careful analysis may reveal that the jet is even longer than previously known, and by combining with higher frequency measurements the spectral index variations within the tails can be accurately mapped to help further understand the particle acceleration mechanisms within the tails.

gmw_00001797.name: IC 708
gmw_00001797.overview: IC 708 is a wide-angle-tail radio galaxy located in the Abell 1314 cluster [arXiv:1610.07783, arXiv:2205.01413]. The galaxy features a highly bent radio morphology with distinct northern and southern lobes that exhibit structured polarization and a line-of-sight magnetic field reversal [arXiv:1610.07783, arXiv:2205.01413].

sources.summary: The paper contrasts IC 708 with IC 711, identifying IC 708 as a bent wide-angle-tail radio galaxy with separate lobes and a comparatively non-steep spectrum.

quotes.quote: The galaxy lies in Abell cluster 1314 (redshift z 0.034 ) which hosts another radio galaxy IC 708 in the peculiar shape of a highly bent WAT. The radio structures of the radio galaxies IC 711 and IC 708 are very dissimilar. The radio galaxy IC 711 has a narrow long tail of total angular extent 17 arcmin. On the other hand IC 708 has a rather 'squat' radio morphology, with a wide-angle tail structure with a maximum extension of about 2 arcmin. These two objects have been studied with the Westerbork telescope (Vall ee Wilson 1976; Wilson Vall ee 1977; J agers 1987; Vall ee Strom 1988) and the Very Large Array (O'Dea and Owen 1985; Owen Ledlow 1997).

On the other hand for IC 708 N and S components or even of the whole radio source the spectrum is not steep (Table 2). In fact from the 5 GHz map of Vall ee Wilson (1976) it seems the break frequency for IC 708 N and S components is > _ 5 GHz. Our integrated 1300 MHz flux density measurements 791 24 mJy of IC 708 are consistent with the measurements 835 80 mJy at 1.4 GHz by Wilson Vall ee (1977). Also our 610 MHz flux density value (1363 42 mJy) is equally consistent with the value 1432 70 mJy at 610 MHz, as measured by Vall ee Wilson (1976).

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 708 as a wide-angle-tail radio galaxy in Abell 1314 with structured polarization, high RM dispersion, and lobe RM sign reversal.

quotes.quote: The brightest of these, IC 708 and IC 711, are bent tail galaxies, and IC 711 in particular has a very long radio tail extending for almost a mega-parsec in a SE-NW direction across the cluster vallee1976 .

The wide angle tail galaxy IC 708 (Source 3; Table tab:a1314-sources ) shows significant structure in polarisation across its lobes and this is reflected in the high RM dispersion value of _ RM = 58.49 rad m ^ 2 for this source, see Figure fig:ic708 and Table tab:a1314_pol . The host galaxy of IC 708 has a much higher RM than the lobes: _ peak = 136.5 0.17 rad m ^ 2 , suggesting that it is affected by more local magneto-ionic structure from the interstellar medium of the host galaxy. Table tab:ic708_pol shows RM statistics separately for the core region, and the North and South lobes of IC 708. The areas used to define these regions are indicated in Figure fig:ic708 . We note that the high RM dispersion indicated for the core region is heavily affected by the sharp transition in RM at the base of the jet for the Northern lobe.

From Table tab:ic708_pol it can also be seen that the North and South lobes have different signs for their average RM, indicating that there is a field reversal along the line of sight between these lobes.

gmw_00001798.name: NGC 2699
gmw_00001798.overview: NGC 2699 is an early-type galaxy with a central kinematic major axis that is misaligned from its photometric major axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0404034]. Its central region contains a rapidly rotating component with a local velocity maximum and a weak hint of a decoupled structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0404034].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 2699 has a misaligned central kinematic major axis, a rapidly rotating component, anticorrelated h3 structure, and long-slit hints of decoupling.

quotes.quote: The kinematical major-axis in the central region is misaligned from the photometric major-axis. The mean velocity map shows a rapidly rotating component with a local maximum at about 5 \ from the centre: within this region, h_3 departs significantly from zero and is anticorrelated with V . There is a weak hint of such a decoupled structure in the data of SP00 .

gmw_00001799.name: NGC 3489
gmw_00001799.overview: NGC 3489 is a lenticular satellite galaxy of the Leo Group [arXiv:1304.1382, arXiv:1712.05833] that contains a weak large-scale bar [arXiv:0912.2511] and a pseudobulge [arXiv:1012.3073, arXiv:0912.2511]. The galaxy is in a post-starburst phase [arXiv:astro-ph/0311204] with disturbed ionized and molecular gas [arXiv:0912.0274], likely due to the recent accretion of a small gas cloud or companion [arXiv:1007.2059].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3489 as a lower-mass star-forming early type with modest SFR, older SSP ages, disturbed ionized and molecular gas, and young populations outside current PAH-emitting regions.

quotes.quote: Widespread Star-Forming Fast Rotators In Figure sfrmstar , the \ galaxies (NGC 3032, NGC 3156, NGC 3489, NGC 4150, NGC 4550) are seen to be those star-forming early-type galaxies with lower stellar masses. Given the comparable molecular content of all star-forming galaxies in our sample (see Table tbl:sfr ), the \ galaxies are consequently also characterized by higher gas fractions and specific star formation rates. As a result, the current star formation event dominates the single stellar population (SSP) ages, which are measured to be young throughout these systems ( < 1 3 Gyr; Kuntschner et al. in prep ). This is consistent with the location of the \ galaxies in Figure ks ; these systems have molecular gas and star formation surface densities similar to those found in late-type galaxies undergoing global star formation.

NGC 3156, NGC 3489, NGC 4150; SFR = 0.03 0.06 \ ) have SSP ages older by 1 2 Gyr, an order of magnitude less molecular gas, and high / \ ratios that are inconsistent with significant on-going star formation .

Lower SFRs in a \ galaxy also correlates with an increasingly irregular star formation and gas distribution, from a regular, disk-like morphology (NGC 3032) to disturbed ionized and molecular gas distributions (NGC 3489; Figure Main-SF and Crocker et al. in prep ) to highly asymmetric molecular gas distributions (NGC 4550; Cro+09 ). These increasingly irregular morphologies additionally correspond to more centralized star formation at lower SFR (compare NGC 3032, NGC 3156, and NGC 4550 in Figure profiles a ). In the absence of a replenishing gas reservoir, \ galaxies will experience declining SFRs, causing them to move to the left in Figure sfrmstar ; the system with the most intense star formation (NGC 3032) will therefore be expected to eventually move towards the other \ galaxies. Likewise, there is evidence that the \ galaxies with lower SFRs previously had more intense star formation events that extended to larger radii, implying earlier positions to the right of their current location in Figure sfrmstar . In particular, the young SSP ages (1 3 Gyr in NGC 3156, NGC 3489, NGC 4150; 6 Gyr in NGC 4550; Kuntschner et al. in prep ) found throughout these galaxies, beyond the star-forming regions observed in PAH emission, indicate that star formation activity occurred out to larger radii in the past than in the present.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3489 as a Leo Group satellite S0 in a six-galaxy lenticular sample, using planetary nebulae to separate spheroid and disc kinematics out to large radii.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we therefore apply the kinematic bulge--disc decomposition technique developed in Ari to the sample of six S0s with suitable PNe data presented in Ari2 . This sample was selected to span a range of environments in order to see if the evolutionary properties depend systematically on surroundings: NGC 7457 and NGC 3115 are isolated; NGC 1023 and NGC 2768 are the dominant galaxies of two small groups (5-6 members); while NGC 3384 and NGC 3489 are satellite galaxies of the Leo Group (30 members). The Hubble types of these galaxies and the adopted distance moduli are listed in Table tab:galfitinput .

In this paper, we have analysed the kinematics of a sample of six lenticular galaxies using the planetary nebulae data presented in Ari2 to determine their dynamical properties out to large radii. This analysis has emphasised the importance of treating the spheroidal and disc components of these systems as distinct entities both photometrically and kinematically, and doing a careful job of separating them. Through this process, the rather heterogeneous properties of S0s over-all start to be resolved into more consistent sub-components, and a reasonably coherent picture begins to emerge.

sources.summary: The paper reports strong central H absorption in NGC 3489, a younger central population, no clear counter-rotation, and a fast-rotating subcomponent with a mild velocity twist.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:sau_oas_comp e compares the H absorption index observed with and for NGC 3489, which shows strong H absorption in the center. The improved image-quality of the observations shows that the strength of absorption in the center is increasing within the resolution limit of . This makes a significant difference to the luminosity-weighted age determined in the central lenslets.

The fourth object, NGC 3489, does not exhibit the clear counter-rotation that the other objects show. Closer inspection of the velocity field does, however, indicate that there is a central fast-rotating sub-component. The curve of zero velocity also shows some evidence of a (spatially resolved) mild twist across the galaxy center, indicating that there may be some departure from regular axisymmetric rotation in the central parts.

sources.summary: NGC 3489 has an aligned inner rotating H I structure, a low-column-density tail, and molecular gas, suggesting accretion of a small gas cloud or companion.

quotes.quote: NGC 3489 In NGC 3489 we find an inner rotating \ structure aligned with the galaxy, as well as a low-column density tail. The total \ mass of this barred galaxy is 5.8 10^6 . This galaxy might be in the process of accreting a small gas cloud or companion, and forming an inner disc from this material. The central \ structure shows regular rotation with the same sense of rotation as the ionised gas and the stellar component. Molecular gas with a mass of 1.2 10^7 has been detected in this galaxy by .

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC3489 to its sample and treats it as a pseudobulge galaxy; it is consistent with the classical-bulge relation and remains within the observed scatter.

quotes.quote: To increase the number of objects with low , we add to the sample NGC3368 and NGC3489 whose \ is estimated in Nowak et al. (2010).

Figure fg:mbh illustrates that 4 out of 9 pseudobulges (NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594) are consistent with the correlation for classical bulges, while the others (CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368) are outliers, at more than 4 below the LINMIX ERR linear regression.
The other pseudobulges NGC3489, NGC4258, NGC4374, NGC4594 are within the observed scatter.

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 3489 from the early-type sample because its unusual spiral-arm molecular gas and irregular velocity field suggest instability or recent disturbance.

quotes.quote: This sample is made of all galaxies for which this information is available, after removing one galaxy (NGC 3489). NGC 3489 has a very unusual molecular gas distribution, including spiral arms. This suggests that the gas is gravitationally unstable, and is consistent with NGC 3489 being a flat, discy galaxy, with stars that might contribute to the disc instability. In addition, the molecular gas has an irregular velocity field suggesting that it has been recently accreted or disturbed Crocker2011 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3489 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This S0 galaxy belongs to the Leo I group. The ATLAS^ 3D kinematic map 2011MNRAS.414.2923K shows a fast rotation along the photometric major axis, whose amplitude reaches 115 at 1 . PN data show that the halo of this galaxy is also rotating at >40 . The dispersion is around 80 , consistent with 2011MNRAS.413..813C .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3489 as a post-starburst galaxy with strong central H absorption, a young central stellar population, and a luminosity-weighted core age of about 1.5 Gyr.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:ngc3489_stellarpop presents a map of H absorption strength for the galaxy NGC 3489 (left panel) which shows a pronounced peak in the central 1 , indicative of a young central stellar population. The right panel of Figure fig:ngc3489_stellarpop quantifies this, plotting H absorption strength against the abundance-insensitive metallicity indicator [ MgFe50 ] ^ [ MgFe50 ] ^ = 0.45 Mg b + Fe5015 2 (Kuntschner et al. in prep.) from the data. The young stellar population in the core of this galaxy indicates that it is in a post-starburst phase, with a luminosity-weighted age of around 1.5 Gyr. Equivalent data are also shown, illustrating that both data sets are consistent (see caption for details).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3489 as dust-perturbed in photometry, with inner iso-velocity changes indicating fast rotation and h3 behavior that turns over inward.

quotes.quote: The photometry is perturbed by dust extinction. The effect is not evident in the kinematic maps although it may explain the local increase in the dispersion map at about 15 \ West from the centre Ca00 . Inside 4 \ the opening angle of the iso-velocities decreases, indicating a fast rotating structure. h_3 decreases inwards, with a turn over at a radius of about 4 , inside of which it shows a small increase.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3489 as an S0 galaxy with a weak bar, pseudobulge and classical bulge, weak nuclear activity, and a modeled SMBH mass near 6 x 10^6 solar masses.

quotes.quote: The S0 galaxy NGC 3489 has only a weak large-scale bar, a small pseudobulge and a small classical bulge.
Both galaxies show weak nuclear activity in the optical, indicative of the presence of a supermassive black hole.

For NGC 3489, a solution without black hole cannot be excluded when modelling the SINFONI data alone, but can be clearly ruled out when modelling a combination of SINFONI, OASIS and SAURON data, for which we obtain M_ =(6.00^ +0.56 _ 0.54 |_ stat 0.64|_ sys ) 10^ 6 M _ .

gmw_00001800.name: NGC 4270
gmw_00001800.overview: NGC 4270 is an early-type galaxy characterized by a flat velocity curve in its central 5 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0404034]. At large radii, the galaxy exhibits a nearly cylindrical velocity field [arXiv:astro-ph/0404034].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4270 has a nearly cylindrical large-radius velocity field, changing inner iso-velocity contours, increasing h3 amplitude, and a flat central velocity curve.

quotes.quote: At large radii, the velocity field is close to cylindrical. Inside 10 \ the opening angle of the iso-velocity contours decreases, with a corresponding increase in the h_3 amplitude. The velocity curve in the central 5 \ is flat SP97a .

gmw_00001801.name: J0003 1053
gmw_00001801.overview: J0003 1053 is a multi-component radio source with differing near-infrared and optical centroid positions [arXiv:1512.07707]. Its near-infrared emission lines indicate a redshift of 1.474, whereas its optical photometric redshift is estimated at 0.57 [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0003 1053 as a multi-component source with differing NIR/optical centroids, a very red SED, and evidence for an obscured background object or foreground galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0003 1053 has four components in its 4.9 GHz and 8.5 GHz images. Its NIR centroid position is coincident with the ``C'' component, which has an inverted spectral index. All other components have steep spectral indices < -0.5 . However, the optical centroid position is close to the ``B'' component. A redshift of 1.474 0.001 is obtained from emission lines in the NIR (see Paper 1 ), but the photometric redshift given by SDSS DR8 is 0.57 0.12 .

gmw_00001802.name: J0807 + 5327
gmw_00001802.overview: J0807 + 5327 is a radio galaxy that exhibits a double-lobe radio structure [arXiv:1512.07707]. This entire radio structure is offset to the south of the optical and near-infrared centroid positions [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper finds J0807 + 5327 has a double-lobe radio structure offset from the optical/NIR object, with a compact radio source and diffuse galaxy alignment favorable for absorption searches.

quotes.quote: J0807 + 5327 has a double-lobe radio structure. However, the entire radio structure is to the south of the optical and NIR centroid positions by 1.5 , which makes it possible that the radio source is not associated with the optical + NIR object we detect. If this is a core-jet object with a core in the ``A'' component, the core is still 0.5 \ away from the NIR centroid position. The VLBA detected source appears similar morphologically to a hot spot in a single radio lobe, identified as component ``A'' in Figure 5 in the Appendix. However, there is no bright source to the NE of this position which would be the second associated lobe of this source.

gmw_00001803.name: J0917 + 4725
gmw_00001803.overview: J0917 + 4725 is a radio source that exhibits extended structure in its 4.9 GHz and 8.5 GHz images [arXiv:1512.07707]. Its morphology is consistent with a radio-lobe and hotspot system, though its true optical host galaxy remains unknown [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper argues J0917 + 4725 is likely a radio-lobe/hotspot system offset from the NIR galaxy, making absorption detection unlikely.

quotes.quote: J0917 + 4725 shows extended structure in its 4.9 GHz and 8.5 GHz images, and the radio center is 1 3 away from the NIR center. We overlay a larger field-of-view FIRST field with an band SDSS image in Figure fig:J0917 . The maps in Figure 5 in Appendix I are at higher resolution than the FIRST image and show a morphology consistent with being a single radio lobe with its hot spot detected by the VLBA. A second radio lobe is seen in the northeast 40 \ away, making it unlikely that the NIR object shown in Figure fig:J0917 is the host galaxy. It is unknown which is the optical counterpart to the radio source.

gmw_00001804.name: J0920 + 2714
gmw_00001804.overview: J0920 + 2714 is a background, double-lobe radio source with a steep spectrum [arXiv:1512.07707]. It is offset on the sky from a foreground S0 galaxy at a redshift of z=0.2064, which exhibits a slightly redshifted H I absorption line [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0920 + 2714 as an LSO behind or offset from an S0 galaxy with detected H I absorption, whose components may trace disk gas.

quotes.quote: J0920 + 2714 is an LSO with the brighter lobe overlapping with but not centered on an elliptical galaxy at z=0.209 (1 5 away from optical center). An H 1 absorption line has been detected at the redshift of the elliptical galaxy (see Section sec:HIobs . We conclude that the optical--NIR galaxy is not the counterpart to the radio source; the radio source counterpart is too faint and/or too obscured to be seen on our deepest optical and NIR images. The USS for this radio source and the absence of a strong core suggest that this radio source is likely at high- z .

J0920 + 2714 See Figure fig:0920 for the 21 cm spectrum from the GBT where we observed J0920 + 2714 in 2006 through an exploratory program. We later obtained an optical redshift in 2009 and found that the broad 21 cm absorption feature is slightly redshifted ( v 65 km s ^ 1 ) with respect to the galaxy nucleus. This is an ``intervening'' H I system in which a double-lobe, steep spectrum radio source is in the background (unknown redshift) and is well-offset on the sky from the center of an S0 galaxy at z=0.2064 0.0002 in the foreground. The S0 classification is based both upon a visual inspection of the SDSS and APO 3.5m NIR images and the optical + NIR SED which is matched quite well by an S0 template from mannucci:2001 .

gmw_00001805.name: J1341 + 1032
gmw_00001805.overview: J1341 + 1032 is a medium-sized symmetric radio galaxy [arXiv:1512.07707]. The galaxy is offset from its optical and near-infrared center, making it a candidate for detecting absorption lines at the redshift of a diffuse foreground galaxy [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1341 + 1032 as an MSO offset from its optical/NIR center, making it a candidate for absorption at the redshift of a diffuse foreground galaxy.

quotes.quote: J1341 + 1032 is also an MSO which is offset from its optical + NIR center similar to J0807 + 5327 and J0920 + 2714. This makes it a good candidate to detect absorption lines at the redshift of the offset foreground galaxy, which is very diffuse and has an SDSS z_ phot 0.4. A possible detection of the optical/NIR counterpart to the radio source can be seen between components A B in the NIR image in the Appendix.

gmw_00001806.name: J1559 + 4349
gmw_00001806.overview: J1559 + 4349 is a likely gravitational lens system where the foreground lens dominates the optical light [arXiv:1512.07707]. Conversely, the background active galactic nucleus host galaxy, which has a redshift of 1.232, dominates the near-infrared emission [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1559 + 4349 as a likely gravitational-lens case where optical light is dominated by the foreground lens and the z=1.232 AGN host dominates in the NIR.

quotes.quote: J1559 + 4349 has a NIR redshift of 1.232 0.001 (Paper 1 ) and an optical photometric redshift of 0.42 0.09 according to SDSS DR8 eisenstein:2011 . We classify its optical + NIR SED as Q+abs because it is bluer than an Sc-type galaxy. Figure fig:J1559 shows that the K band center (red cross) is close to the radio core position. If the radio structure is part of an Einstein ring with radius 0 64, the center of the ring is close to the band center (blue cross). Therefore, we suggest that the foreground lens dominates the optical light, while the background AGN host at z=1.232 is dominant in the NIR.

gmw_00001807.name: J1147 + 4818
gmw_00001807.overview: J1147 + 4818 is classified as a core-jet radio source based on its VLA-A images [arXiv:1512.07707]. High-resolution VLBA imaging of the object reveals an arc-like string of components lying on a circle with a radius of 0.20 arcseconds [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1147 + 4818 as a core-jet source with arc-like VLBA components but concludes it is a less likely gravitationally lensed background source.

quotes.quote: J1147 + 4818 is classified as a CJ object based on its VLA-A images. No emission from component ``B'' (as is seen on the VLA-A 4.9 and 8.5 GHz maps in the Appendix) is detected in the 1.4 GHz VLBA image. We show in Figure fig:J1147 that the arc-like string of VLBA components lies on a circle with radius 0 20. The optical and NIR centroid positions are coincident and both are in the vicinity of the arc although their uncertainties are large. J1147 + 4818 is a Q type object but DR8 gives a photometric redshift of z=0.17 0.05 by assuming it is a galaxy.

gmw_00001808.name: J0834 + 1700
gmw_00001808.overview: J0834 + 1700 is a radio galaxy that constitutes one lobe of a double-lobe structure [arXiv:1512.07707]. This radio source is closely aligned with its optical and near-infrared counterpart, which spans a distance of 25 arcseconds [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0834 + 1700 as one lobe of a double-lobe structure aligned with the optical/NIR object, but a poor intervening-absorption candidate.

quotes.quote: J0834 + 1700 is one lobe in a double-lobe structure 25 \ apart (see Figure fig:J0834 ), similar to another single-lobe object J0917 + 4725. The radio source is so well-aligned with the optical + NIR object that no significant positional offset is present, unlike in the case of J0917 + 4725. Absorption lines may be detected if the radio source is in the background, although the large amount of extended radio flux makes this a poor candidate for finding intervening absorption.

gmw_00001809.name: J0901 + 0304
gmw_00001809.overview: J0901 + 0304 is an Sc-type host galaxy [arXiv:1512.07707, arXiv:2108.08122] that may be an interacting system with diffuse structure extending to the northeast [arXiv:1512.07707, arXiv:2108.08122]. It contains a LINER or weak Seyfert ionization source [arXiv:1512.07707] and exhibits two H I absorption components [arXiv:1512.07707, arXiv:2108.08122].

sources.summary: The paper reports confirmed H I absorption in J0901 + 0304, a GPS/possible CSO Sc galaxy with LINER or weak Seyfert lines and infalling gas features.

quotes.quote: We first observed this object using the GBT and found two tentative absorption features. One feature is in a small RFI-free window while the other is badly contaminated by RFI at Green Bank. We later confirmed both features using the VLA and GMRT where the RFI was not as difficult an issue. J0901 + 0304 has a pure Sc galaxy (``G''-type) optical + NIR SED based on template SEDs from mannucci:2001 and its optical and NIR images suggest that it may be an interacting system with some diffuse structure extending to the NE of the main galaxy. The optical spectrum obtained by us at the APO 3.5m telescope includes moderate strength narrow emission lines whose line ratios suggest a LINER or weak Seyfert ionization source kewley:2006 .

Two H 1 absorption lines are detected, one coincident with the optical redshift of z =0.2872 0.0001, the other redshifted by a velocity of 328 km s ^ 1 . Since the broad line has a velocity consistent with the nucleus, we identify this absorption as disk gas, which leaves the narrow component as a likely infalling high-velocity cloud (HVC). This broad-narrow absorption-line pairing is quite similar to the H I absorption lines found by keeney:2011 at low-redshift ( z =0.018) in PKS 1327 206.

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J090151+030423 has two absorption components, an unresolved JVLA continuum, an Sc/interacting host morphology, and a 380 mJy radio flux.

quotes.quote: Our JVLA \ absorption spectrum of SDSS J090151+030423 (hereafter J0901+0304) is shown in Fig. fig:det_spectra . The absorption has two components: a shallow component, blueshifted by 80 \ from the systemic velocity, and a narrow deep absorption 240 , \ redshifted with respect to the systemic velocity. Both the components have an FWZI of 120 . The \ absorption was already reported from independent observations by Yan16 . Even though the frequency range is affected by RFI, multiple observations with the VLA and the GMRT Yan16 , and the JVLA (this work) agree on the two-component profile.

From their optical and NIR observations, further suggest that the host galaxy is an Sc galaxy based on the SED templates from Mannucci01 and its optical and NIR images suggest that it may be an interacting system with some diffuse structure extending to the north-east of the main galaxy. We discuss the interpretation of the absorption profile also taking into account the difference in the systemic velocity in Sect. sec:0901_results . The radio continuum is unresolved at the arcsec resolution of our observations (Fig. fig:continuum_maps and Table radio_obs ). We measure a total flux density of 380 40 mJy.

gmw_00001810.name: J1129 + 5638
gmw_00001810.overview: J1129 + 5638 is a narrow-line radio galaxy [arXiv:1512.07707] and a steep-spectrum compact symmetric object candidate [arXiv:1512.07707]. It exhibits broad 21 cm absorption consistent with normal disk gas [arXiv:1512.07707], and its amorphous optical morphology features a blue southern extension that may be a companion galaxy [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1129 + 5638 as a steep-spectrum CSO candidate/narrow-line radio galaxy with broad 21 cm absorption interpreted as disk gas in a massive galaxy.

quotes.quote: J1129 + 5638 See Figure fig:1129 for the redshifted H I spectrum of J1129 + 5638 taken at the GBT. It is a steep-spectrum source which is a CSO candidate according to our VLBA observations (see the radio images in the Appendix). Its optical/NIR SED is best fit by a pure ``G''-type SED with an Sc-type spiral galaxy template and at ground-based resolution the optical/NIR morphology is amorphous (see Appendix) with a blue extension to the south that may be a companion galaxy. We recently used Gemini/GMOS to obtain a redshift of 0.8925 0.0008 from strong narrow emission lines (see Figure fig:1129gmos ). The presence of the high ionization [ Ne 5 ] line classifies this source as a narrow-line radio galaxy.

The 21 cm absorption is quite broad (FWHM = 318 km s ^ 1 if fit by a single Gaussian profile) at a redshift consistent with the redshift of the optical emission lines to within rather broad errors ( 120 km s ^ 1 ). Thus, this detection is consistent either with normal disk gas or outflowing material. However, the CSO-type radio morphology is not consistent with an outflow in our direction.

gmw_00001811.name: J1357 + 0046
gmw_00001811.overview: J1357 + 0046 is a radio galaxy with a compact core surrounded by diffuse emission [arXiv:1512.07707]. Very Long Baseline Array observations of the source reveal a clear compact symmetric object structure [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper reports H I absorption in J1357 + 0046 despite lacking a spectroscopic redshift, describing a compact diffuse source with obvious CSO structure and ambiguous absorber origin.

quotes.quote: J1357 + 0046 See Figure fig:1357 for the redshifted GBT spectrum for this source. This is the only successful detection made in our program without the benefit of an optical/NIR spectroscopic redshift and was made only because the GBT PF-1 receiver has a very broad bandwidth since the H I detection redshift ( z= 0.7971,0.7962) is quite different from the z_ phot = 0.57 of the optical galaxy. This large discrepancy is due almost certainly to the presence of a significant amount of non-thermal continuum in the spectrum producing a substantial error in the photo- z estimate; i.e., the optical/NIR SED classification is ``G + Q''. The SDSS and APO NIR images (see Appendix) are quite compact but with a significant amount of diffuse emission around what may be a compact core. J1357 + 0046 shows an obvious CSO VLBA structure (see Appendix).

gmw_00001812.name: J1604 + 6050
gmw_00001812.overview: J1604 + 6050 is a compact symmetric object candidate with an optical and near-infrared spectral energy distribution best fit by an S0-type galaxy template [arXiv:1512.07707]. It exhibits rather amorphous optical and near-infrared images and shows a tentative, extremely narrow 21 cm H I absorption line [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1604 + 6050 as a CSO candidate with tentative narrow 21 cm absorption at its Gemini optical redshift and a pure galaxy SED.

quotes.quote: J1604 + 6050 See Figure fig:1604 for a tentative detection of a single, narrow 21 cm absorption component at the optical redshift obtained by Gemini-GMOS (Figure fig:1604gmos ). The optical redshift of the weak, narrow emission lines is 0.5590 0.0004 and that of the tentative H I absorption line is 0.55913 0.00015 ( v 25 km s ^ 1 ). The tentative detection is an extremely narrow line with an FWHM of only 8 km/s. J1604 + 6050 is a CSO candidate in our VLBA map (see Appendix) with an optical + NIR SED best-fit by a pure ``G''-type SED with an S0-type galaxy template. The rather amorphous optical/NIR galaxy images have centroids consistent with the CSO centroid seen with the VLBA.

gmw_00001813.name: J1616 + 2647
gmw_00001813.overview: J1616 + 2647 is a compact radio galaxy at a redshift of 0.7553 [arXiv:1512.07707]. It is classified as a gigahertz-peaked spectrum source and a compact symmetric object candidate that exhibits an exceptionally broad H I absorption profile centered at its systemic redshift [arXiv:1512.07707].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1616 + 2647 as a GPS/CSO-candidate galaxy with broad H I absorption centered at systemic redshift, possibly disk gas in a massive galaxy or cluster environment.

quotes.quote: J1616 + 2647 See Figure fig:1616 for the 21 cm GBT spectrum of J1616 + 2647 which reveals a very broad, symmetric profile at the optical redshift. J1616 + 2647 is a GPS source which is a CSO candidate according to our VLBA observations (see Appendix). Its optical + NIR SED is best fit by an Sc-type galaxy template (i.e., pure ``G''-type). The optical/NIR images reveal a compact structure, well-centered at the CSO position. This source was observed by Gemini/GMOS to obtain a redshift of 0.7553 0.0003 from very strong, narrow emission lines (Figure fig:1616gmos ).

J1616 + 2647 has one of the broadest H I absorption systems detected so far with an FWHM = 447 km s ^ 1 . There are four objects that have 21 cm absorption lines broader than 400 km/s, including 3C 84 and PKS 2322 12, both cD galaxies close to the center of galaxy clusters inoue:1996,sarazin:1995 . While it would be natural to associate these broad absorptions with out-flowing gas, the absorption in J1616 + 2647 is centered at the systemic redshift and could also be partly or largely disk gas in an extremely massive galaxy.

gmw_00001814.name: GAL-CLUS-022058s
gmw_00001814.overview: GAL-CLUS-022058s is an ultrabright lensed submillimeter galaxy at a redshift of approximately 1.48 [arXiv:2106.14281]. It is gravitationally magnified by a foreground galaxy cluster and exhibits main-sequence star formation with a large molecular gas content [arXiv:2106.14281].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GAL-CLUS-022058s as a z=1.4796 lensed ultrabright submillimeter galaxy with strong magnification, large gas content, and main-sequence star formation.

quotes.quote: We report an ultra-bright lensed submillimeter galaxy at z_ spec =1.4796 , identified as a result of a full-sky cross-correlation of the AllWISE and Planck compact source catalogs aimed to search for bright submillimeter galaxies at z 1.5-2.8 . APEX/LABOCA observations of the candidate galaxy reveal a source with flux, S _ 870 m = 54 8 mJy. The position of the APEX source coincides with the position of the AllWISE mid-IR source, and with the Einstein ring GAL-CLUS-022058s, observed with the HST . Archival VLT/FORS observations reveal the redshift of this Einstein ring, z_ spec =1.4796 , and detection of the CO(5-4) line at z_ spec = 1.4802 with APEX/nFLASH230 confirms the redshift of the submillimeter emission. The lensed source appears to be gravitationally magnified by a massive foreground galaxy cluster lens at z = 0.36 .

We have determined an intrinsic rest-frame infrared luminosity of L_ IR 10^ 12 L_ and a likely star formation rate of 70-170 M_ \ yr^ 1 . The molecular gas mass is M_ mol 2.6 10^ 10 M_ and the gas fraction is f = 0.34 0.07 . We also obtain a stellar mass log (M_ /M_ ) = 10.7 0.1 and a specific star formation rate log (sSFR/Gyr^ 1 )=0.15 0.03 .

gmw_00001815.name: 4C 43.15
gmw_00001815.overview: 4C 43.15 is a high-redshift Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.4 [arXiv:astro-ph/0001349, arXiv:2306.00071]. It features a flat-spectrum radio core [arXiv:2306.00071], double-lobed radio-emitting hot spots [arXiv:astro-ph/0001349, arXiv:2306.00071], and extended hydrogen ionization cones powered by a hidden active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0001349].

sources.summary: The paper studies B3 0731+438 as a z=2.429 FR II radio galaxy, finding biconical H ionization cones powered by a hidden AGN and a massive stellar host.

quotes.quote: To examine the structure and properties of the H emission-line region in a high-redshift radio galaxy, we carried out high-resolution infrared imaging observations of a powerful radio galaxy, B3 0731+438, at z= 2.429 in the K^ band and the 2.25 m narrow-band. This object is a typical FR II radio galaxy with strong double-lobed radio-emitting hot spots and a central core (Carilli et al. \ 1997). The optical R band image shows a diffuse morphology with a few knots, while the narrow-band image presents aligned Ly emission clouds (McCarthy 1991).

Thus, we suggest that the emission mechanism of the extended H + [ N ii ] cones of B3 0731+438 is nebular emission from clouds ionized by the strong UV radiation of the hidden AGN. Spectral Energy Distribution Assuming that the exponential disk component of the K continuum image is a host galaxy of B3 0731+438 and the compact core a type-2 AGN, we reconstructed the SED of the B3 0731+438 with model spectra of a type-2 AGN and a galaxy.

The SED of the whole radio galaxy is modeled by a model spectrum of a 500 Myr-old instantaneous burst galaxy and dust-scattered power-law continuum with A_V=1.9 mag extinction. The stellar mass of the galaxy is 3 10^ 11 M_ , which is comparable to that of a typical 3C radio galaxy at z=1 .

sources.summary: The paper presents subarcsecond LOFAR observations of the z=2.4 radio galaxy 4C 43.15, measuring spectral-index structure, spectral age, injection indices, and inverse-Compton-loss implications.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: This gave us a position of _ K = 7 h 35 m 21.92 s , _ K = 43 44'20.70'' .

quotes.quote: Here we present subarcsecond observations of a z = 2.4 radio galaxy at frequencies between 121 MHz and 166 MHz. We measure the spatial distribution of spectra, and discuss the implications for models of the z relation. We targeted 4C 43.15 with the High Band Antennas (HBAs) of the International LOFAR Telescope (ILT) with a range of baselines up to 1300 \ km .

By complementing our data with archival Very Large Array (VLA) data we study the spectral index distribution across 4C 43.15 between 55 \ MHz and 8.4 \ GHz at resolutions of 0.4'' and 0.9'' . With a magnetic field strength of B = 5.2 nT and fitted injection indices of ^ north _ inj = -0.8 and ^ south _ inj = -0.6 , fitting a Tribble spectral ageing model results in a spectral age of _ spec = 1.1 0.1 Myr. We conclude that our data on 4C 43.15 indicates that inverse Compton losses could become comparable to or exceed synchrotron losses at higher redshifts and that inverse Compton losses could be a viable explanation for the z relation.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 43.15 as a z=2.4 high-redshift FR-II radio galaxy with a bright Ly halo, flat-spectrum core, and asymmetric gas around the northern hot spots.

quotes.quote: 4C 43.15 : High resolution LOFAR ILT maps of this HzRG have already been published, both with the HBA sweijen2022high and the LBA morabito2016lofar . It has a redshift of z=2.4 , a bright Ly halo villar2003kinematically, motoharafrared , and it is located in a dense region, but with a lower overdensity than most HzRGs wylezalek2013galaxy .

As with 4C 34.34, the radio structure of 4C 43.15 is typical for a high-luminosity FR-II edge-brightened double radio source, with hot spots at the northern and southern extremities. The spectral indices of the hot spots are normal, both with = -0.70 0.02 and that of the lobe is steeper, with = -1.10 0.03 . There is a flat--spectrum radio core, bright on the VLA X-band image and faint on the ILT HBA image that has a spectral index of = -0.40 0.07 .

gmw_00001816.name: NGP6 D1
gmw_00001816.overview: NGP6 D1 is a bright submillimeter source with no detected counterparts at shorter wavelengths, making it a SPIRE-dropout candidate [arXiv:1910.03512]. It is classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy with a high dust mass and a star formation rate of 500 solar masses per year [arXiv:1910.03512].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGP6 D1 as a bright SPIRE-dropout dusty star-forming galaxy candidate, likely at z=5.8-8.3, with ULIRG luminosity, high dust mass, and strong star formation.

quotes.quote: NGP6 D1 is a bright 850 m source (12.3 2.5 mJy) with no counterparts at shorter wavelengths (a SPIRE dropout). Interferometric observations confirm it is a single source, with no evidence for any optical or NIR emission, or nearby likely foreground lensing sources. No >3 detected lines are seen in both LMT RSR and IRAM 30m EMIR spectra of NGP6 D1 across 32 GHz of bandwidth despite reaching detection limits of 1 mJy/500 km s^ 1 , so the redshift remains unknown.

Template fitting suggests that NGP6 D1 is most likely between z = 5.8 and 8.3. SED analysis finds that NGP6 D1 is a ULIRG, with a dust mass 10^8 10^9 M_ and a SFR of 500 M_  yr^ 1 . We place upper limits on the gas mass of NGP6 D1 of M_ H2 < (1.1   3.5) 10^ 11 M_ , consistent with a gas-to-dust ratio of 100 - 1000.

gmw_00001817.name: NGC 4513
gmw_00001817.overview: NGC 4513 is a nearby, moderate-luminosity lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1912.12412] that features an outer star-forming ring [arXiv:1912.12412, arXiv:1605.01883]. The ionized gas in this ring counterrotates with respect to the stellar disk [arXiv:1912.12412, arXiv:1605.01883], which suggests the gas was accreted from an external source [arXiv:1912.12412].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4513 as a nearby S0 galaxy with a star-forming outer ring, counterrotating accreted gas, subsolar ring metallicity, and likely tidal or filamentary origin.

quotes.quote: The moderate-luminosity nearby S0 galaxy, NGC 4513, is studied here. By applying long-slit spectroscopy along the major axis of NGC 4513, we have measured gas and star kinematics, Lick indices for the main body of the galaxy, and strong emission-line flux ratios in the ring. After inspecting the gas excitation in the ring using the line ratios diagnostic diagrams and have assured that it is ionized by young stars, we have determined the gas oxygen abundance by using popular strong-line calibration methods. We have estimated star formation rate (SFR) in the outer ring by using the archival Galaxy Evolution Explorer (GALEX) ultraviolet images of the galaxy.

The ionized gas counterrotates the stars over the whole extension of NGC 4513 so being accreted from outside. The gas metallicity in the ring is slightly subsolar, [ O/H ] =-0.2 dex, matching the metallicity of the stellar component of the main galactic disc. However the stellar component of the ring is much more massive than can be explained by the current star formation level in the ring. We conclude that probably the ring of NGC 4513 is a result of tidal disruption of a massive gas-rich satellite, or it may be a consequence of a long star-formation event provoked by a gas accretion from a cosmological filament having started some 3 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC 4513 as a ring galaxy whose counter-rotating ring creates strong shear and dynamo drivers, yet the modeled magnetic response remains unexpectedly moderate.

quotes.quote: An especially interesting case is that of NGC 4513 where the ring counter-rotates with respect to the disc. Strong shear in the region between the disc and the ring is associated with unusually strong dynamo drivers for the counter-rotators. The effect of the strong drivers is found to be unexpectedly moderate. With counter-rotation in the disc, a generic model shows that a steady mixed parity magnetic configuration, unknown for classical spiral galaxies, may be excited, although we do not specifically model NGC 4513.

gmw_00001818.name: GMRT022318-044526
gmw_00001818.overview: GMRT022318-044526 is a candidate remnant radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.15 [arXiv:2207.07871]. It exhibits a double-lobed morphology spanning a physical size of 643 kpc, with edge-brightened lobes similar to FR-II radio galaxies but no detected core [arXiv:2207.07871].

sources.summary: The paper describes GMRT022318-044526 as a large double-lobed remnant radio galaxy candidate with no detected core, an identified optical host, and a young spectral age for its size.

quotes.quote: GMRT022318-044526: This source shows a clear double-lobed radio morphology with a total end-to-end projected size of 78 ^ that corresponds to the physical size of 643 kpc at the redshift z = 1.15 0.17 of the potential host. The deep 1.5 GHz JVLA observations detect no core but only two lobes that appear edge-brightened similar to the FR-II radio galaxies. Our new 400 MHz uGMRT observations revealed nearly the same structures that were seen in the previous 325 MHz observations (see Figure  fig:ImSED , top left panel ). Based on the radio contours overlaid on the HSC-SSP i band image, a galaxy J022318.1-044520.7 matching close to the centroid of the low-frequency radio emission, is identified as the potential host with an i band magnitude ( m_ i ) 24.09 0.08 and photometric redshift ( z_ phot ) 1.15 0.17.

We note that one of our sample sources GMRT022318-044526 has the radio size of 643 kpc, but it has source age ( t_ s ) of only 20.3 Myr. The low value of t_ OFF / t_ s = 0.25 suggests it to be a relatively younger remnant. Notably, GMRT022318-044526 reside at a much higher redshift ( z ) = 1.15 (see Table  tab:sample ).

gmw_00001819.name: GMRT022338-045418
gmw_00001819.overview: GMRT022338-045418 is a radio galaxy that exhibits a wing-shaped radio morphology [arXiv:2207.07871]. This source is characterized by very low surface-brightness emission and contains faint, diffuse radio components [arXiv:2207.07871].

sources.summary: The paper reports GMRT022338-045418 as a low-surface-brightness remnant with newly discovered wing-shaped radio morphology, faint diffuse components, and an uncertain but likely single-source interpretation.

quotes.quote: GMRT022338-045418: The band-3 uGMRT image of GMRT022338-045418 shows a wing-shaped radio morphology (see Figure  fig:uGMRTz ), wherein the northern (upper) component resembles a back-flow-like feature, while the southern (lower) component shows a double-peaked emission. This source has very low surface-brightness emission (36 mJy arcmin ^ 2 at 400 MHz) such that the southern component is completely undetected in the 1.5 GHz JVLA image, while 325 MHz GMRT image of relatively low resolution (10 ^ .2   7 ^ .9) shows only north-south elongation with no apparent structures (see Figure  fig:ImSED , middle left panel ). Interestingly, the northern component which resembles with a backflow tail in the 400 MHz uGMRT image clearly shows two peaks in the 1.5 GHz JVLA image (see Figure  fig:ImSED , middle left panel ). Thus, we find that both northern and southern components have two-peak emission, which is commonly seen in the winged radio galaxies classified as the Z shaped and X shaped radio sources, wherein diagonally opposite bright peaks correspond to primary lobes, and fainter peaks correspond to secondary lobes, possibly from the previous episode of AGN jet activity Lal19,Bera22 .

We note that the discovery of wing-shaped radio morphology in GMRT022338-045418 is credited to the sensitivity and resolution of band-3 uGMRT observations that are adequate to detect faint diffuse radio emissions. The total flux density at 400 MHz of this source is only 3.1 0.5 mJy, while northern and southern components have a flux density of 1.62 0.3 mJy and 1.48 0.3 mJy, respectively. The lack of its complete detection in all but band-3 observations poses a challenge in characterizing this source.

gmw_00001820.name: GMRT022737-052139
gmw_00001820.overview: GMRT022737-052139 is a remnant radio galaxy with a total size of 190 kpc, making it the smallest source in its sample [arXiv:2207.07871]. It exhibits an east-west double-lobe-like radio structure, low surface brightness, and strong spectral curvature [arXiv:2207.07871].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GMRT022737-052139 as the smallest source in the remnant sample, with east-west double-lobe-like radio structure, a potential optical host, low surface brightness, and strong spectral curvature.

quotes.quote: GMRT022737-052139: The radio morphology of this source shows east-west elongation. The high resolution 1.5 GHz JVLA image indicates the double-lobe-like structure (see Figure  fig:ImSED , left panel ). The two lobes are not well resolved due to the small angular size (28 ^ ) of this source.

Our new uGMRT observations show emission consistent with the 325 MHz GMRT observations. With radio contours overlaid on the i band optical image we identify J022737-052139 as a potential host with m_ i = 24.17 0.03 with z_ phot = 0.62 0.13. With a total size of 190 kpc this source is the smallest in our sample. The strong spectral curvature and low surface brightness of 56 mJy arcsec ^ 2 at 400 MHz suggest it to be a remnant source.

gmw_00001821.name: GMRT022802-041417
gmw_00001821.overview: GMRT022802-041417 is a small remnant radio source hosted by a bright elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2207.07871]. High-frequency observations reveal an east-west elongation with two tentative peaks, suggesting a double-lobed structure [arXiv:2207.07871].

sources.summary: The paper describes GMRT022802-041417 as a small remnant radio source hosted by a bright elliptical galaxy, with tentative double-lobed structure and the largest remnant-phase fraction in the sample.

quotes.quote: GMRT022802-041417: This source is hosted in a nearby bright ( m_ i = 19.45 0.01) elliptical galaxy with a photometric redshift of 0.44 0.05. The angular size (38 ^ ) of this source corresponds to the total projected linear size of 216 kpc. Due to its relatively small angular size, 325 MHz and 400 MHz observations do not reveal clear structures. However, 1.5 GHz JVLA image showing east-west elongation and two tentative peaks infer it to be a double-lobed source (see Figure  fig:ImSED , left panel ).

One of the noteworthy examples in our sample is GMRT022802-041417, which has spent nearly 63 per cent of its total life in the remnant phase. The active phase duration of this source is only 15.4 Myr, while it has spent nearly 26.0 Myr in the remnant phase. Thus, we emphasize that despite the limited frequency coverage (150 MHz 1.5 GHz) our observations are capable of detecting remnants in the different phases.

gmw_00001822.name: IC 630
gmw_00001822.overview: IC 630 is a nearby early-type radio galaxy [arXiv:1709.03367]. Its circumnuclear gas excitation is primarily driven by star formation, featuring a young starburst age of approximately 6 Myr and a clumpy, spiral-like structure [arXiv:1709.03367].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 630 as a nearby early-type radio galaxy whose circumnuclear gas, starburst age, outflow, and clumpy structure match the simulated starburst phase.

quotes.quote: IC 630 is a nearby early-type galaxy, classified as a radio galaxy Brown_11 . With the help of VLT-SINFONI and Gemini North-NIFS adaptive optics observations, Durre_17 show that the excitation of the circumnuclear gas within a few 100 pc of the centre can mostly be explained by star formation. The measured SFR (1-2 M _ yr ^ 1 ), SNR ( 4 10^ 3 yr ^ 1 ), the gas outflow rate (0.18 M _ yr ^ 1 ), total gas masses (a few times 10^7 M _ ) as well as the line of sight velocity channel maps of the gas all are in line (within a factor of a few) with our simulated system during the peak of the starbursting phase, seen face-on. Especially the inferred young starburst age of roughly 6 Myr (derived from the equivalent width of the Brackett- line) and the observed cylindrical rather than conical shape of the outflow points into a starburst-, rather than AGN-driven event. Also the clumpy structure in the observed NIR J, H and K band maps as well as the hint of a spiral structure is in good agreement with the structural evolution found from our violent disc instability scenario.

gmw_00001823.name: GMASS 0953
gmw_00001823.overview: GMASS 0953 is a compact star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2303.16227, arXiv:1802.06083] hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.02891, arXiv:1802.06083] that drives a galactic wind [arXiv:2004.02891]. The galaxy features a compact, rapidly rotating molecular gas disk [arXiv:2303.16227, arXiv:1802.06083] and is expected to evolve into a quiescent galaxy [arXiv:2303.16227, arXiv:1802.06083].

sources.summary: ID15 is a compact star-forming galaxy expected to become quiescent; ALMA CO shows a compact rotating disk, but velocity dispersion and possible warp or non-circular motions remain poorly constrained.

quotes.quote: ID15 ID15 is known as the prototypical example of a compact star-forming galaxy that is rapidly consuming its gas reservoir and is expected to evolve into a quiescent galaxy Popping_2017 . The kinematics of the CO(6-5) emission line is studied in Talia_2018 that identify the presence of a rotating disk with a velocity V = 320^ +92 _ 53 km s ^ 1 . Further, Loiacono_2019 analyzed the H and [ OIII ] kinematics, finding evidence of rotation motions, with V 200 ^ +17 _ 20 km s ^ 1 for an inclination of 75 . Here, we assume an inclination of 60 derived from modeling the HST data. The CO(3-2) data clearly show the presence of a compact rotating disk.

However, similarly to previous studies Talia_2018, Loiacono_2019 , the velocity dispersion values of ID15 are poorly constrained, having uncertainties of 50-60 at all radii because of the low angular resolution of ALMA data. We note that ID15 has distorted iso-velocity contours (Fig. fig:pv4 ) and residuals in the channel maps that may indicate the presence of a warp or non-circular motions. However, higher angular resolution data are needed to robustly analyze them and constrain the driving mechanisms. htbp ID16 - 17 ID16 and 17 are two galaxies, separated by 3 and connected by a bridge of gas and dust Fu_2013 .

sources.summary: The paper presents K20-ID5 as a compact star-forming AGN host whose SINFONI-AO data show nuclear outflow-dominated emission, a rotating extended disk, and potential rapid depletion of molecular gas.

quotes.quote: K20-ID5 has a stellar mass of log(M _* /M _ ) = 11.2 and a UV + IR SFR of 335 M _ yr ^ 1 Wuyts11b,Wuyts11 , placing it on the upper envelope of the main sequence (purple star in Figure fig:main_sequence ) Talia18 and Scholtz20 reported similar values for the stellar mass and SFR (consistent within 0.2 dex), based on SED modelling covering rest frame wavelengths of 0.1-1000 m . . The galaxy is classified as an AGN host based on the X-ray luminosity, radio luminosity, mid-infrared colors and optical line ratios e.g. vanDokkum05, Genzel14, Newman14 . The black hole accretion is driving a galactic wind which has been detected as a broad blueshifted component in the rest-frame optical emission lines Genzel14, NMFS14, Loiacono19, Scholtz20 , the rest-frame UV absorption lines Cimatti13 , and tentatively in the CO(6-5) emission line Talia18 .

K20-ID5 has a luminous compact core and a fainter, regularly rotating extended disk. Our SINFONI-AO data reveal strong deviations from regular disk kinematics in the central 0.4'' (3.3 kpc), spatially coincident with elevated line widths and large \ ratios. We conclude that the majority of the line emission in the nuclear region traces the AGN-driven outflow, with a minor contribution from star formation. The outflow can be traced well beyond the effective radius of the galaxy, to a distance of 5 kpc, at an approximately constant velocity of 1400 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses GS30274 as a z=2.225 compact star-forming galaxy with starburst-like ISM, low gas fraction, high efficiency, and merger-triggered core growth.

quotes.quote: In particular, 2017A&A...602A..11P employed it to characterize the ISM properties of a cSFGs at z = 2.225 located within the scatter of the MS (namely GS30274). They found that the galaxy has SB-like ISM properties, low gas fraction, and high efficiency compared to normal SFGs. The authors interpreted that a previous merger event triggered a central starburst that is quickly building up a dense core of stars responsible for the compact distribution of stellar light.

We performed PDR modeling for this galaxy and also for GS30274 in 2017A&A...602A..11P for consistency in the methodology and to avoid systematics in the comparison. Besides, we calculated GS30274 _ QGs and checked that it is a cSFGs according to 2017ApJ...840...47B criterion. Note that the structural parameters of GS30274 are poorly constrained, as also mentioned in 2017A&A...602A..11P , and do not meet the same quality criteria applied to our sample.

sources.summary: The paper models CDFS-695 as an active high-redshift galaxy, deriving cloud distances for AGN and starburst regions and linking its line ratios to shocks across the galaxy.

quotes.quote: CDFS-695 . We cross-checked the results obtained by modelling the line spectrum (Table 2, col.3 and 4) calculating the distance of the emitting cloud from the AGN in the NLR. The flux observed at Earth (2.9 10 ^ 17 ) after reddening correction (Osterbrock 1974, eq 7.6, fig. 7.1) results _ obs = 6.10 ^ 16 . Combining it with the flux calculated at the cloud ( _ mod ) : _ obs d ^2 = _ mod ^2 (where d is the distance to Earth) and adopting a filling factor 1 throughout the NLR, results 1.8 kpc, in rough agreement with van Dokkum et al who suggested the presence of shocks on the basis of the line ratios extending to 10 kpc from the nucleus. The distance of the emitting clouds from the SB region, calculated in the same way, results < 1.9 kpc, adopting a filling factor < 1. The SB region does not cover the entire galaxy.

sources.summary: XID 435S has spectral fits consistent with Compton-thick absorption, lacks a clear Fe K line, and its absorption-corrected X-ray luminosity agrees with MIR expectations.

quotes.quote: XID 435S: Fitting this source spectrum with model 2 (see Sect. xray ) results in a column density of =1.1_ 0.7 ^ +1.2 10^ 25  cm ^ 2 (with =1.89^ +0.7 _ 0.6 ), which is consistent with heavily CT material. Using the PLCABS and Torus models, we obtained smaller \ values of (3-7) 10^ 24  cm ^ 2 ( 1.9 ), but still consistent with the source being CT (Table tab3 ). However the spectrum does not show a clear signature of a strong Fe K line. We note that, although these models are too complex for the limited counting statistics to provide tight constraints on the spectral parameters, the intrinsic 2-10  keV X-ray luminosity corrected for the CT column densities derived from the spectral fits is in good agreement with the expectations from the MIR 6  m AGN luminosity, according to the L_ X L_ 6 m relation. XID 435S was also classified as a CT AGN also by brightman2012 .

sources.summary: The paper finds GMASS 0953 to be a compact z=2.226 star-forming AGN host with dense gas, short depletion time, and rotating molecular gas likely evolving toward a compact quiescent galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA observations at 107.291 GHz (band 3) and 214.532 GHz (band 6) of GMASS 0953, a star-forming galaxy at z =2.226 hosting an obscured AGN that has been proposed as a progenitor of compact quiescent galaxies (QG). We measure for the first time the size of the dust and molecular gas emission of GMASS 0953 that we find to be extremely compact ( 1 kpc). This result, coupled with a very high ISM density ( n 10^ 5.5 cm ^ 3 ), a low gas mass fraction ( 0.2) and a short gas depletion timescale ( 150 Myr) imply that GMASS 0953 is experiencing an episode of intense star-formation in its central region that will rapidly exhaust its gas reservoirs, likely aided by AGN-induced feedback, confirming its fate as a compact QG. Kinematic analysis of the CO(6-5) line shows evidence of rapidly-rotating gas ( V_ rot =320 ^ +92 _ 53 km s ^ 1 ), as observed also in a handful of similar sources at the same redshift. On-going quenching mechanisms could either destroy the rotation or leave it intact leading the galaxy to evolve into a rotating QG.

gmw_00001824.name: G4Jy 1748 G4Jy 1749
gmw_00001824.overview: G4Jy 1748 G4Jy 1749 is a lobe-dominated quasar [arXiv:2308.10071] and Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy [arXiv:2007.10474, arXiv:2308.11370] surrounded by a hot X-ray halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0412547]. The system features a one-sided nuclear jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0412547] that interacts with a nearby gas cloud [arXiv:1205.4726, arXiv:0912.3001], inflating distinct X-ray cavities [arXiv:1205.4726, arXiv:2308.10071].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS B2152-699 as a nearby radio galaxy with jet-cloud interaction, X-ray cavities, shocked lobes, and energetic feedback dominated by shocked gas.

quotes.quote: PKS B2152-699, which has radio power characteristic of sources that dominate radio feedback, is exceptional in showing a wide range of features associated with radio-galaxy/gas interactions. We present new deep radio (ATCA), X-ray ( ) and ground-based optical observations, and test the energetics of the feedback model. We report the first high-resolution observations of the radio jet, finding that the inner jet extends 8.5 kpc ( 10^ viewing angle) in the direction of an optical emission-line High Ionization Cloud (HIC) before taking a zig-zag path to a position offset from the HIC. Jet synchrotron radiation to X-ray energies is seen. The HIC is associated with cool, kT 0.3 keV, X-ray gas of anomalously low metallicity.

On larger scales, the radio galaxy displays all three X-ray features that together confirm supersonic expansion of the lobes into the external medium: gas cavities, inverse-Compton emission showing excess internal lobe pressure, and high-contrast arms of temperature above the kT 1  keV ambient medium. The well-formed southern lobe on the counterjet side is expanding with a Mach number between 2.2 and 3. The lobe energy appears to be more gently dissipated in the north.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2153 69 as an FR II source with an elongated jet and the sample's fastest apparent motions, increasing with core distance along the parsec-scale jet.

quotes.quote: 2152 699 This FR II source shows a relatively elongated jet, extending 50 mas from the core to the north-east. The extended jet is best seen in the first TANAMI epoch (see Fig. fig:wallpaper ), due to better (u,v) coverage on short spacings. Previous VLBI observations by showed a one-sided jet extending for 20 mas to the north-east. Space-VLBI images with VSOP show a narrow, smooth jet extending in the same direction for 6 mas 2002ApJS..141..311T .

2152 699 This source shows the fastest apparent motions in this sample, up to _ app = 3.6 0.8 for component J4. Interestingly, there is a clear trend of increasing apparent component speed with increasing core distance. This can be seen in Fig. 2152_accel , where this effect has been quantified by means of a simple linear fit, which yields _ app = 0.19d+0.34 (where d is the angular core separation). This behavior is similar to the one revealed by TANAMI data for the pc-scale jet of Centaurus A 2014A&A...569A.115M , although both the apparent speed and the distance from the core have much larger values in PKS 2153 69.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra and LBA observations of PKS 2153--69, describing its hot X-ray halo, radio lobes, hot spots, active nucleus, and jet/cloud interaction.

quotes.quote: We report Chandra X-ray Observatory and 1.4 GHz Australian Long Baseline Array (LBA) observations of the radio galaxy PKS 2153--69 and its environment. The Chandra image reveals a roughly spherical halo of hot gas extending out to 30 around PKS 2153--69. Two depressions in the surface brightness of the X-ray halo correspond to the large scale radio lobes, and interpreting these as cavities inflated with radio plasma we infer a jet power of 4 10^ 42 . Both radio lobes contain hot spots that are detected by Chandra.

The radio to X-ray spectra of the hot spots are consistent with a simple power law emission model. The nucleus has an X-ray spectrum typical of a type 1 active galactic nucleus, and the LBA observation shows a one-sided nuclear jet on 0 1 scales. Approximately 10 northeast of the nucleus, X-ray emission is associated with an extra-nuclear cloud. The X-ray emission from the cloud can be divided into two regions, an unresolved western component associated with a knot of radio emission (in a low resolution map), and a spatially extended eastern component aligned with the pc-scale jet and associated with highly ionized optical line-emitting clouds.

sources.summary: The paper describes G4Jy 1748 as a lobe-dominated QSO with radio-galaxy gas interactions, FR II lobes, X-ray cavities, hotspots, and high-energy detections.

quotes.quote: G4Jy 1748 G4Jy 1749: the former one is a lobe dominated QSO a.k.a. PKS 2152-69 jones92,morganti93 with a z =0.0281 marenbach82,tadhunter93 and showing a wide range of features associated with radio-galaxy/gas interactions typical of sources where radio mode feedback processes are occurring worrall12 . High resolution radio observations reveal a radio component at 10 arcsec in the north-eastern direction from the core, close to an optical highly ionized cloud. At larger scale, G4Jy 1748 shows a FR II morphology with the northern lobe having a ``relaxed'' structure, while the southern lobe shows an edge-brightened, arc-like structure fosbury98 . The X-ray surface brightness has two depressions spatially associated with the radio lobes thus suggesting the presence of X-ray cavities inflated with radio plasma young05 . Both radio lobes have their hotspot detected in the X-rays see also ly05,massaro11a .

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra thermal X-ray emission around PKS 2152-699's core and hotspots, with optical/radio offsets interpreted as a jet-cloud interaction in the radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: A Chandra ACIS-S observation of PKS 2152-699 reveals thermal emission from a diffuse region around the core and a hotspot located 10 northeast from the core.
