3CR 234 is characterized by a very high degree of polarization ( 17 after starlight subtraction) of both the optical continuum and the broad H line antonucci84 , which can be explained only with polarization induced by scattering; also the K band continuum is polarized, at the lower level of 4.6 0.4 ( sitko91 ; see also young98 ). The flux and polarization properties at optical and NIR wavelengths have been modeled by young98 , who concluded that the observed light comprises a scattered component and a dichroic view through the torus which becomes important only at the longer wavelengths. This is qualitatively consistent with our results, although the nuclear extinction they estimated ( 60 mag) is larger than ours (22 2 mag).

sources.summary: The atlas presents 3C 234 as a broad-line FR II radio galaxy with a quasar-like nucleus, optical and infrared tidal-arm features, nearby western sources, and strong extended emission lines.

quotes.quote: 3C 234; z=0.185 3C 234 exhibits a bright quasar-like nucleus in the NICMOS image, and is known to be a broad-line radio galaxy mccarthy+95 with classical double FR II radio source. In the optical dekoff+96,mclure+99 there are features emanating from the nucleus in the east and west direction. The western feature, described as a tidal arm by McLure et al. is detectable on the new NICMOS image.

There are two additional sources to the west in the same direction, the furthest of which is also detectable in F555W, and F702W. Both are detectable in F675W. This galaxy is known to have strong extended emission lines which likely explain these features.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes 3C 234, finding that added thermal components produce acceptable fits while a power-law jet component dominates its soft X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: 3C 234 is a NLRG that famously shows polarized broad emission lines in the optical (Antonucci 1982; Antonucci Barvainis 1990); thus it is one of only a few objects to show unambiguous optical evidence for the presence of the `hidden quasar' predicted by unified models. The long XMM-Newton observation of 3C 234 has been analysed by Piconcelli \ (2008), who argue that there are strong soft emission lines in the spectrum and therefore that the soft excess in this object cannot be jet-related (see Section jetrelated ). We extracted spectra from the data in the standard way, fitting as usual in the well-calibrated 0.3-8.0 keV band, and confirm that our standard model (one power law with Galactic absorption only, one power law with Galactic and intrinsic absorption, and a Gaussian representing the Fe K feature) does not give an acceptable fit to the data: there are significant residuals at soft energies (Fig. \ 234-spectrum ).

However, we find an acceptable fit ( ^2 = 362 for 320 degrees of freedom) if we add a single MEKAL model to the data, and a good fit ( ^2 = 332 for 318 degrees of freedom) with two MEKAL components, having kT = 0.61 0.07 and 0.15 0.01 keV (with fixed abundances of 0.35 solar). The addition of the MEKAL models not only removes the soft residuals but also reduces those at the crossover between the two power laws at 2 keV. Crucially, in these models, the power-law component dominates the soft X-ray emission from 3C 234, providing 60 per cent of the X-ray flux between 0.5 and 2.0 keV in the two-MEKAL model, while there is no evidence for soft residuals in the fit (Fig. \ 234-spectrum ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 234 as an FR II source whose polarized spectrum resembles a hidden QSO; its HST compact core is interpreted as a compact scattering region.

quotes.quote: Another puzzling source is 3C 234. Spectropolarimetric studies of this source showed that its polarized spectrum closely matches that of a QSO, with prominent featureless continuum and broad lines (Antonucci , Tran et al. , Hurt et al. ). This has been interpreted as the result of scattering of light from a hidden QSO nucleus into our line of sight. Nonetheless, its representative point lies on the correlation (within 2 ).

The CCC flux in the HST image of 3C 234 is 1.1 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 A ^ 1 . . We can then argue that the nuclear source in 3C 234 is a compact scattering region (which appears as unresolved also in high resolution polarimetric images obtained with the Keck telescope, Cohen ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 234 as an FR II high-excitation radio galaxy with an elongated emission-line region, tidal arm, detected SW hotspot, and thermal X-ray spectrum indicating a hidden quasar.

quotes.quote: 3C 234 is an FR II radio galaxy optically classified as HEG. Its optical images tremblay09 show an elongated emission line region that includes a tidal arm carleton84 that appears to be roughly parallel with that of the radio jet axis on large scales see also for more details privon08 . The SW hotspot is clearly detected in the X-ray band. The X-ray spectral analysis of the nuclear emission in 3C 234 has been presented by Piconcelli et al. (2008). They found a thermal dominated X-ray spectrum, rich of emission lines, confirming the presence of a hidden quasar in this source and claiming that the origin of the X-ray emission in radio-loud AGNs with high-excitation optical lines does not always arise from jet non-thermal radiation.

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 234.0 has complicated X-ray fitting below 1 keV and is one of three jetted high-obscuration objects with less reliable total column estimates.

quotes.quote: However, in the case of 3C 234.0, fitting complications arose, in particular at energies <1 keV, see Figure fig0 . The residuals showed a significant deficit that it was not possible to eliminate introducing an intrinsic absorption. Furthermore, these residuals showed a peculiar trend made by several bumps.

These objects include 4C +29.30 and 3C 234.0, both radio sources, the latter involving a complicated fitting process (refer to Section sec2.2 ), and 2MASX J 04234080+0408017 with small radio jets . For radio-emitting objects, significant obscuration is not contradictory to the UM.

sources.summary: 3C 234 has an unusually flat far-IR-to-UV SED, the highest XUV in the sample, ambiguous YSP or power-law fits, and MIR/FIR dust.

quotes.quote: 3C 234. An unusual SED, almost flat from the far-IR to the ultraviolet (which even increases toward the far-UV). This source has the highest XUV (4.18) in our sample. Excellent fits are allowed either by a YSP or by a power-law component. (see Sect. discussion for a more detailed discussion). MIR and FIR data are available, which can be fitted by a mix of dust with temperatures of 100 K ( M_d = 4.0 10^5 M_ ), 400 K ( M_d = 8.0 10^2 M_ ), and 1000 K ( M_d = 1.5 M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 234 as a HEG with high [O III]/H ratios west of the nucleus, disturbed ionized gas, and [O III] extending farther than H.

quotes.quote: Another interesting case is 3C 234, where the highest line ratio values (6-8) are located at 1-2 (3-6 kpc) to the west from the nuclear region.

The extended disturbed patches of ionized gas observed in 3C 33, 3C 234 and 3C 285 seem to be associated with regions of intense star formation, induced by jet shocks or by a tidal merger/interaction, as suggested by the HST images madrid06,floyd10,baldi08 . These sources are all classified as HEGs.

However, for at least 5 ( 26 ) galaxies in our sample, all HEGs (3C 33, 3C 180, 3C 227, 3C 234, 3C 285), [ O III ] extended emission is clearly observed at larger scales (on kpc scale) than that of H .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C234 as aligned with the radio source, with core extension along the nebular major axis, perpendicular filaments, and broad polarized lines resembling narrow-line radio galaxies.

quotes.quote: 3C234 (Figs. 3C234-montage 3C234-overlay ) is aligned with the radio source and the core shows slight extension along the major axis of the nebula. There are also filaments extending almost perpendicular to the radio axis. The broad lines in 3C234 are highly polarized so it is not surprising the properties of this source are similar to that of NLRGs Antonucci84 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C234 as a near-infrared polarized radio galaxy, confirming previous polarimetry and relating its polarized flux to optical and near-infrared measurements.

quotes.quote: In our sample, 3C234 and 3C265 are in this category. Our near-IR polarimetry result for 3C234 confirms that of SZ91 with a higher S/N (Table tab-pol ), and is also consistent with the near-IR spectropolarimetric results of Yo98 . The near-IR polarized flux of 3C234 in relation to other wavelengths has been discussed in detail by Yo98 (see also Ki01 , who quote the polarized flux slope of F_ ^ 1.6 over the optical to near-IR).

gmw_00001870.name: Hercules
gmw_00001870.overview: Hercules is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy located approximately 140 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:astro-ph/0608448, arXiv:1110.4545]. It is highly elongated [arXiv:0708.2612, arXiv:0805.2945], hosting an ancient stellar population older than 10 billion years that shows evidence of early, rapid chemical enrichment [arXiv:1010.5683, arXiv:1901.05465].

sources.summary: The paper studies Hercules RGB stars, finding the dSph more metal-poor than earlier photometry, with at least a 1 dex metallicity spread, old red giants, and a Ca/Fe trend implying early supernova enrichment.

quotes.quote: We find that the red-giant branch stars of the Hercules dSph galaxy are more metal-poor than estimated in our previous study that was based on photometry alone. From this, we derive a new metallicity calibration for the Str omgren photometry. Additionally, we find an abundance trend such that [ Ca/Fe ] is higher for more metal-poor stars, and lower for more metal-rich stars, with a spread of about 0.8 dex. The [ Ca/Fe ] trend suggests an early rapid chemical enrichment through supernovae of type II, followed by a phase of slow star formation dominated by enrichment through supernovae of type Ia. A comparison with isochrones indicates that the red giants in Hercules are older than 10 Gyr.

We have studied confirmed RGB stars in the ultra-faint Hercules dSph galaxy with FLAMES high-resolution spectroscopy. Abundances were determined by solving the radiative transfer calculations using the codes Eqwi/Bsyn in marcs model atmospheres. We find that the RGB stars of the Hercules dSph galaxy included in this study are more metal-poor than estimated in 2009A&A...506.1147A , however in good agreement with 2008ApJ...685L..43K , with a metallicity spread of at least 1 dex.

sources.summary: The paper describes Hercules as an elongated dwarf galaxy with BHB members and likely unbound stars, arguing that its structure indicates ongoing tidal disruption.

quotes.quote: July 2012 firstpage - lastpage 2012 We present VLT/FORS2 spectroscopy of candidate blue horizontal branch (BHB) stars in the vicinity of the Hercules ultrafaint dwarf galaxy. We identify eight convincing Hercules BHB members, and a further five stars with similar systemic velocities to that of Hercules, but 0.5 kpc from the centre of the galaxy along its major axis. It is likely that these stars once belonged to Hercules, but have been tidally stripped and are now unbound. We emphasise the usefulness of looking for any gradient in the systemic velocity of this stretched system, which would further support our interpretation of the origin of its elongated and distended morphology.

Thus, it is unlikely that these distant stars are drawn from the same velocity distribution as the central field. Note that if the 2 stars with significant velocity differences are included in this test, then the likelihood is less than the control sample likelihood in all trials. We conclude that if these stars are members of Hercules then it is very unlikely that they are bound. The presence of unbound members in addition to the elongated structure of Hercules strongly suggests that this dwarf is being tidally disrupted.

sources.summary: The paper searches for stripped Hercules members, estimates a 40 percent probability of tidal stripping, and uses tidal-evolution tracks to compare possible progenitors, with Leo II the closest match.

quotes.quote: We also search for spectroscopic members stripped from Hercules in the possible extratidal stellar overdensities surrounding the dwarf. For both galaxies, we calculate proper motions using Gaia DR2 astrometry, and use their full 6D phase space information to evaluate the probability that their orbits approach sufficiently close to the Milky Way to experience tidal stripping. Given the available kinematic data, we find a probability of 40 that Hercules has suffered tidal stripping.

The Hercules ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) has long been speculated to be a tidally disrupted object. Our orbital calculations show that it is possible for Hercules to have experienced tidal interactions with the Milky Way. We therefore perform the same tidal evolution track investigation for Hercules that we did for Crater II, and present our results in Figure fig:hercevo . First, we attempt to identify analogs to the progenitor of Hercules among the currently-known dwarf galaxies. For both the cuspy and cored dark matter profile cases, we find that Leo II is the closest match to a possible progenitor of Hercules.

sources.summary: The paper describes Hercules as an ultra-faint dwarf at about 140 kpc that may be dissolving into a stellar stream, using its radial-velocity gradient to infer tangential motion and a recent close Milky Way passage.

quotes.quote: Here, I present the results of two studies that use radial velocity gradients to determine the system's orbit: for Hercules, one of the `ultra-faint' dwarf galaxies exhibiting a large ellipticity and located at a distance of 140 kpc, showing that it may in fact be a stellar stream, and for a string of high-velocity H clouds belonging to the GCN complex, indicating its likelihood for being a gaseous stream at a distance of approximately 20 kpc.

Using the radial velocity measurements of Hercules member stars as observed by , we employ a maximum-likelihood approach in order to determine the probability distribution function of the radial velocity gradient (and from this, that of the tangential velocity) and thereby establish whether Hercules could have had a recent, close perigalactic passage. We assume a distance to Hercules of 138 7 , use a Galactic potential composed of a Miyamoto-Nagai disk and bulge as defined by and a Navarro-Frenk-White dark-matter halo with parameters as constrained by , and use equation (2) of to deduce the tangential (or transverse) velocity from the radial-velocity gradient. Note that using this equation gives two possible solutions, but one of these corresponds to sets of tangential velocities so large that the pericentre would be very large and the orbit of Hercules would be unbound.

Given the sky position, radial velocity, and the distance to and direction of motion of the assumed Hercules stream, determining the likelihood distribution of the tangential velocity is equivalent to deducing the likelihood distribution of the orbit. By integrating the orbit backward and forward in time, we obtain the likelihood distribution for the perigalactic distance (right-most panel of Figure fig:MLpdfs ), yielding tight constraints on the small pericentric distance of 6^ +9 _ 2 . The best model puts this flyby at 0.6 ago.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Hercules dwarf spheroidal, finding very low heavy-element abundances and chemical patterns consistent with enrichment by only a few massive explosive events.

quotes.quote: Small-scale hero: massive-star enrichment in the Hercules dwarf spheroidal 98.52.Wz, 98.62.Bj Stars: abundances --- stars: Population II --- nuclear reactions, nucleosynthesis, abundances --- galaxies: evolution --- galaxies: dwarf --- galaxies: individual: Hercules Andreas Koch address= Landessternwarte, Zentrum f ur Astronomie der Universit at Heidelberg, K onigstuhl 12, 69117 Heidelberg, Germany Francesca Matteucci address= Dipartimento di Astronomia, Universit a di Trieste, via G. Here we present evidence for remarkably low heavy element abundances in the metal poor Hercules UFD. Combined with other peculiar abundance patterns this indicates that Hercules was likely only influenced by very few, massive explosive events -- thus bearing the traces of an early, localized chemical enrichment with only very little other contributions from other sources at later times. At M_V=-6.6 mag, Hercules (Her) is one of the brighter UFDs.

1 also shows chemical evolution models specific to Her . Ca) in two of the more metal rich stars in Her (at [ Fe/H ] 2 dex). As a result, Ba in Her is very low, at [ Ba/H ] <-3 dex (Koch, Ad en, Feltzing 2012, in prep.).

sources.summary: The paper finds Hercules has very low barium over a broad metallicity range, contrasting with its iron and calcium dispersions and implying enrichment by massive stars with inefficient star formation.

quotes.quote: Tiepolo 11, 34143 Trieste, Italy We present an assessment of the barium abundance ratios for red giant member stars in the faint Hercules dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy. Our results are drawn from intermediate-resolution FLAMES/GIRAFFE spectra around the Ba II 6141.71 absorption line at low signal-to-noise ratios. For three brighter stars we were able to gain estimates from direct equivalent-width measurements, while for the remaining eight stars only upper limits could be obtained. These results are investigated in a statistical manner and indicate very low Ba abundances of log (Ba) 0.7 dex (3 ).

This contrasts with the high dispersions in iron and calcium (in excess of 1 dex) in this galaxy. While the latter spreads are typical of the very low luminosity, dark-matter dominated dSphs, a high level of depletion in heavy elements suggests that chemical enrichment in Hercules was governed by very massive stars, coupled with a very low star formation efficiency. While very low abundances of some heavy elements are also found in individual stars of other dwarf galaxies, this is the first time that a very low Ba abundance is found within an entire dSph over a broad metallicity range.

sources.summary: The paper models Hercules as an ultra-faint dwarf with early star formation, strong elongation, limited abundance data, and chemical evolution constrained by dark-halo assumptions.

quotes.quote: Hercules UfD The Hercules UfD was discovered by belokurov2007 from the analysis of Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) data. The line of sight toward Hercules is heavily contaminated by Galactic foreground stars since it resides at a lower Galactic latitude than the other UfDs dejong2008 . Furthermore, the mean radial velocity of Hercules stars is very similar to the mean radial velocity of thick disk stars. Hercules appears clearly highly elongated, without any evidence of internal rotation ( _ v 3.72 km s ^ 1 , from aden2009b ).

Chemical evolution of the Hercules UfD The chemical evolution models we ran for the Hercules UfD adopt the same theoretical prescriptions - and so the same numerical code - as the ones adopted for the dSph galaxies. The mass of the DM halo M_ DM , the effective radius of the luminous (baryonic) component of the galaxy, as well as the SFH of the galaxy have been taken from the observations and kept fixed for all the models. The main difference resides in the fact that UfDs suffered an even slower SFR than dSphs (see also salvadori2009 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds Hercules dSph highly elongated, considers rotation and tidal distortion, and argues the favored tidal explanation would require an unusually extreme orbit around the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: We have found Hercules to be highly elongated. The ellipticity of this system ( e = 0.65 ) gives a major-to-minor axis ratio of 3:1 , which is significantly greater than the 2:1 values measured for other dSphs (excluding the highly disrupted Sagittarius system). Three scenarios suggest themselves as explanations for this unusual structure.

Thus, as a second secnario, Hercules may be rotating, however this is inconsistent with our understanding of dSphs, and is not seen in a recent kinematic survey of Hercules . The third scenario is that Hercules has been tidally distorted by the gravitational field of the Milky Way, an effect seen in other systems. However, Hercules is relatively distant ( 132 12 kpc; ), hence tidal distortion would require this system to be on an extreme orbit.

sources.summary: Hercules is one of the more distant HST ultra-faint dwarfs and the comparison frame for composite CMDs; its age is close to M92, and its IMF is flatter than Galactic-field values.

quotes.quote: For the distant ( > 130 kpc) satellites in the sample (Hercules, Leo IV, and Canes Venatici II), we surveyed a narrow pencil beam (1 or 2 tiles in each camera) more deeply (10--16 orbits, faint limit of V 29 mag). The varying depth and area of each survey was intended to provide a sample of a few hundred stars in the vicinity of the MSTO with a photometric precision of 0.01 mag, thus providing tight constraints on age when coupled with independent knowledge of the metallicity distribution from Keck.

To empirically demonstrate the extent of synchronization in the SFHs of the UFDs, we show in Figure 2 the composite CMD from our entire sample, with each UFD shifted to the frame (distance and reddening) of Hercules. The fact that each UFD CMD is similar to that of Hercules indicates that their SFHs are largely synchronized, with mean ages agreeing to 1 Gyr; better constraints on the degree of synchronization will come from refinements to the quantitative SFH fitting, which we are pursuing. Using synthetic CMD analysis, brown12 found that the mean age of Hercules was only 0.1 Gyr younger than that of M92.

We are also investigating the IMF of each galaxy; our analysis of the first two galaxies observed in our survey (Hercules and Leo IV) found that their IMFs are flatter than those in the Galactic field and luminous dwarf satellites geha13 .

sources.summary: The paper focuses on the Hercules ultra-faint dwarf, finding RR Lyrae stars beyond its tidal radius and interpreting them as evidence for substantial tidal stripping and disruption.

quotes.quote: We investigate the hypothesized tidal disruption of the Hercules ultra-faint dwarf galaxy (UFD). Previous tidal disruption studies of the Hercules UFD have been hindered by the high degree of foreground contamination in the direction of the dwarf. We bypass this issue by using RR Lyrae stars, which are standard candles with a very low field-volume density at the distance of Hercules.

Combining color, magnitude, and light-curve information, we identify three new RR Lyrae stars associated with Hercules. All three of these new RR Lyrae stars lie outside its published tidal radius. When considered with the nine RR Lyrae stars already known within the tidal radius, these results suggest that a substantial fraction of Hercules' stellar content has been stripped.

sources.summary: The review discusses Hercules as an ultra-faint dwarf with old stellar populations, HST-based IMF measurements, and possible tidal-stripping indicators including high elongation.

quotes.quote: Star Formation Histories The first analysis of deep HST imaging of UFDs was carried out by brown12 , studying Hercules, Leo IV, and UMa I. They concluded that the three galaxies have similar ages, and are each as old or older than the prototypical ancient globular cluster M92. brown14 expanded the sample to six UFDs, adding Boo I, Canes Venatici II (CVn II), and Coma Berenices (Com Ber) to the previous three. By incorporating improved spectroscopic MDFs and updated isochrones matched to observed dwarf galaxy chemical abundance patterns, brown14 determined that all of the galaxies except UMa I had formed more than 75 of their stars by z 10 . Using a star formation model consisting of two bursts, the best fit for UMa I has approximately half of its stars forming at z 3 . A large majority of the stars in all six dwarfs had formed by the end of reionization at z 6 , consistent with the idea that gas heating by reionization ended star formation in such objects e.g., bullock00,somerville02,benson02 . Note, however, that quenching by reionization does not necessarily mean that star formation ends precisely at the redshift of reionization, since sufficiently high-density molecular gas can survive somewhat beyond reionization even in low-mass halos e.g., onorbe15 . Star formation histories have also been derived for Hercules, Leo IV, and CVn II by weisz14a from shallower WFPC2 data. weisz14a found that >90 of the stars in Hercules and Leo IV are older than 11 Gyr, consistent with the brown14 results.

geha13 measured the IMF in two ultra-faint dwarfs, Hercules and Leo IV, using star counts from the HST photometry of brown12 . Over the mass range from 0.5-0.8 , they found that the best fitting power law had a slope of 1.2 , much shallower than the salpeter55 value of = 2.35 . While the uncertainties for Leo IV are quite large, in Hercules the slope disagrees with a Salpeter IMF at 5.8 .

The physical reasoning supporting the idea of tidal stripping or tidal disruption for these satellites ranges from morphology (highly elongated shapes; Hercules and UMa I), apparent extratidal features (Hercules, Segue 1, and Tuc III), and possible velocity gradients (Hercules and Leo V) to deviations from the luminosity-metallicity relation (Segue 2).

sources.summary: The paper studies Hercules as an elongated ultra-faint dwarf whose stellar extension, possible tidal disruption, distance, and lack of conclusive distance gradient are constrained with HST and Gaia.

quotes.quote: The ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Hercules has an extremely elongated morphology with both photometric overdensities and kinematic members at large radii, suggesting that it may be tidally disrupting due to a previous close encounter with the Milky Way. To explain its observational peculiarities, we present a deep Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging study of Hercules and its surrounding regions and investigate its tidal history through a careful search for a distance gradient along its stretched body. Our off-center HST data clearly resolve a main sequence, showing that the stellar extension seen along the major-axis of Hercules is genuine, not a clump of background galaxies.

Utilizing Gaia DR2 data, we clean the region around Hercules of field contamination, and find four new plausible member stars, all of which are located at the outskirts of the dwarf galaxy. We update the distance to Hercules, and find 130.6 1.2 kpc ( m-M=20.58 0.02 ) for the main body, which is consistent with earlier estimates in the literature. While we find no conclusive evidence for a distance gradient, our work demonstrates that constraining a distance gradient in such a faint system is not trivial, and the possible thickness of the dwarf along the line of sight and field contamination make it harder to make decisive conclusions even with these high-precision data.

sources.summary: The paper presents new spectroscopy of the dwarf galaxy Hercules, identifies three distant member stars, and finds no velocity gradient or evidence that Hercules is tidally disrupting.

quotes.quote: We present a new spectroscopic study of the dwarf galaxy Hercules (d 132 kpc) with data from the Anglo-Australian Telescope and its AAOmega spectrograph together with the Two Degree Field multi-object system to solve the conundrum that whether Hercules is tidally disrupting. We combine broadband photometry, proper motions from Gaia, and our Pristine narrow-band and metallicity-sensitive photometry to efficiently weed out the Milky Way contamination. Such cleaning is particularly critical in this kinematic regime, as both the transverse and heliocentric velocities of Milky Way populations overlap with Hercules.

Thanks to this method, three new member stars are identified, including one at almost 10 _h of the satellite. All three have velocities and metallicities consistent with that of the main body. Combining this new dataset with the entire literature cleaned out from contamination shows that Hercules does not exhibit a velocity gradient (d v /d = 0.1^ +0.4 _ 0.2 km s ^ 1 arcmin ^ 1 ) and, as such, does not show evidence to undergo tidal disruption.

sources.summary: The paper confirms Hercules as one of the most elliptical Milky Way satellites, with maximum-likelihood ellipticity matching deeper LBT follow-up and a similar half-light radius.

quotes.quote: Her - This galaxy is one of the recently discovered galaxies that deeper follow-up data from the Large Binocular Telescope (LBT) revealed to be more elliptical 73 , 0.65 coleman07 than from the smoothed maps of the SDSS 60 , 0.5 belokurov07a . Since it could be the sign that this latter value is driven by the smoothing of the shallow data, it is particularly interesting that our ML value ( =0.68^ +0.06 _ 0.08 ) is very close to the coleman07 value. Even from the SDSS data alone, we confirm that Her is one of the most elliptical satellites of the MW. On the other hand, the half-light radius we measure is similar to that of r_h 8.4' belokurov07a . The one measured from the LBT dataset was erroneously listed as 4.4 0.3' where it should have been 9.4 1.4' (Matthew Coleman, private communication), once again very similar to the value of the ML fit.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Hercules as incompatible with a universal 10^7 solar-mass scale, attributing its low mass estimate to a revised central velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: The Common Mass Scale -- Hercules and Leo IV St08 claimed that all the dSphs shared a common mass scale of 10^7 within 300 pc.

Fig. fig:comparis shows that even within the uncertainties, Hercules and Leo IV are not compatible with a common mass scale of 10^7 . For Hercules, this is because the central velocity dispersion was revised downward to 3.7 0.9 kms ^ 1 by Ad09 , who used Str o mgren photometry to discriminate between foreground Milky Way dwarf stars and Hercules giants. This compares with the value of 5.1 0.9 kms ^ 1 originally given by Si07 . Even allowing for generous model uncertainties, Fig. fig:comparis shows the mass of Hercules lies below 3 10^6 at 300 pc ( 0.9 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats Hercules as a recently discovered Milky Way satellite whose elongation and tentative velocity gradient imply an orbit with a very small perigalacticon, explaining tidal destruction of the dwarf galaxy into a stellar stream.

quotes.quote: We investigate the possibility that Hercules, a recently discovered Milky Way (MW) satellite, is a stellar stream in the process of formation. This hypothesis is motivated by Hercules' highly elongated shape as well as the measurement of a tentative radial velocity gradient along its body . The application of simple analytical techniques on radial velocity data of its member stars provides tight constraints on the tangential velocity of the system ( v_ t = -16^ +6 _ 22 , relative to the Galactic Standard of Rest). Combining this with its large receding velocity ( 145 ) and distance ( 138 ) yields an orbit that would have taken Hercules to within 6^ +9 _ 2 of the Galactic centre approximately 0.6 ago. This very small perigalacticon can naturally explain the violent tidal destruction of the dwarf galaxy in the MW's gravitational potential, inducing its transformation into a stellar stream.

sources.summary: The paper describes Hercules dSph as a dwarf spheroidal with broad metallicity and neutron-capture anomalies; Her-3's binarity does not explain the galaxy's peculiar abundance pattern.

quotes.quote: We discuss the further implications for the chemical enrichment history of the Hercules dSph, but find no compelling binary scenario that could reasonably explain the full, peculiar abundance pattern of the Hercules dSph galaxy.

Previous studies have identified it with a metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] =-2.04 dex) member of the dSph, and it lies towards the more metal-rich tail of Hercules owing to the galaxy's very extended metallicity distribution (reaching below 3 dex; e.g., Ad en et al. 2009, 2011). One of the most striking features of Hercules is a, seemingly, overall deficiency in neutron-capture elements (represented by the Ba-measurements of Koch et al. 2013), along with abundance patterns in individual stars including Her-3 that suggest that the galaxy was imprinted with the ejecta of only a few, massive type II supernovae (SNe II).

Koch et al. (2008, 2013) argued that the anomalies in the Hercules dSph galaxy are possibly the imprints of only a few, early massive ( 35 M _ ) SNe II. The low masses of these dwarf galaxies also incur very low star forming efficiencies, coupled with stochastic, localized chemical enrichment and inhomogeneous mixing of the SNe ejecta with the Interstellar Medium (ISM).

sources.summary: Hercules was an ultra-faint dSph ATCA target; its candidate FoV sources require large annihilation rates, while its small size helps limit profile-shape uncertainty.

quotes.quote: The targets included three classical dSphs (CDS), Carina, Fornax, and Sculptor, and three ultra-faint dSphs (UDS), BootesII, Hercules and Segue2, for a total of 123 hours of observing time.

Intriguingly, only the sources in Carina and Hercules FoVs would require pretty large annihilation rates, while all the other values are well compatible with constraints from other DM indirect searches.

Note that in the case of Hercules (and to a lesser extent of Carina) the variation is quite limited. This is because the size of the dSph is relatively small, and the stellar velocity dispersion is relatively well-known. The latter allows a more precise determination of the DM profile with respect to the other UDS (BootesII and Segue2).

sources.summary: The paper discusses Hercules as a 138 kpc dwarf spheroidal satellite with tidal disruption, a metallicity spread, old giants, and evidence for early star formation.

quotes.quote: Hercules Hercules is a dwarf spheroidal satellite of the Milky Way, found at a distance of 138 kpc. This UfDSph has been discovered by belo2007 . cole2007 performed deep wide-field photometry in B, V and of this galaxy using the Large Binocular Telescope down to 1.5 mag below the main sequence turn-off and found that the Hercules dwarf is highly elongated suggesting tidal disruption as a likely cause. Later, aden2009 analyzed 11 stars in Hercules and obtained a metallicity spread ranging from [ Fe/H ] = 2.03 to 3.17 dex.

A comparison of their spectroscopic stellar parameters with isochrones indicates that the giants in Hercules are older than 10 Gyrs. Deep g,i-band DECam stellar photometry of the Hercules Milky Way satellite galaxy, and its surrounding field, out to a radial distance of 5.4 times the tidal radius was done by .They identified nine extended stellar substructures associated with the dwarf, preferentially distributed along the major axis of the galaxy demonstrating that Hercules is a strongly tidally disrupted system.

sources.summary: The paper tests whether Hercules is a disrupting Milky Way satellite transforming from a bound dwarf galaxy into a stellar stream, deriving an orbit with small pericenter and tidal disruption.

quotes.quote: We investigate the possibility that the recently discovered Hercules Milky Way satellite is in fact a stellar stream in formation, thereby explaining its very elongated shape with an axis ratio of 3 to 1. Under the assumption that Hercules is a stellar stream and that its stars are flowing along the orbit of its progenitor, we find an orbit that would have recently brought the system close enough to the Milky Way to induce its disruption and transformation from a bound dwarf galaxy into a stellar stream. The application of simple analytical techniques to the tentative radial velocity gradient observed in the satellite provides tight constraints on the tangential velocity of the system ( v_ t = -16^ +6 _ 22 in the Galactic Standard of Rest). Combined with its large receding velocity, the determined tangential velocity yields an orbit with a small pericentric distance ( R_ peri = 6^ +9 _ 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper says Hercules is poorly matched by the model at low metallicity and alpha abundance, with sub-solar alpha stars suggesting possible Type Ia enrichment.

quotes.quote: The brown14 model for Hercules has 82 of the stars in the first burst. As with the previous systems, the model overestimates the number of low-metallicity stars, with the cross-correlation showing a lag of 0.6 dex in [ Fe/H ] for the model. Fig. f:afefeh shows that [ /Fe ] observed in Hercules is lower than predicted by the model, suggesting that the gas that formed the first burst of stars was enriched by stars with lower alpha abundances than a 25 M _ supernova. Five of the 13 stars show sub-solar [ /Fe ] , suggesting Type Ia enrichment.

sources.summary: The paper prioritizes Simon data for Hercules, supplements with Aden stars, and includes faint non-spectroscopic candidates because excluding them significantly changes the average velocity.

quotes.quote: Hercules (I) In case of Hercules we assign the highest priority to the work of Simon_07 . We complement the analysis with the stars in Aden_09 , which are were all classified as members in that study, even though not all of them have spectroscopic information, e.g. as some candidate horizontal branch. Since the stars without spectroscopy are faint, excluding them does cause significant changes in the average velocity, given the error-bars. We therefore include them in our final estimate.

sources.summary: The paper notes previous evidence for outer Hercules stars and RRL beyond its tidal radius, including 12 known RRL and membership in the clean extended-population sample.

quotes.quote: Hercules is also known for having stars located in its outer regions: identified 3 RRL that lie outside its tidal radius, and more recently reports 3 new members of Hercules, one of them being at 9.5 R_h from its center.
An exception is Hercules. The galaxy has been reported to have 12 RRL by and , where the latter work showed that Hercules has indeed RRL outside its tidal radius.
The galaxies in the clean sample with RRL at S_ ell 4 R_h are Tucana II, Tucana III, Grus II, Sagittarius II, Pegasus IV, Hercules, Ursa Major I, Centaurus I, Leo IV, and Eridanus II.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Hercules as a new probable dwarf galaxy satellite, placing it about 140 kpc away with a 320 pc half-light radius, extended morphology, and possible multiple stellar populations.

quotes.quote: The first row of Figure  fig:objsa refers to a satellite in Coma Berenices (henceforth Com), the second to a satellite in Canes Venatici (henceforth CVn II), the third to a probable globular cluster (henceforth Segue 1), the fourth to a satellite in Hercules (henceforth Her) and the fifth to a satellite in Leo (henceforth Leo IV)  There is already a dwarf galaxy known in the constellation of Canes Venatici  Zu06a , and two known in Leo. Leo III is an alternative name for the Leo dwarf irregular, also called Leo A  Be00 . .

Her lies at a distance of 140^ +13 _ 12 kpc and has a half-light radius of 320 pc. It has an extended morphology. Its CMD shows not just a giant branch, but both blue and red horizontal branches, which may hint at multiple stellar populations.

sources.summary: The paper cites Hercules as an ultra-faint dwarf whose old stellar population and star-formation history make it a strong fossil candidate relevant to DLA comparisons.

quotes.quote: brown14 found that the stellar populations of five UFDs: Hercules, Leo IV, Ursa Major I, Bootes 1 and Coma Berenices showed similar ages, suggesting that a global event such as reionization truncated their star formation.
For example, Canes Venatici II formed stars until 8-11  Gyr ago, while Hercules and Leo IV formed 90  of their stellar mass by 11-12 Gyr.
They found that Hercules was consistent with having formed all its stars in one burst before and during reionization.

sources.summary: The paper estimates Hercules's enclosed mass at 1.8 half-light radii and at 0.3 kpc, finding agreement with an earlier Hercules mass estimate.

quotes.quote: Hercules (L_ 4 10^4 L_ ) On the low-mass end of Milky Way satellites, for the Hercules dwarf, we find an enclosed mass of M_ est (<1.8 )/ M_ = 6.5_ 2.9 ^ +4.0 10^6 . To compare this value against the mass M<( 0.3 kpc )/ M_ =1.9^ +1.1 _ 0.8 10^6 inferred by Aden2009Hercules , we use the general form of the virial mass estimator (equation eq:massEst ) with ( = 0.3 kpc / ) 2.7 (see Fig. fig:lambdaBias ). We find M_ est (< 0.3 kpc )/ M_ = 2.6_ 1.2 ^ +1.8 10^6 , in good agreement with the findings of Aden2009Hercules .

sources.summary: Hercules is discussed as an elongated ultra-faint dwarf with possible stellar stream extension and earlier velocity-gradient evidence, though newer member-star data argue against ongoing tidal disruption.

quotes.quote: Among the most extreme systems, Hercules ( =0.67 ) seems to be extended by a stream of stars sand2009,roderick2015 a sign that it is a strongly tidally disrupted system. These conclusions have been supported by a tentative evidence for a velocity gradient aden2009 . However, relying on three new star members, longeard2023 didn't confirmed this gradient and concluded that Hercules does not show evidence to undergo tidal disruption.

gmw_00001871.name: MRC 0156 252
gmw_00001871.overview: MRC 0156 252 is a high-redshift radio galaxy [arXiv:1610.02057, arXiv:astro-ph/0412026] that hosts an active galactic nucleus with a direct line of sight [arXiv:1610.02057]. The galaxy features very extended emission-line gas [arXiv:1610.02057] and a host galaxy that is extended parallel to the radio axis in the near-infrared [arXiv:astro-ph/0412026].

sources.summary: MRC 0156 252 has very extended emission-line gas, bright line emission, an AGN line of sight, a large velocity gradient, and jet-swept ambient gas.

quotes.quote: MRC 0156-252 MRC 0156 252 at z = 2.02 is one of our galaxies with very extended emission-line gas. Line emission is bright, but given the somewhat difficult redshift, we only detect [ OIII ] 4959,5007, H , H , and [ NII ] 6583 in the integrated spectrum (Fig. fig:intspec ). We probe [ OIII ] 5007 over an area of 69 kpc 23 kpc along the major and minor axis, respectively. The emission-line region corresponds to a bright inner region, which extends over 1.5 (13 kpc), and two extended emission-line plumes, with the larger plume to the south.

MRC 0156-252 offers a direct line of sight into the AGN, as seen through nuclear H broad-line emission nesvadba11a . MRC 0156 252 has a regular velocity gradient throughout the central regions and the extended lobes with a total velocity offset of 1600 km s ^ 1 , one of the largests in our sample. This is not the case here, where the gas near the north-western lobe appears very extended, with a smooth velocity gradient of 800 km s ^ 1 , which is similar to that in the south-west of the galaxy. This is not what would be expected from a gas cloud within the surrounding dark-matter environment of MRC 0156 252, but rather ambient gas that has been swept up by the radio jet cocoon.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0156--252 as a z=2.09 radio galaxy with an extended near-infrared host, Ly emission spanning the hotspots, shock-related jet deflection, and high northern-hotspot rotation measure.

quotes.quote: 0156--252 at z=2.09 has several characteristics reminiscent of the well-studied radio galaxy PKS 1138--262 carilli02,laura00 . Near-infrared continuum shows a host galaxy extended over 2 parallel to the radio axis, and narrow-band Ly shows emission line gas extended over the entire region encompassed by the northern and southern radio hot spots. The Ly brightness is at a minimum at the position of the host galaxy and peaks at the location where there is a sharp bend in the (northern) radio jet, probably due to shock-induced ionization of the gas where the radio jet is deflected by denser material. The northern hot spot has an intrinsic RM of 1000 rad m ^ 2 .

gmw_00001872.name: MRC 0251 273
gmw_00001872.overview: MRC 0251 273 is a high-redshift radio galaxy [arXiv:1610.02057, arXiv:1010.1385] located at a redshift of approximately 3.17 [arXiv:1610.02057]. It features a compact radio core [arXiv:1010.1385] embedded within a bright, marginally resolved emission-line gas region [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: MRC 0251 273 is a compact radio source and bright line emitter with marginally resolved gas, uniform broad lines, small velocity offsets, and radio structure embedded in emission-line gas.

quotes.quote: MRC 0251-273 MRC 0251 273 at z = 3.17 has a fairly compact radio source with an upper limit of LAS < 3.9 \ debreuck10 and a FIR luminosity <1 10^ 13 L _ measured with SCUBA reuland04 . It is undetected with SPIRE with an upper limit of L^ IR _ tot =7.6 10^ 12 L _ of infrared luminosity drouart14 . It is a bright, marginally spatially resolved line emitter at a seeing of 0.9 0.8 , associated with a fairly bright continuum (Fig. fig:maps ). Velocities and line widths are uniform, with FWHM=681 9 km s ^ 1 (the error refers to [ OIII ] 5007).

The line emission extends over an area of 3.5 2.5 \ on the sky, corresponding to 26 kpc 18 kpc at z = 3.17, and after deconvolving with the size of the seeing disk. The velocity offsets are relatively small, with a total offset of 500 km s ^ 1 . The radio map in the right panels of Fig. fig:maps shows a small double-peaked structure that is embedded within the emission-line gas and well aligned with the region of blueshifted gas around the center of the emission-line region. The areas of broadest line width are mostly found adjacent to the radio contours.

sources.summary: The paper uses new radio imaging and IRAC identification to place the core of MRC 0251 273 and interpret its complex morphology as a likely approaching jet.

quotes.quote: MRC 0251 273 ( z=3.16 ): Our radio map (Appendix B) shows five bright components at 8.46 GHz as well as diffuse emission to the north-northwest (mainly at 4.85 GHz). The lower-resolution radio map of confirms that this diffuse emission is real. Within the radio structure, there is only a single IRAC identification, coinciding within 0 3 with the northernmost of the bright components in the 8.46 GHz map. We therefore identify this component as the radio core. This radio morphology resembles that of B2 0902+34 car95 .

gmw_00001873.name: MP J0340 6507
gmw_00001873.overview: MP J0340 6507 is a galaxy at a redshift of z = 2.28 that exhibits extended line emission along a north-to-south axis [arXiv:1610.02057]. Its asymmetric emission-line region is shifted toward the southwest, while its bright continuum peaks at the same position as the line emission [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: MP J0340 6507 shows extended line emission, asymmetric [OIII], a bright continuum coincident with the line peak, a small velocity gradient, and broad line widths.

quotes.quote: MP J0340 6507 MP J0340 6507 at z = 2.28 shows extended (3.8 1.8 , corrected for the size of the seeing disk), line emission along an axis going from north to south. The [ OIII ] 4959,5007 emission-line region is asymmetry, and shifted by 0.5 \ towards south-west. The continuum is fairly bright and peaks at the same position as the line emission. We note a small velocity gradient of 200 km s ^ 1 . Line widths are FWHM = 800 1000 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00001874.name: PKS 0529 549
gmw_00001874.overview: PKS 0529 549 is a high-redshift radio and starburst galaxy at z ≈ 2.6 that hosts a Type-II active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1610.02057, arXiv:1807.03321]. It features a rotating molecular gas disk, cold dust emission, and ionized gas outflows aligned with its two radio lobes [arXiv:1610.02057, arXiv:1807.03321].

sources.summary: PKS 0529 549 has extended oxygen and hydrogen emission, a regular velocity gradient, broad lines, a strong rotation-measure environment, and radio lobes aligned with the emission-line region.

quotes.quote: PKS 0529-549 PKS 0529 549 at z = 2.57 has extended [ OIII ] 4959,5007 and H morphologies and a regular velocity gradient of 460 km s ^ 1 (Fig. fig:maps2 ). Line widths are of-order FWHM 700 km s ^ 1 (Table tab:emlines ). In total we detect H , H , H , [ OIII ] 4959,5007, [ OI ] 6300,6363, [ NII ] 6583, and [ SII ] 6724.

We detect the continuum in the 'deep' cube which is unresolved at a spatial resolution of 1.2 1.2 \ and well centered relative to the emission-line region and velocity gradient. broderick07 found a very high rotation measure of 9600 rad m ^ 2 , the largest found so far in a radio galaxy at z 2, suggesting a particularly strong magnetic field, dense environment, or both, and a radio source with largest angular size of 1.2 . humphrey11 found bright 1.1 m emission from cold dust with AzTEC. The radio morphology shows a small double source, which has a very similar size to the emission-line region and is well aligned with it. The velocity jump seen in the gas appears to occur between the two radio lobes, consistent with expectations from a back-to-back outflow. The broadest line widths are found inbetween the two radio lobes, which are each associated with gas with FWHM 700-800 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0529-549 as a z=2.6 AGN-host starburst/radio galaxy with rotating molecular gas, ionized outflow, and baryon-dominated inner dynamics.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We present a multiwavelength study of the AGN-host starburst galaxy PKS 0529-549 at z 2.6 . We use (1) new ALMA observations of the dust continuum and of the \ 370 m line, tracing molecular gas, (2) SINFONI spectroscopy of the 5007 \ line, tracing ionized gas, and (3) ATCA radio continuum images, tracing synchrotron emission. Both \ and \ show regular velocity gradients, but their systemic velocities and position angles differ by 300 \ and 30 ^ , respectively. The \ is consistent with a rotating disc, aligned with the dust and stellar continuum, while the \ likely traces an outflow, aligned with two AGN-driven radio lobes.

Here we compare neutral and ionized gas kinematics in the starburst galaxy PKS 0529-549 at z 2.6 , combining new (2-1) data from ALMA with IFS data from SINFONI Nesvadba2017 . PKS 0529-549 is a well-studied radio galaxy with an exquisite set of ancillary data: optical spectroscopy, ground-based NIR imaging, and radio polarimetry Broderick2007 , Spitzer /IRAC imaging deBreuck2010 , and 1.1 mm data from AzTEC Humphrey2011 . PKS 0529-549 hosts a Type-II AGN and shows two radio lobes.

gmw_00001875.name: 5C 07.269
gmw_00001875.overview: 5C 07.269 is a faint radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 2.22 [arXiv:1610.02057]. It features extended emission-line gas with an irregular velocity distribution and a central region of broad line widths associated with its radio core [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: 5C 07.269 is described as a fainter radio source with detected optical lines, faint continuum, extended emission-line gas, irregular velocities, and central broad line widths.

quotes.quote: 5C 07.269 5C 07.269 at z=2.22 is one of our fainter sources in the radio, with P_ 500 = 27.8 W Hz ^ 1 at 500 MHz in the rest-frame, and a largest angular size of 7.6 \ in the radio debreuck10 . The integrated spectrum shows [ OIII ] 4959,5007 in the H band, and H , [ NII ] 6548,6583, and [ SII ] 6716,6731 in the K-band. We also detect the faint continuum near the geometric center of the emission-line region. The emission-line maps in Fig. fig:maps2 show a source that is extended along an axis that goes from the south-east to the north-west.

The velocity distribution is irregular, with a total velocity range of 350 km s ^ 1 around an average redshift z = 2.22505, but much of that scatter might be due to the low signal-to-noise ratio of this source. The distribution of line widths appears more regular, with a maximum of 1100 km s ^ 1 near the center, and more moderate velocities of 200-300 km s ^ 1 in the periphery. The broadest gas near the center seems to be associated with the small, unresolved radio core (right panels of Fig. fig:maps2 ).

gmw_00001876.name: TN J1112 2948
gmw_00001876.overview: TN J1112 2948 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.1 [arXiv:1610.02057]. It features very extended line emission asymmetric about its radio core, a resolved nuclear region, and a south-eastern gas plume extending 38 kpc from the nucleus [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: TN J1112 2948 has very extended line emission, a resolved nuclear region, a south-eastern gas plume, asymmetric emission around the radio core, and a large velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: TN J1112-2948 Line emission in TN J1112 2948 at z = 3.1 is very extended with a size of 6.7 2.2 \ (52.3 kpc 17.3 kpc at z = 3.1, Fig. fig:maps2 ). We detect [ OIII ] 4959,5007, H , and [ OII ] 3727. The emission-line maps shown in Fig. fig:maps2 show high surface-brightness line emission in a resolved area around the nucleus, extending over 2.3 2.0 \ (18 kpc 16 kpc at z = 3.1) with a small velocity gradient of 180 km s ^ 1 and line widths between FWHM 600 and 800 km s ^ 1 . Towards south-east, we see an extended gas plume extending out to a distance of 38 kpc from the nucleus, with velocities of up to 400 km s ^ 1 and FWHM line widths of 300 500 km s ^ 1 .

The extended line emission in TN J1112 2948 is asymmetric about the radio core, which is associated with the northern cloud. The southern cloud is directly adjacent to the southern radio hotspot, although not perfectly aligned. The overall velocities in TN J1112 2948 are monotonically increasing from the north-west to the south-east with a total gradient of 700 km s ^ 1 . reuland04 detected TN J1112 2948 with SCUBA, Herschel photometry has unfortunately not been obtained for this galaxy.

gmw_00001877.name: TXS 1113 178
gmw_00001877.overview: TXS 1113 178 is a high-redshift radio galaxy at a redshift of z = 2.24 [arXiv:1610.02057]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with nuclear broad H-alpha emission and a strong velocity gradient aligned with its radio jets [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: TXS 1113 178 shows nuclear broad H emission, compact residual line emission, identified optical lines, a strong velocity gradient, and alignment with the radio jets.

quotes.quote: TXS 1113-178 TXS 1113 178 at z = 2.24 shows a broad H emission line coming from the nucleus nesvadba11a , which made it necessary to remove the nuclear point source before analyzing the extended line emission in this source ( ssec:blrremoval ). We do detect residual line emission associated with the continuum emission from the nucleus, although it is fairly compact, extending over 1.3 1.1 . We identify [ OIII ] 4959,5007, H , H , and [ NII ] 6583 in the integrated spectrum shown in Fig. fig:intspec .

In spite of the low spatial resolution of the data, we do detect a velocity gradient of 700 km s ^ 1 from north-west to south-east, and potentially a gradient in FWHM line width of 1200 km s ^ 1 to 500 km s ^ 1 from the center to the periphery of the emission-line region. The velocity gradient is well aligned with the direction of the radio jets in TXS 1113 178 (Fig. fig:maps2 ), although we do not see extended gas. This could be the consequence of the short exposure time for this source.

gmw_00001878.name: USS1243 + 036
gmw_00001878.overview: The high-redshift radio galaxy USS1243 + 036, also known as 4C 03.24, features highly irregular and extended emission-line regions [arXiv:1610.02057] alongside a point X-ray source [arXiv:1702.04725]. It exhibits diffuse radio emission [arXiv:2105.03467], extended X-ray lobes [arXiv:1702.04725], and strong jet-gas interactions [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: USS1243 + 036 has very extended and irregular line emission, offset blobs, strong jet-gas interaction signatures, and exceptionally broad velocity and linewidth ranges.

quotes.quote: USS 1243+036 USS 1243 + 036 at z = 2.36 is one of the galaxies with very extended line emission in our sample. The projected size of the emission-line region is 5.3 0.9 \ (corresponding to 38 kpc 4 kpc after deconvolution with the seeing disk), and the emission-line morphology is very irregular. The nucleus as probed by the continuum emission is roughly centered within a resolved inner emission line region, but surrounded by two more extended emission-line blobs towards the north-west and south-east, which are very different from each other. The region where the jet is escaping from the emission-line region first noted by vanojik96 shows particularly large velocity dispersions and a strong blueshift, as expected for regions of strong interactions between jet and gas.

Velocities and FWHM line widths in USS1243 + 036 have an exceptionally large dynamic range, which is why we present two sets of [ OIII ] 5007 line maps in Fig. fig:maps2 , which only differ in their dynamic range for the line widths and velocity gradients. The velocity gradient in the extended gas (lower panel of Fig. fig:maps2 ) is 500 km s ^ 1 between the south-eastern and north-western lobe, with a monotonic velocity increase from the north-west to the south-east. The south-eastern lobe has broader lines, FWHM = 700 900 km s ^ 1 than the north-western lobe, which has FWHM 300 km s ^ 1 . The right panel of Fig. fig:maps2 shows that the southern radio hotspot is associated with the most strongly blueshifted gas in the southern emission-line region, and the region of broadest line widths.

sources.summary: The paper detects a point X-ray source and extended lobes in 4C 03.24, compares its radial profile to the PSF, and measures extended and point-source fluxes.

quotes.quote: A similar procedure to what described above for 4C 41.17 was adopted to analyze the ACIS data of 4C 03.24, consisting of one observation with (good) exposure time of 90.6 ks. The full-band, raw \ image and the smoothed subpixel ( 0.12 ) image are shown in the top panels of Fig. 4c03_fig . A point \ source is clearly detected. Extended \ lobes appear to be along the NW-S direction. The 1.4 GHz radio contours are also shown in the upper right panel, overlaid on the smoothed \ image.

The radial photon density profiles of 4C 03.24 is compared to the simulated PSF's in Fig. 4c03_ctsden_fig , showing evidence for extended \ emission beyond 1 . The total, full-band counts for the extended emission and the point-source are 34.1^ +6.9 _ 5.8 and 4.6^ +3.3 _ 2.1 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C + 03.24 as having diffuse radio emission beyond its hot spots, uncertain lobe size, extended X-ray emission, and a northeast lobe undetected in radio.

quotes.quote: 4C + 03.24 The best interferometric images of 4C + 03.24 show diffuse radio emission beyond the hot spots, but the size of any lobe cannot be reliably measured. Thus, we base the size of the lobe on the smoothed X-ray image, shown in Figure fig:images . This leads to some uncertainty on the X-ray flux and volume for the lobe beyond that reported in Table table:decomposed_lobes , but it is clear that the X-ray emission extends beyond the bright radio contours.
Meanwhile, the lobe of B3 0727+409, the northeast lobe of 4C + 03.24, and the southern lobe of 4C + 19.71 are not detected in the radio, so we compute lower limits to the / \ and / \ ratios.

sources.summary: For 4C+03.24, the paper fixes systemic redshift from [C I] because He II is undetected, and interprets narrow 40-50 km/s emission as single molecular-cloud gas.

quotes.quote: In two sources, the He II line cannot be fit due to its low S/N. Here, the [ C i ] line is used to constrain the systemic redshift for the 4C+03.24 and 4C+19.71. In 4C+03.24, He II is not detected and we fix the redshift to the centre of the [ C i ] line (bottom panel of Fig. fig:galaxy-spectra-images ).

In MRC 0943-242 and 4C+03.24, the dispersions range from 40-50 km s ^ 1 and are insufficiently broad to cover the rotational velocity of the host galaxy and can therefore only trace single molecular clouds within the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper compares 4C 03.24 with another z>3.5 radio galaxy, noting extended X-ray emission and hotspot/lobe energy-density behavior in jet SED modelling.

quotes.quote: Although we considered admittedly extreme either-or scenarios, the results from this modelling exercise are not incompatible with the conclusions drawn by wu17 , who followed the same approach in modelling the SED of 4C 41.17 and 4C 03.24, i.e. the two other (known) z > 3.5 radio galaxies with (reliably) extended X-ray emission. For both systems, the energy density in magnetic field was found to vastly exceed that of the CMB radiation in the hotspots (whereas the opposite is true for the extended lobes). This implies that, in both systems, most of the X-ray signal is likely produced within the lobes, via IC/CMB, whereas most of the radio signal is produced within the hotspots, via synchrotron, thus washing out the steep (1+z)^4 enhancement of the X-ray vs. radio luminosity expected of IC/CMB.

gmw_00001879.name: MG 1251 + 1104
gmw_00001879.overview: MG 1251 + 1104 is a compact emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.32 [arXiv:1610.02057]. Its spectrum reveals oxygen, hydrogen, and nitrogen lines with broad central widths, alongside a compact radio structure [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: MG 1251 + 1104 is a compact line emitter with detected oxygen, hydrogen, and nitrogen lines, possible redshifted zones, broad central line widths, and compact radio size.

quotes.quote: MG 1251 + 1104 MG 1251 + 1104 at z = 2.32 is a rather compact line emitter, for which we have only shallow data with 2400 s of on-source exposure time with an average seeing of FWHM = 0.8 0.8 . The FWHM size of the line image is 0.9 , observed at a seeing of FWHM=0.8 . In the integrated spectrum we detect [ OIII ] 5007, H , and [ NII ] 6583. The latter two lines are blended owing to the broad line widths of FWHM = 914 32 km s ^ 1 .

While we do not detect a large velocity offset, to the north-west and south-east from the nucleus there appears to be a zone of more redshifted line emission, with a total velocity offset of 200 km s ^ 1 relative to the most blueshifted gas. Line widths are up to 1300 km s ^ 1 near the center and decrease to 500 km s ^ 1 at the periphery. We cannot exclude that deeper observations would have revealed fainter extended line emission. For MG 1251 + 1104, we do not have a high-resolution radio image available. debreuck00 list it as a compact source with LAS < 1.2 , which corresponds roughly to the size of the emission-line region.

gmw_00001880.name: MRC 1324 262
gmw_00001880.overview: MRC 1324 262 is a galaxy at redshift z = 2.28 with a bright, broad [OIII] emission line [arXiv:1610.02057]. It exhibits a slight east-western spatial extension, an undetected continuum, and radio hotspots located near the outer edges of its emission line region [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: MRC 1324 262 is a shallow-data source with bright broad [OIII], several detected lines, slight spatial extension, no detected continuum, and radio hotspots near the emission-line edges.

quotes.quote: MRC 1324 262 MRC 1324 262 at z = 2.28 is another source for which we only have shallow data with an on-source exposure time of 2400 s and seeing of 1.1 1.1 . The [ OIII ] 5007 line is bright, well detected, and fairly broad, with FWHM = 1253 36 km s ^ 1 . We also detect H , H , and [ SII ] 6716,6731. The integrated spectrum is shown in Fig. fig:intspec and individual line properties are listed in Table tab:emlines .

The source appears almost compact in this data set, perhaps with a slight extension in east-western direction. The continuum is not detected (Fig. fig:maps ). We do not detect any distinctive features in the velocity map, and given the large seeing and shallow data set, irregularities in the map of FWHM line widths could be dominated by noise. The radio maps shown in Fig. fig:maps2 show a small double source, where each radio hotspot is near the outer edge of the emission line region.

gmw_00001881.name: TN J1338 1942
gmw_00001881.overview: TN J1338 1942 is a massive radio galaxy at z ≈ 4.1 [arXiv:1610.02057, arXiv:2212.09769] that exhibits a double-lobed structure [arXiv:2401.11612] and hosts an active galactic nucleus driving large-scale ionized gas outflows [arXiv:2401.11612, arXiv:2401.12199]. Located within a dense protocluster environment [arXiv:astro-ph/0606298, arXiv:astro-ph/0610567], the galaxy features extensive jet-associated star formation [arXiv:2212.09769] and a highly asymmetric Lyman-alpha halo [arXiv:1610.02057, arXiv:2212.09769].

sources.summary: TN J1338 1942 is a high-redshift galaxy detected in [NeIII] and [OII], with resolved line emission matching previous Ly and continuum structure and compact radio morphology.

quotes.quote: TN J1338-1942 The second-highest redshift galaxy in our sample at z =4.1 . Unfortunately, TN J1338 1942 falls at a somewhat difficult redshift for NIR observations, where the bright [ OIII ] 4959,5007 and H lines are at wavelengths redward of the K-band. [ OII ] 3737 falls into the spectral region of low atmospheric transmission between the H and K band and [ NeIII ] falls into the blue part of the K-band at 2 m. In spite of these challenges we detect TN J1338 1942 in [ NeIII ] 3869,3968 and in [ OII ] 3727. The brightest line is [ NeIII ] 3869, which we also resolve spatially (Fig. fig:maps2 ).

Line emission extends over 1.5 \ and matches the size and orientation of the Ly and continuum emission of zirm05 , which is embedded in a > 100 kpc, highly asymmetric Ly halo venemans02 . The S/N of the line emission is very low, but suggests a velocity gradient of-order 500 km s ^ 1 , and very faint line emission with a velocity offset of 500 km s ^ 1 towards west. The radio morphology of TN J1338 1942 is compact. The nucleus is found just east of this wedge, and conincides with the area of highest FWHMs.

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST/NIRCam observations confirming TN J1338--1942 as a massive z=4.11 radio galaxy with AGN jets, shocks, and extensive recent jet-associated star formation.

quotes.quote: We present the first JWST observations of the z=4.11 luminous radio galaxy TN J1338--1942, obtained as part of the ``Prime Extragalactic Areas for Reionization and Lensing Science'' (``PEARLS'') project. Our NIRCam observations, designed to probe the key rest-frame optical continuum and emission line features at this redshift, enable resolved spectral energy distribution modelling that incorporates both a range of stellar population assumptions and radiative shock models. With an estimated stellar mass of _ 10 (M/ M _ ) 10.9 , TN J1338--1942 is confirmed to be one of the most massive galaxies known at this epoch. Our observations also reveal extremely high equivalent-width nebular emission coincident with the luminous AGN jets that is consistent with radiative shocks surrounded by extensive recent star-formation. We estimate the total star-formation rate (SFR) could be as high as 1800 M _ yr ^ 1 , with the SFR that we attribute to the jet induced burst conservatively 500 M _ yr ^ 1 .

The luminous high redshift radio galaxy (HzRG), TN J1338--1942 TNJ1338 hereafter; z=4.11 DeBreuck1999 , is one of the most powerful radio sources known in the early Universe. Initially selected based on its bright radio continuum emission and ultra-steep radio spectral index S_ ^ 1.31 DeBreuck2000 , TNJ1338 was subsequently discovered to reside at the heart of a significant over-density of galaxies extending to Mpc scales Venemans2002, Miley2004, Zirm2005, Intema2006, Overzier2008, Saito2015 , making it one of the earliest known proto-cluster structures, and a potential progenitor of the most massive clusters (and brightest cluster galaxies) in the local Universe. As host to extremely luminous extended Ly emission L_ Ly 4 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 ; DeBreuck2000,Venemans2002,Swinbank2015 , potential jet-induced star-formation activity Miley2004, Zirm2005 and evidence of large active galactic nuclei (AGN) driven outflows Swinbank2015 , TNJ1338 and its wider environment therefore represent a unique laboratory for studying both AGN feedback in action and the earliest stages of proto-cluster formation.

sources.summary: The paper uses JWST/NIRSpec to show TNJ1338-1942 is a z=4.104 radio galaxy with double-lobed FRII structure and powerful jet-driven ionized outflows.

quotes.quote: We report observations of a powerful ionized gas outflow in a z = 4.1 luminous ( L_ 1.4GHz 10^ 28.3 \ W \ Hz^ 1 ) radio galaxy TNJ1338-1942 hosting an obscured quasar using the Near Infrared Spectrograph (NIRSpec) on board JWST. We spatially resolve a large-scale ( 15 kpc) outflow and measure resolved outflow rates. The outflowing gas shows velocities exceeding 900 km \ s^ 1 and broad line profiles with line widths exceeding 1200 km \ s^ 1 located at 10 kpc projected distance from the central nucleus. The outflowing nebula spatially overlaps with the brightest radio lobe, indicating that the powerful radio jets are responsible for the extraordinary kinematics exhibited by the ionized gas. The ionized gas is possibly ionized by the central obscured quasar with a contribution from shocks.

The luminous high redshift radio galaxy TNJ1338-1942 hereafter TNJ1338; debreuck99 is one of the most powerful radio sources known in the early Universe ( L_ 1.4 \ GHz 10^ 28.3 \ W \ Hz^ 1 at z = 4.104) and one of the first z>4 radio galaxy to be discovered in the southern sky. The radio morphology from VLA indicates a double-lobed structure, resembling a typical FRII radio galaxy, with a projected linear size of 36 kpc. The brightest radio lobe is located 8.8 kpc north of the host galaxy and the fainter one 27.7 kpc to the south (Fig. fig:tnj_RGB ).

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST/NIRSpec IFU spectroscopy of TN J1338 1942, resolving a complex high-redshift radio galaxy with jets, ionized gas clumps, outflows, and possible dual AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We present rest-frame optical spectroscopy using JWST /NIRSpec IFU for the radio galaxy TN J1338 1942 at z=4.1 , one of the most luminous galaxies in the early universe with powerful extended radio jets. Previous observations showed evidence for strong, large-scale outflows on the basis of its large ( 150 kpc) halo detected in , and high velocity \ emission features detected in ground-based IFU data. Our NIRSpec/IFU observations spatially resolve the emission line properties across the host galaxy in great detail. We find at least five concentrations of line emission, coinciding with discrete continuum features previously detected in imaging from HST and JWST , over an extent of 2 \ ( 15 kpc).

These observations have helped shed light on the true nature of TNJ1338 1942, confirming that TNJ1338-a is likely the bright, obscured type-2 AGN photoionizing its surroundings and driving the radio jets observed. However, a combination of our findings have also led to the possibility that TNJ1338 1942 may host a second AGN, at the location of TNJ1338-b. The secondary AGN could trace another galaxy with an active SMBH that is merging with the main system as seen in other dual AGN systems comerford09,comerford14 or it could be due to the presence of a gravitational wave recoiling BH chiaberge17,chiaberge18 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies TN J1338--1942 as a z=4.1 high-redshift radio galaxy embedded in an LBG/LAE overdensity; its surrounding protocluster is the most prominent structure in the field.

quotes.quote: Using the VLT, Venemans et al. ( ) spectroscopically confirmed 20 Lyman- emitters (LAEs) in a 7' 7' field around HzRG TN J1338--1942 at a redshift of z = 4.1 and identified these LAEs with a z = 4.1 protocluster. Further evidence that this LAE overdensity was indeed associated with a protocluster was provided by observations with the HST/ACS that revealed an excess and non-uniform distribution of candidate Lyman break galaxies (LBGs) around the radio galaxy (Miley et al. ; Overzier et al. ). Although the LAE search around TN J1338--1942 was extended with a second 7' 7' field (Venemans ), this was insufficient to determine the boundary of the protocluster.

We draw several conclusions from the present observations: (i) TN J1338--1942 is located in an overdensity of LBGs, both in projection and in redshift space. The new wide-field results are consistent with previous observations which revealed the presence of a protocluster through the overdensities of LAEs (Venemans et al. ; Venemans ) and LBGs (Miley et al. ; Overzier et al. ). This further supports the hypothesis that HzRGs are located in dense environments. The apparent size of the overdensity and the relative position of TN J1338--1942 within the overdensity are similar to that found by Venemans et al. for LAEs.

sources.summary: TN J1338-1942 has spatially extended He II with two spectral components, high star-formation rate, no traceable [C I] in the host, and only an upper-limit molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: The He II emission of TN J1338-1942 is spatially extended and, when extracted to form a 1D spectrum, it is seen to have two distinct spectral profiles: one at the position of the host galaxy and the other offset in projected distance from the host galaxy illustrated in Fig. fig:HeII-IRAC2-TNJ1338 . As a result, a double Gaussian profile, with components that are independent of one another, is fit to the spectrum. The peak, width and line centres are constrained by the same boundary conditions as those used for the single Gaussian fits.

Another anomalous case from our sample is TN J1338-1942 which has a relatively high star-formation rate of 461 M _ yr ^ 1 but no traceable [ C I ] line emission within its host galaxy. Its inferred molecular gas mass is an upper limit of <1.08 10 M _ . For this source, Falkendal2019 reported a 92 GHz ALMA detection which coincides spatially with the northern radio lobe.

sources.summary: The paper describes TN J1338--1942 as a bright z=4.1 radio galaxy whose surrounding Ly-alpha and LBG overdensities confirm a protocluster.

quotes.quote: TN J1338--1942, z = 4.11 This radio galaxy has a redshift of 4.1 deb99,deb01 and is one of the brightest known in \ deb99,deb01 . Because no narrow-band filter is available at the VLT that is centred on the wavelength of a \ line at z=4.1 , we used a custom narrow-band filter with an effective wavelength of 6199 \ and a FWHM of 59 . Narrow-band and R band imaging and follow-up spectroscopy with the VLT of the field of TN J1338--1942 revealed 20 \ emitters within a projected distance of 1.3 Mpc and 600 \ of the radio galaxy ven02 . The structure is overdense in \ emitters by a factor of 4--15 and could be the ancestor of a rich cluster of galaxies. Multi-color imaging with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) on board the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) revealed an anomalously large number of LBGs near the radio galaxy, confirming the presence of a protocluster at z = 4.1 mil04,ove06a .
To further determine the extent and shape of the protocluster region, the field near TN J1338--1942 was imaged at a second position.

sources.summary: The paper cites TN J1338-1942 as a z about 4 radio galaxy likely to be a proto-BCG, comparing its UV luminosity, color, and larger effective radius with the sample.

quotes.quote: Overzier08 found a protocluster at z 4 around a radio galaxy called TN J1338-1942 and suggested that this radio galaxy is likely to be a proto-BCG. Its rest-UV absolute magnitude is 23.0 mag, and the i-z color is 0.1 mag. These values are in good agreement with our proto-BCG sample ( M_ UV 23.2 \ mag , i-z = 0.17 \ mag ). They estimated the effective radius from the z band as R_ e 4.3 \ kpc . Our proto-BCGs have R_ e 2.04 \ kpc , so TN J1338 is larger than our proto-BCG, possibly due to the radio jet.

gmw_00001882.name: USS 1410 011
gmw_00001882.overview: USS 1410 011 is a high-redshift radio galaxy at z = 2.36 with a compact continuum and a large emission-line region [arXiv:1610.02057]. This emission-line gas exhibits a strong north-south velocity gradient, while the galaxy's radio structure is significantly more extended [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: USS 1410 011 has a large emission-line region, a strong north-south velocity gradient, compact continuum, multiple detected lines, and radio structure linked to redshifted gas.

quotes.quote: USS 1410 001 USS 1410 011 at z =2.36 is another source with a particularly large emission-line region (Fig. fig:maps ) extending over an area of 5.6 2 \ along the major and minor axis, respectively (corresponding to 41 kpc 14 kpc after deconvolving with the size of the seeing disk). [ OIII ] 4959,5007 and H line emission extends nearly along an axis going from south to north with a monotonically increasing velocity gradient of v= 685 km s ^ 1 . Line widths are FWHM =400-900 km s ^ 1 , except in a small region to the north-east of the nucleus where FWHMs up to 1300 km s ^ 1 are reached. All lines are well fit with single Gaussian components. The continuum is compact and approximately centered on the line-emitting clouds.

The [ NII ] 6583 /H ratio is fairly low, [ NII ] 6583 /H =0.17 0.02 . We also detect the [ SII ] 6716,6731 doublet at good S/N and identify each line of the doublet individually, but no [ OI ] 6300. USS 1410 011 has a very large radio source with LAS =25.2 significantly more extended than the emission-line gas (Fig. fig:mapuss1410big ). The northern radio source shows extended faint emission along an axis that is associated with the region of the most redshifted line emission in the northern emission-line region of USS 1410 011.

gmw_00001883.name: MRC 1558 003
gmw_00001883.overview: MRC 1558 003 is a radio galaxy at redshift z = 2.53 that anchors a protocluster [arXiv:1610.02057, arXiv:1805.06569]. It hosts a luminous X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1805.06569] surrounded by an extended emission-line nebulosity with complex gas kinematics [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: MRC 1558 003 shows broad nuclear H emission, extended line nebulosity centered on the nucleus, complex gas kinematics, broad line widths, and radio extension.

quotes.quote: MRC 1558 003 MRC 1558 003 at z = 2.53 is one of the 6 galaxies of nesvadba11a which have broad nuclear H line emission heralding a direct sight line into the AGN. We removed the nuclear point source before fitting the extended line emission ( ssec:blrremoval ). We find that the extended emission-line nebulosity is roughly centered on the nucleus with a size of 5.1 2.8 \ (41 kpc 22 kpc at z = 2.53 and after deconvolving with the seeing disk) along the major and minor axis with complex kinematics.

Relative velocities peak in a region south from the nucleus with 500 km s ^ 1 , blueshifts with up to 500 km s ^ 1 are found in the periphery of the emission-line gas. The largest widths are found along a ridge south of the nucleus with FWHM=1200 km s ^ 1 . Most line emission has widths of about FWHM = 700 900 km s ^ 1 . MRC 1558 003 is also extended in the radio, with LAS =7.7 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C-00.62 as the z=2.53 radio galaxy anchoring the protocluster, detects it as a luminous X-ray AGN, and shows how including it affects AGN-fraction estimates.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present a measurement of the X-ray AGN fraction in the \ protocluster associated with the z=2.53 radio galaxy 4C-00.62.
