At a 2 \ beam, intrinsic source sizes of unlensed galaxies would be at most 16 kpc, making it implausible to see a small group of high-redshift galaxies within a single beam.

Fig.  fig:decompir shows that we can rule out a bolometrically dominant AGN contribution from buried quasars, in all cases. Only three galaxies have best-fit results formally inconsistent with pure starbursts. In PLCK G092.5 + 42.9 and PLCK G244.8 + 54.9, the putative AGN contribution to the FIR luminosity is below 10 , and it is below 30 in PLCK G145.2 + 50.9. In all other cases, DecompIR finds the best fit for a pure starburst SED.

However, most sources are isolated enough to conclude that the radio counterpart is at the position of the high-redshift galaxy, and not coincident with a massive, intermediate-redshift galaxy that could host a radio-loud AGN. This is particularly the case for PLCK G244.8 + 54.9.

sources.summary: The paper discusses LPs-J105353 as a lensed Planck starburst with reconstructed CO structure and an intrinsic emitting radius consistent with its radiative-transfer model.

quotes.quote: We can further test the reliability of our Turbulence model if we consider two of the \ with estimates of the intrinsic, lens model reconstruction of the source size, based on high-angular resolution data: LPs-J105353 canameras2017b and LPs-J0209 geach2018, rivera2019 .

LPs-J105353 Also referred to as PLCK G244.8+54.9. is proposed to consist of two independent regions (roughly 1.5 kpc in length along the major axis) in the reconstructed source plane CO(4-3) line image canameras2017b , each separated by 500 pc, corresponding to an intrinsic emitting size radius of 2 kpc.

Our modelling would suggest an expected intrinsic emitting radius for LPs-J105353 of R_ eff / _ L = 14.5/ 26.8 3 kpc, which is consistent within the uncertainties considering both the lens model and our radiative transfer model.

gmw_00001919.name: NGC 157
gmw_00001919.overview: NGC 157 is a transition-object spiral galaxy located approximately 16.1 Mpc away [arXiv:2207.08013]. The galaxy features a weak central oval distortion [arXiv:1204.5599], a two-armed logarithmic spiral pattern [arXiv:1204.5599], and diffuse ionized gas associated with its H II regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0108135].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 157 to model diffuse ionized gas, combining an H-region catalogue, surface-density maps, and escape-fraction prescriptions to reproduce the galaxy's diffuse emission.

quotes.quote: A basic input element of any such model is a position and luminosity catalogue in \ of the \ regions in the galaxy under test, down to a low limiting luminosity, and we had previously produced a catalogue of this type for NGC 157. An initial family of models can then be generated in which the Lyc escaping from an \ region is parametrized in terms of the observed \ luminosity of the region and the escaping fluxes allowed through the diffuse disc gas. These models can then be refined using a measured map of \ surface density to effect the down-convertion of the Lyc to . For NGC 157 an \ map was available. Although its moderate angular resolution did limit the accuracy with which we could test our models, the predicted diffuse \ surface brightness distributions from our mo dels were compared with the observed distributions showing that, in general terms, the hypothesis of density bounding for the \ regions allows us to predict well the spatial distribution of the diffuse ionized gas.

In the model yielding the best fit to the data, the regions of lower luminosity lose a constant fraction of their ionizing flux to their surroundings, while for \ region luminosities above a specific transition va lue the ionizing escape fraction is a rising function of the \ luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 157 as a transition-object SAB(rs)bc galaxy, notes no clear AGN evidence, and says its emission-line ratios are also compatible with Seyferts and H II regions.

quotes.quote: In the case of the mini-DIVING ^ 3D sample, the nuclear emission-line spectra of four objects were classified as characteristic of transition objects: NGC 157, NGC 253, NGC 1808, and NGC 3585. NGC 157 is an SAB(rs)bc galaxy, at a distance of 16.1 Mpc. There is not much information in the literature about the central region of this galaxy, but, so far, no evidence of an AGN was detected (e.g. hug05 ).
Such a scenario, however, may not be the most likely one, specially in the case of NGC 157, due to the absence of clear evidence of an AGN in this object.
Similarly, at the 2 level, the emission line ratios of NGC 157 are compatible with those of Seyferts and H II regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 157 as having a weak central oval and a two-armed logarithmic spiral that peaks near 40 arcsec before giving way to a tighter outer pattern.

quotes.quote: NGC 157: The galaxy has a weak oval distortion at its center, which reaches to a radius of around 10 \ and has a relative amplitude of a_2 2 . There is a transition zone to 20 , where a two-armed, logarithmic spiral with a pitch angle of 33.4 \ starts. Its relative amplitude peaks around 40 in the range 30 << 50 . The main m=2 pattern terminates close to 60 , outside which a weaker, tighter pattern exists.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 157 to illustrate smoothing corrections for erratic interior isophote fits, producing a more rational ellipse solution and more stable intensities.

quotes.quote: An example of prf smooth 's corrections can be seen in Figure 4, the J image of NGC 157. Several interior ellipses display erratic behavior, but prf smooth took the mean average of nearby ellipses (in green) to produce a more rational fit. The resulting intensities were also more stable, although the RMS is going to be highly than the typical isophotes found in an elliptical.

gmw_00001920.name: NGC 3585
gmw_00001920.overview: NGC 3585 is an isolated E6 elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1212.1451, arXiv:2207.08013] that hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2207.08013]. The galaxy features a red central disk containing diffuse dust [arXiv:1212.1451, arXiv:1008.5149], a bimodal globular cluster color distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0505448], and a distinct lack of star formation [arXiv:1212.1451].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3585 as an isolated E6 elliptical with high rotation and asymmetric isophotes, a flat colour profile, little star formation, and a red central disc.

quotes.quote: Here we report on two interesting examples of isolated elliptical galaxies, namely NGC 3585 and NGC 5812. NGC 3585 is classified as E6 by and a high rotational velocity of at least , or even higher . Furthermore, it appears to have some asymmetry in its outer isophotes . Both of these properties are indicative of a recent merger, however, remnants of a merger have yet to be uncovered.

Two dimensional colour maps, and profiles, were produced and show a distinct lack of any star formation, or dust (except in the very centres of the galaxies), which seems to indicate no recent major merger activity. In addition, the very slight blue colour trend for the minor axis and red, 40'' diameter, disc in the core of NGC 3585, originally shown by , are both confirmed. Our colour profiles, and 2D colour maps, reveal a red discy region with a semi-major axis of 45'' , probably associated with diffuse dust, in the core of NGC 3585.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3585 as a transition-object galaxy, reports Chandra evidence for a low-luminosity AGN, and notes uncertain emission-line ratios.

quotes.quote: Finally, NGC 3585 is an E6 galaxy, at a distance of 17.3 Mpc. As in the case of NGC 157, there is not much information in the literature about the central region of this object. Using Chandra data, she17 found evidence of a low-luminosity AGN (LLAGN) in this galaxy.

The uncertainties of the [ O iii ] 5007/H ratios of NGC 157 and NGC 3585 are considerably high (see Fig. fig2 and Table tbl3 ). At the 1 or 2 levels, the emission line ratios of NGC 3585 are also compatible with those of LINERs and Seyferts.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3585 as an early-type host galaxy in its globular-cluster spectroscopy sample and reports a clearly bimodal globular-cluster colour distribution.

quotes.quote: The host galaxies (NGC 1380, NGC 2434, NGC 3115, NGC 3379, NGC 3585, NGC 5846, and NGC 7192) were selected to sample a significant range in environmental density at intermediate galaxy luminosity and velocity dispersion in the range 19.2 M_B 21.2 and 184 264 km s ^ 1 , respectively. All galaxies are of early type ( T<-2 ) according to the RC3 galaxy catalog RC3 . Our sample includes five elliptical and two lenticular galaxies (see Tab. tab:galdat ).
We find clear signs for bi-modality in the globular cluster colour distributions of NGC 1380, 3115, and 3585.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3585 as having a redder inner disk that implies dust, disky inner isophotes, and an inner disk within the dominant bulge that prevents reliable modeling there.

quotes.quote: The B-R colour image of NGC3585 shows that the inner disk is redder with respect to the bulge (see also Fisher et al. \ 1996), implying the presence of dust. Since the inner isophotes of NGC3585 are disky, suggesting the presence of a inner disk within the dominant bulge, we cannot model the underlying galaxy in that region by using our method.

gmw_00001921.name: IGR J1248.2--5828
gmw_00001921.overview: IGR J1248.2--5828 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert 1.9 galaxy [arXiv:1003.1214]. The galaxy is associated with a radio source and exhibits variable, absorbed X-ray emission that can vary by a factor of 2.5 over a few months [arXiv:1003.1214].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the hard X-ray counterpart of IGR J1248.2--5828 with a 2MASS extended source, radio emission, variable absorbed X-rays, and an optical Seyfert 1.9 galaxy classification.

quotes.quote: The only object still visible above 3 keV is source 3; it has a counterpart in a USNO--B1.0 source having magnitude R=14.6-15.1 , and it is also listed in the 2MASS Extended survey (2MASX J12475784--5829599). The X-ray position is also compatible with a radio source belonging to the MGPS--2 (Molonglo Galactic Plane Survey 2nd Epoch Compact Source, Murphy et al. 2007) catalogue, with a 36 cm flux of 16.5 1.1 mJy.

The X-ray spectrum of source 3 (see Figure fig2 , right panel) is fitted with a flat ( 0.9) slightly absorbed power law, having a 2--10 keV flux of 4 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (see Table Tab2 ). The fit is still acceptable if we fix the photon index to 1.8 and provides a high intrinsic absorption of 2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . This source shows a flux variability of a factor of 2.5 on a time-scale of a few months during XRT pointings. These properties suggest its associations with IGR J1248.2--5828 and, in particular, its characteristics in radio and infrared seem to indicate that we are dealing with an AGN. This suggestion has recently been confirmed by optical follow-up observations (Masetti et al. in preparation).

gmw_00001922.name: IGR J13107--5626
gmw_00001922.overview: IGR J13107--5626 is an unclassified galaxy associated with a 2MASS extended object [arXiv:1003.1214]. Multiwaveband characteristics, including a hard absorbed X-ray spectrum and a radio counterpart, suggest that it is an absorbed active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1003.1214].

sources.summary: The paper links IGR J13107--5626 to a 2MASS extended galaxy, radio counterpart, hard absorbed X-ray spectrum, and proposes it as an absorbed AGN.

quotes.quote: There is only one source detected in the whole XRT field of view, which is well located in the middle of the IBIS error circle (Bird et al. 2010). It has a counterpart in a 2MASS Extended object (2MASX J13103701--5626551) pointing to a galaxy still unclassified. It is also listed in the USNO--B1.0 catalogue with magnitude R=16.6-17.2 . The XRT position is compatible with a radio source belonging to the MGPS--2 catalogue with a 36 cm flux of 35.4 1.6 Jy. As shown in Figure fig3 (left panel), despite the partial overlap between the IBIS uncertainty and the BAT error circle of Swift J1312.1--5631, the 2MASS Extended object, proposed by Tueller et al. (2010) as the counterpart of the BAT source, is well located within the IBIS uncertainty, but it is 1^ .9 away from the border of the BAT error circle and 9^ .4 from the BAT centroid position.

If we fit the XRT spectrum (see Figure fig3 , right panel) with an absorbed power law by fixing the photon index to 1.8, we find an absorption in excess to the Galactic one of 4 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and a 2--10 keV flux of 1 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (see Table Tab2 ). Based on the multiwaveband characteristics, we propose for IGR J13107--5626 an absorbed AGN classification.

gmw_00001923.name: IGR J14080--3023
gmw_00001923.overview: IGR J14080--3023 is a type 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1502.06771, arXiv:0912.1519] classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1003.1214, arXiv:0912.1519] at a redshift of z = 0.024 [arXiv:1502.06771, arXiv:0912.1519]. The galaxy exhibits soft X-ray emission [arXiv:1003.1214] and displays flux variability over a timescale of a few months [arXiv:1003.1214, arXiv:0912.1519].

sources.summary: The paper monitors the Seyfert 1 AGN PGC50427, measuring BLR and dust-torus lags, estimating its black-hole mass, and placing the dust outside the BLR.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a three years monitoring campaigns of the z = 0.024 type-1 active galactic nucleus (AGN) PGC50427. Using robotic telescopes of the Universit atssternwarte Bochum near Cerro Armazones in Chile, we monitored PGC50427 in the optical and near-infrared (NIR). Through the use of Photometric Reverberation Mapping with broad and narrow band filters, we determine the size of the broad-line emitting region by measuring the time delay between the variability of the continuum and the H emission line. The H emission line responds to blue continuum variations with an average rest frame lag of 19.0 1.23 days. Using single epoch spectroscopy obtained with the Southern African Large Telescope (SALT) we determined a broad-line H velocity width of 1020 km s ^ 1 and in combination with the rest frame lag and adoption a geometric scaling factor f = 5.5 , we calculate a black hole mass of M_ BH 17 10^ 6 M_ .

We find that the hot dust emission ( 1800K ) lags the optical variations with an average rest frame lag of 46.2 2.60 days. The dust reverberation radius and the nuclear NIR luminosity permit us to derive the position of PGC50427 on the known M V diagram. The simultaneus observations for the broad-line region and dust thermal emission demonstrate that the innermost dust torus is located outside the BLR in PGC50427, supporting the unified scheme for AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper associates IGR J14080--3023 with a Seyfert 1.5 2MASS extended object and reports XRT/XMM fluxes, soft emission, variability, and AGN-compatible X-ray properties.

quotes.quote: This source recently appeared in the Swift /BAT survey of Cusumano et al. (2010) where it is associated with a 2MASS Extended object (2MASX J14080674--3023537) classified as a Seyfert 1.5 (V eron-Cetty V eron 2001). Indeed, the only source detected by XRT within the IBIS error circle (Bird et al. 2010), as shown in Figure fig4 , left panel, coincides with the infrared object, which has a counterpart in the USNO--B1.0 catalogue with magnitude R=13.7-14.2 and it is also listed in the XMM-Newton Slew Survey (XMMSL1 J140806.7--302348) with a 0.2--12 keV flux of 3.2 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , lower than the range measured during the XRT pointings ( ( 0.9-1.3) 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ) in the same energy band. The fit of the X-ray spectrum, in addition to the power law passing through Galactic absorption (Kalberla et al. 2005), requires a blackbody component to account for the presence of soft emission below 2 keV (see Figure fig4 , right panel and Table Tab2 ). This best-fit model provides a photon index 1.4 and a blackbody temperature kT 81 eV. No extra absorption in excess to the Galactic one is required by the data.

The source shows a flux variability of a factor of 1.3 with a time-scale of a few months, but no changes in the spectral shape are observed. The X-ray properties are compatible with the AGN classification proposed for this source.

sources.summary: The paper associates IGR J14080--3023 with a Seyfert galaxy, fits its X-ray spectrum with a soft component, and finds type 1 AGN characteristics.

quotes.quote: IGR J14080--3023 This source also appears in the recent Swift /BAT survey of Cusumano et al. (2009) where it is associated with a 2MASS Extended object (2MASX J14080674--3023537) classified as a Seyfert 1 at z=0.024 in SIMBAD. Indeed, the only object detected by XRT within the IBIS error circle (see Figure fig13 ), at 41 confidence level, coincides with the Seyfert galaxy. This source is reported in the USNO--B1.0 catalogue with magnitude R=13.72-14.17 and it is also listed in the XMM-Newton Slew Survey (XMMSL1 J140806.7--302348) with a positional uncertainty greater ( 8.8 arcsec) than that of XRT (see Table tab2 ) and a 0.2--12 keV flux of 3.2 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , lower than the range measured during the XRT pointings ( ( 0.9-1.3) 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ) in the same energy band. Here too a closer look at the literature (V eron-Cetty V eron 2001) indicates that the source is better classified as a Seyfert of intermediate (1.5) type.

The data do not require extra absorption in excess to the Galactic one. The source shows a slight flux variability (by a factor of 1.3), with a time-scale of a few months, but no variations in spectral shape are observed. The X-ray spectral characteristics are consistent with the AGN type 1 classification proposed for this source.

gmw_00001924.name: Bo otes II
gmw_00001924.overview: Boötes II is an old, very metal-poor ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1408.3628, arXiv:1510.05401] located approximately 42 to 60 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:1110.1086, arXiv:1510.05401]. It exhibits chemical enrichment typical of a very low-mass system [arXiv:1408.3628] and shows no detection of mass segregation [arXiv:2112.04689].

sources.summary: The paper applies DECam u-band metallicity selection to Boötes II, mapping candidate member stars and finding four northeastern large-radius candidates, with one promising spectroscopic target after CMD vetting.

quotes.quote: We conducted an in-depth analysis of candidate member stars located in the peripheries of three ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxy satellites of the Milky Way: Boötes I (Boo1), Boötes II (Boo2), and Segue I (Seg1). Studying these peripheral stars has previously been difficult due to contamination from the Milky Way foreground. We used u band photometry from the Dark Energy Camera (DECam) to derive metallicities to efficiently select UFD candidate member stars.

In Figure fig:spa_dist_all , we present the spatial distribution of candidate member stars in Boo2 that satisfy our isochrone, proper motion, and metallicity cuts. Most of our identified stars with m_g < 20 lie within 2 r_h of the system. For the sample brighter than m_g = 20.2 , we detect 4 stars with consistent proper motions in the DECam FOV north-east of the system at large radial distances. Three of them, indicated by hollow circles in Figure fig:spa_dist_all appear as outliers on the CMD despite passing our selection, so we flag them as unlikely members. The remaining candidate is a potential target for future spectroscopic follow-up studies.

sources.summary: The paper uses high-resolution spectroscopy and recalibrated CaT metallicities to confirm Bo otes II as an old, very metal-poor dwarf galaxy with typical low-mass enrichment rather than Sagittarius Stream debris.

quotes.quote: We present a chemical abundance study of the brightest confirmed member star of the ultrafaint dwarf galaxy Bo otes II from Keck/HIRES high-resolution spectroscopy at moderate signal-to-noise ratios. At [ Fe/H ] = 2.93 0.03 (stat.) 0.17 (sys.) this star chemically resembles metal-poor halo field stars and the signatures of other faint dwarf spheroidal galaxies at the same metallicities in that it shows enhanced [ /Fe ] ratios, Solar Fe-peak element abundances, and low upper limits on the neutron-capture element Ba. Moreover, this star shows no chemical peculiarities in any of the eight elements we were able to measure. This implies that the chemical outliers found in other systems remain outliers pertaining to the unusual enrichment histories of the respective environments, while Boo II appears to have experienced an enrichment history typical of its very low mass.

The resulting broad metallicity spread, in excess of one dex, the very metal poor mean, and the chemical abundance patterns of the present star imply that Bo otes II is a low-mass, old, metal poor dwarf galaxy and not an overdensity associated with the Sagittarius Stream as has been previously suggested based on its sky position and kinematics. The low, mean CaT metallicity of 2.7 dex falls right on the luminosity-metallicity relation delineated over four orders of magnitude from the more luminous to the faintest galaxies. Thus Bo otes II's chemical enrichment appears representative of the galaxy's original mass, while tidal stripping and other mass loss mechanisms were probably not significant as for other low-mass satellites.

sources.summary: The paper treats Bo otes II as a least-massive UFD prototype; low-SFE IGIMF models match stellar mass but fail abundance ratios and the observed MDF.

quotes.quote: We test the integrated galactic initial mass function (IGIMF) on the chemical evolution of 16 ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies discussing in detail the results obtained for three of them: Bo otes I, Bo otes II and Canes Venatici I, taken as prototypes of the smallest and the largest UFDs. These objects have very small stellar masses ( 10^3-10^4 M_ ) and quite low metallicities ( [ Fe/H ] <-1.0 dex). We consider four observational constraints: the present-day stellar mass, the [ /Fe ] vs. [ Fe/H ] relation, the stellar metallicity distribution function and the cumulative star formation history.

Bo otes II was discovered by as a resolved stellar overdensity in an automated search of the SDSS DR5 imaging data. The data samples of chemical abundances have been taken from and while for the construction of the MDF we added the data of .

The models adopting the IGIMF are able to reproduce the present-day stellar mass if low SFEs are assumed. Nevertheless, the same models are not able to fit either the abundance ratios or the observed MDF.

sources.summary: The paper uses Boo II as one of two example ultra-faint dwarfs, giving its luminosity, Galactic coordinates, half-light radius, distance, and large SDSS-based size uncertainty.

quotes.quote: To illustrate how our simulations can guide the data analysis for study of UFDs, we carry out mock observations for two example dwarf galaxies: Boo II M_ V =-2.7 0.9 , l=353^ .7 , b=68^ .8 ; martin08a and Coma Berenices ComBer, M_ V =-3.8 0.6 l=241^ .9 , b=83^ .6 ; munoz10a . The former is one of the smallest and faintest of the UFDs, while the latter is rather ``typical''.

From this catalog, Boo II has a measured half-light radius of 4.2 ^ +1.1 _ 1.4 and is located at a distance of 42 kpc. In terms of the variables studied by our simulations, this results in _ 0 3 , R_ 1/2 =0.035 and N_ T =3940 . The simulations show that this combination of parameters for Boo II yields a _ R_ 1/2 of roughly 30 (similar to the uncertainty reported by martin08a ) and a _ L 90 , indicating that the measurement based on SDSS data is highly inaccurate. It is worth pointing out that walsh08a , using deeper photometric observations of Boo II by roughly one magnitude, reported a R_ 1/2 =2.5 0.8 , nearly 40 smaller than the 4.2 found by martin08a , consistent with the uncertainty obtained from our simulations.

sources.summary: The paper reports Bootes II as a first-time weak gamma-ray-excess candidate, with low TS values; a dark-matter interpretation requires specified annihilation cross sections and remains inconclusive.

quotes.quote: Other sources having mild TS values in our analyses are Bootes II and Willman 1. These two dSphs are both for the first time being reported to have weak ray excesses. We generate residual TS maps of them, which are shown in Fig. fig:bs . Very weak excesses actually appear around the two sources, however no more conclusive information can be drawn from the TS maps.

If modeled as DM signals, for Bootes II, the largest TS value is about 6.4 (5.4) for ^ + ^ ( b b ) at m_ 14 GeV (100 GeV). To reproduce the signal, a v _ ^ + ^ 4.1 10 ^ 26 cm^3 s^ 1 ( v _ b b 1.9 10 ^ 25 cm^3 s^ 1 ) is needed adopting the empirical J-factor.

sources.summary: The paper treats Bo otes II as an ambiguous ultra-faint Milky Way companion at the dwarf galaxy/globular-cluster boundary, important for detection limits and extrapolated satellite census estimates.

quotes.quote: Willman 1, Segue 1 and Bo otes II willman1,quintet,boo2 occupy a region of size-luminosity space not previously known to be inhabited by old stellar populations, at the intersection of MW dSphs and globular clusters. Spectroscopic studies spectro showed that Willman 1 may be resident inside a dark matter subhalo with a mass-to-light ratio of 470 . If these ambiguous objects are gravitationally bound, then tidal arguments also favor them being dark matter dominated boot .
Objects with Segue 1, Bo otes II, or Willman 1-like luminosities would not have been detectable with scale sizes of 100 pc or larger, even at very nearby distances.

If we include the ambiguous objects Segue 1, Willman 1, and Bo otes II 1 in the calculation, then the R_ 90 ( R_ 99 ) DR6 estimate would be 56 ( 85 ) satellites only 15 of which are known, or 224 ( 340 ) across the sky. The ambiguity of Segue 1, Willman 1, and Bo otes II has considerable effect on the extrapolated MW census, underscoring the need for an understanding of these extremely faint systems.

sources.summary: BootesII was an ultra-faint dSph target whose uncertain stellar kinematics required estimated halo parameters, with a source included despite its poorly known stellar center.

quotes.quote: The targets included three classical dSphs (CDS), Carina, Fornax, and Sculptor, and three ultra-faint dSphs (UDS), BootesII, Hercules and Segue2, for a total of 123 hours of observing time.

In the case of BootesII, a robust determination of dynamical properties is hindered by the fact that only few tens of stars have been observed in its FoV , and the observed velocity dispersion is quite uncertain . Structural properties seem however to suggest a picture in between other two ultra-faint dSphs, Coma Berenices and WillmanI . For simplicity, and, in order to have an estimate of the order of magnitude for the expected signal, we compute _0 and r_0 for BootesII, by averaging the values of v_ max and r_ max of Coma Berenices and WillmanI in (which differ very little from each other).
In the case of the BootesII dSph, we include a source at a distance slightly larger than 2' , taking into account that the stellar population, and in turn its center, is currently poorly known.

sources.summary: The paper studies Bootes II as an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal, noting its old metal-poor population, 60 kpc distance, and two observed metal-poor member stars.

quotes.quote: This paper reports on the detailed abundance determination of stars belonging to the UfDSphs Bootes II, Canes Venatici I, Canes Venatici II, Hercules and Leo IV. Bootes II The discovery of Bootes II was reported by walsh2007 as an over density on the Sloan Digital Sky Data Release 5 ( hereafter SDSS DR5) distribution. From isochrone fitting techniques and accurate color-magnitude diagram, they described Bootes II as an old (12 Gr), metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] 2.00 dex) galaxy with a distance estimated at 60 kpc. Bootes II We have observed two stars in the Galaxy (BooII-7 and BooII-15) and found metallicities [ Fe/H ] = 2.98 dex and 3.08 dex.

sources.summary: The paper classifies Bo o tes II as a dwarf galaxy, citing its large half-light radius, very low metallicity, weak neutron-capture abundance, and lack of significant mass segregation.

quotes.quote: Bo o tes II was discovered in SDSS data by , who speculated that it was a dwarf galaxy based on its large half-light radius (about 70 pc). Spectroscopic observations by kochrich2014 and later found a very low metal abundance and a low fraction of neutron capture elements, in agreement with the assumption that Bo o tes II is a dwarf galaxy. Our results are also compatible with this assumption since we find R_ H,Bright /R_ H,Faint =0.95 0.07 , i.e. no significant detection of mass segregation. We conclude that Bo o tes II is a dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents Boo II as a faint, ambiguous companion to Boo I, giving its luminosity, half-light radius, separation, and outer-isophote ellipticity evidence.

quotes.quote: For example, Boo I and Boo II appear to have similar distances and are located at a projected separation of only 1.8 kpc wal07 . Are these two objects companion galaxies, or is Boo II (M _ v = -3.1, R _ h =72 pc) a globular cluster companion to Boo I (M _ v = -5.7, R _ h = 227 pc)? Inspection of the outer isophotes of Boo II published by wal07 suggests that 0.1 (a-b)/a 0.2, which is consistent with its being either a galaxy or a cluster.

gmw_00001925.name: IGR J16385-2057
gmw_00001925.overview: IGR J16385-2057 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galaxy [arXiv:1107.5155, arXiv:0806.4824] that hosts a water megamaser [arXiv:1107.5155, arXiv:2006.08280]. It exhibits radio emission at its optical peak [arXiv:1107.5155] and displays variable ionized iron lines in its X-ray spectrum [arXiv:1106.3023].

sources.summary: The paper reports IGR J16385-2057 as a newly detected maser NLS1 galaxy, with NVSS radio emission at the optical peak and requested follow-up observations.

quotes.quote: We discovered maser emission in two NLS1 galaxies: IGR J16385-2057, and IRAS 03450+0055. In addition to the three previously known maser detections in the NLS1s Mrk 766, NGC 4051, and NGC 5506, this yields a water maser detection rate in NLS1 galaxies of 7 (5/71).

IGR J16385 For IGR J16385, only the 1.4 GHz radio continuum map from the National Radio Astronomy Observatory VLA Sky Survey (NVSS) at 1.4 GHz is available. The NVSS map shows a radio source of 10 mJy at the position of the optical peak of the galaxy. Expanded VLA (EVLA) B-array observations of the radio continuum (X-Band) and maser line (K-Band) in IGR J16385 have been requested and await evaluation.

sources.summary: The paper reports a new GBT water maser detection in the NLSy1 galaxy IGR J16385-2057 and uses it as part of evidence that NLSy1 AGN can have high maser detection rates.

quotes.quote: During our survey, a new water maser was detected with the GBT in the narrow line Seyfert 1 (NLSy1) galaxy IGR J16385-2057 on March 28, 2010. This discovery, and hence the line profile and the characteristics of the water maser emission, has been anticipated in a previous paper by our team, which was focused on water maser emission in NLSy1 galaxies tarchi11b . Here, we report in Table 1A the isotropic line luminosity.

Our sample includes 9 NLSy1 with 22 GHz measurements and 3 water maser detections (NGC 4051, Mkn 766, and IGR J16385-2057), which implies rates close to 30 . This confirms previous results obtained by Tarchi et al. ( tarchi11b ) in a dedicated study of this type of AGN, in which the authors suggested an outflow origin for water maser emission.

sources.summary: The paper classifies IGR J16385-2057 as a bona-fide NLS1 and reports radio emission, excess X-ray absorption, and high-energy cutoff/reflection evidence.

quotes.quote: Overall we can conclude saying that 3 objects (IGR J16385-2057, IGR J19378 and Swift J21247.4+5654) in our sample are bona-fide NLS1 while the remaining 2 can be considered at this stage only NLS1 candidates with IGR J14552-5133 probably a more convincing case than IGR J16185-5928. Clearly more detailed optical spectroscopy is necessary and encouraged to refine and confirm the optical classification of these last two objects.
IGR J16385-2057 is the only source which requires absorption in excess to the Galactic value at a level of 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 .

From this analysis we found that a high energy cut-off and a reflection hump is required by the data in IGR J16385-2057 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654 at 99.99 and 98 /97 respectively; the cutoff lies in the range 23-52 keV while the broken power law break occurs at at around 30 keV (see Table 4) as expected in the presence of the a reflection component.

sources.summary: The paper describes IGR J16385-2057 as having a steep hard X-ray slope, variable ionized Fe lines, and soft-energy spectral variability.

quotes.quote: The optical classification of IGR J14552-5133, IGR J16185-5928, IGR J16385-2057, IGR J19378-0617 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654 has been extensively discussed in Malizia et al.

In Mrk 783, IRAS 15091-2107 and in IGR J16385-2057 the hard X-ray photon index is much steeper than the XRT and XMM- Newton one, however the cut-off energy measured from the broad-band analysis would be physically unexpected (E _ cut-off 20 keV).

In IRAS 15091-2107 the line profile is consistent with a moderately broad Gaussian component, while between the two XMM- Newton observations of IGR J16385-2057 the line has varied its energy; also, marginal evidence for a narrow line at 7 keV (EW = 60 ^ +50 _ 40 eV) in both observations suggest the presence of variable ionized Fe lines in this source.

sources.summary: J1638 is classified as complex despite subtler features, with prior modeling using continuum, soft plasma emission, multiblackbody emission, and two iron K-shell lines.

quotes.quote: J1638 (IRAS 16355-2049) The spectral fitting of J1638 gives a ^2_ = 1.20 using a simple power-law between 2-12 keV. Even though the complex feature is not as evident as in some well-know C-NLS1s, considering the confidence level of the null hypothesis, we classified this source as a C-NLS1. This is in agreement with what was found in Panessa2011 , who modeled the combined XMM-Newton EPIC-PN (0.3-10.0 keV) and INTEGRAL IBIS (20-100 keV) spectra of J1638 by a power-law continuum, a soft emission from plasmas, a multi-blackbody component, and two iron K-shell emission lines.

gmw_00001926.name: PKS 0558--504
gmw_00001926.overview: PKS 0558--504 is a highly variable, radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1305.3573, arXiv:2308.08528]. The galaxy features a two-sided radio jet structure [arXiv:2308.08528] and exhibits strong, rapid variability across the optical, ultraviolet, and X-ray bands [arXiv:1305.3573, arXiv:1301.5785].

sources.summary: The paper monitors PKS 0558-504 across optical, UV, and X-ray bands, linking its strong variability to accretion-flow propagation and soft-excess seed photons.

quotes.quote: PKS 0558­-504 is a highly variable, X-ray bright, radio-loud, Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 galaxy with super-Eddington accretion rate and extended jets that do not dominate the emission beyond the radio band. Therefore, this source represents an ideal laboratory to shed some light on the central engine in highly accreting systems and specifically on the link between accretion disc and corona. Here we present the results from a 1.5 year monitoring with \ XRT and UVOT.

The simultaneous coverage at several wavelengths confirms that PKS 0558­-504 is highly variable in any band from optical, to UV and X-rays, with the latter showing the largest amplitude changes but with the UV emission dominating the radiative output. A cross-correlation analysis reveals a tight link between the emission in the optical and UV bands and provides suggestive evidence in favor of a scenario where the variability originates in the outer part of the accretion flow and propagates inwards before triggering the activity of the X-ray emitting corona. Finally, a positive correlation between the soft X-ray flux and the hard photon index suggests that in PKS 0558­-504 the seed photons are provided to the corona by the soft excess component.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 0558--504 as a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1, finding a soft X-ray excess, hot Comptonising corona, and spin implications that disfavor large disk truncation or retrograde spin.

quotes.quote: Here we study the soft X-ray excess, the hard X-ray spectrum and the optical/UV emission from the radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy PKS 0558--504 using and observations. The broadband X-ray continuum of PKS 0558--504 consists of a soft X-ray excess emission below 2 that is well described by a blackbody ( kT 0.13 ) and high energy emission that is well described by a thermal Comptonisation (compps) model with kT_e 250 , optical depth 0.05 (spherical corona) or kT_e 90 , 0.5 (slab corona). The Comptonising corona in PKS 0558--504 is likely hotter than in radio-quiet Seyferts such as IC 4329A and Swift J2127.4+5654.

The observed soft X-ray excess can be modeled as blurred reflection from an ionised accretion disk or optically thick thermal Comptonisation in a low temperature plasma. Both the soft X-ray excess emission when interpreted as the blurred reflection and the optical/UV to soft X-ray emission interpreted as intrinsic disk Comptonised emission implies spinning ( a>0.6 ) black hole. These results suggest that disk truncation at large radii and retrograde black hole spin both are unlikely to be the necessary conditions for launching the jets.

sources.summary: PKS 0558-504 resembles Ark 564 in its three PCA components, including low and high energy suppression, steepening with flux, and standard NLS1-like variability despite radio loudness.

quotes.quote: PKS 0558-504 PKS 0558-504 shows very similar variability to Ark 564, producing three components which show the same features. The first PC is suppressed at both low and high energies, and the second shows the same steepening with flux. The minimum of the third component appears to be at a lower energy than in Ark 564, but it shows the same correlation between low and high energy bins. This object is radio loud, which indicates the presence of a jet. However, its spectrum and variability appear to be fairly standard for a NLS1 papadakis10 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0558--504 as a local active galaxy in its bare-AGN sample and analyzes XMM-Newton/Swift X-ray spectra, including continuum, soft-excess, and spectral-fitting behavior.

quotes.quote: PKS 0558--504 (z=0.137) is a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 AGN whose spectrum is similar to that of radio-quiet narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxies Komossa2006 . Radio observations (VLA) of the AGN revealed the two-sided radio structure Doi2012 . Multiwavelength observations constrained the central black hole mass to 2.5 10^8 M _ Gliozzi2010 . From the spectral energy distribution (SED) of PKS 0550-504, it was reported that the SED is mostly dominated by optical-UV photons and the jet emission does not dominate beyond the radio band Gliozzi2010 . PKS 0558--504 was observed with XMM-Newton and Swift /XRT multiple times from 2000 to 2016 (see Table table:obs_log ).

sources.summary: The review describes PKS 0558 504 as the first radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1, with narrow hydrogen lines, rapid X-ray variability, and evidence for relativistic beaming.

quotes.quote: The first RLNLS1 -- PKS 0558 504 ( z=0.137 ) -- was discovered in 1986 by Remillard et al. during the identification of eight sources detected by HEAO 1 . It was not yet recognized as NLS1 (the seminal paper by Osterbrock Pogge was published just the previous year), but they wrote: ``One of the I Zw 1 types, the previously unidentified radio source PKS 0558-504, is a QSO with unusually narrow hydrogen lines for a high-luminosity object ( M_ v =-25.1 ).'' Later, again Remillard et al. , reported strong and rapidly variable X-ray emission ( +67 in 3 minutes) from the same source detected by Ginga . The measured energies and time scales required relativistic beaming to be reasonably explained.

sources.summary: The catalogue paper identifies PKS 0558-504 as the most variable DR1 AGN, showing a roughly threefold count-rate drop over about 14 hours without violating the efficiency limit.

quotes.quote: The DR1 light curves for PKS 0558 504 and PKS 0447 439 are shown in Fig. fig:AGN_DR1_e1-e5 . The most variable AGN is PKS 0558-504 with an AMPLMAX value of 3.45 2.43 \ and an NEV value of 1.97 0.03 \ . The source shows a drop in count rate by a factor of about 3 within about 14 hours typical for BL Lac sources. The efficiency limit was not violated during the eRASS1 observations.

sources.summary: The paper presents year-long RXTE monitoring of PKS 0558--504, finding strong week-to-month X-ray flux changes, spectral variability, and occasional rapid brightening events.

quotes.quote: We present X-ray observations of the Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 galaxy PKS 0558--504 obtained with \ during a 1-year monitoring campaign. This source, which is one of the very few radio-loud NLS1 galaxies, shows strong X-ray flux variability on time scales of weeks-months accompanied by spectral variability. The main goal of this study is to investigate the spectral variability with model-independent methods and time-resolved spectroscopy in order to shed light on the origin of the X-rays. The main results can be summarized as follows: 1) The flux typically changes by a factor of 1.5-2 on time scales of 10-30 days, with few extreme events where the flux increases by a factor of 4 in 3 days.

gmw_00001927.name: GS30668
gmw_00001927.overview: GS30668 is a high-redshift star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1611.07038] and a likely Lyman continuum leaker [arXiv:2110.11961]. It shows no signs of active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1611.07038] and is located within the star-forming region of the mass-excitation diagram [arXiv:1611.07038].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GS30668 as a strong star-forming LyC emitter candidate, not detected in Chandra, within the star-forming excitation region, and similar to Ion2 in high [O III] strength.

quotes.quote: The first three of our candidates are the most convincing as they show no signs of AGN activity and no nearby, potentially contaminating galaxies. These are GS30668, GS35257, and GS14633. These sources are not detected in the deepest available Chandra GOODS images and associated CANDELS-matched catalogs that include even extremely faint X-ray sources Cappelluti16 . For these three source we can further examine the Mass-Excitation diagram Juneau14 , since reliable [ O 3 ] and H measurements are available. GS30668 and GS14633 are within the star-forming region of the diagram while GS35257 lies on the border separating AGN and star-forming galaxies.

Ion2 and GS30668 share many remarkable similarities. Both display an extreme EW( [ O 3 ] _ 4959+5007 + H ) ( Ion2 : 1600 ; GS30668: 1350 ) and [ O 3 ] / [ O 2 ] 10 . Even the multi-band fitted _ UV ( Ion2:-2.2 0.2 ; GS30668: 2.2 0.1 ), EW( H ) ( Ion2 :112 60 ; GS30668:130 45 ) and E(B-V) =0 for these sources resemble each other.

sources.summary: The paper treats XLS-26/GS-30668 as an archetypal high-escape LyC leaker, noting secure systemic redshift measurements and prior direct LyC imaging that left it awaiting spectroscopic follow-up.

quotes.quote: XLS-26 (Figure fig:XLSfesc ) is the archetype of such sources. We emphasize again that the systemic redshifts for all our selected sources are highly secure -- e.g., for XLS-26 the z_ sys is confirmed with several lines across multiple X-SHOOTER arms (H , H , 4960,5008 , , O iii ] 1666 ).

Supporting this interpretation, we note that XLS-26 was identified as a likely LyC leaker with f_ esc =60^ +40 _ 38 ``GS-30668" in Naidu17 with direct LyC imaging from the Hubble Deep UV Survey ( Oesch18b , which also incorporates earlier UV imaging from Rafelski15 ). Since its was based on a probabilistic method (similar to the search that yielded Ion2, Vanzella15 ), GS-30668 was presented as a likely candidate pending spectroscopic follow-up.

gmw_00001928.name: GS35257
gmw_00001928.overview: GS35257 is a high-redshift galaxy identified as a convincing Lyman continuum emitter candidate [arXiv:1611.07038]. The galaxy shows no signs of active galactic nucleus activity, lying instead on the boundary separating active galactic nuclei and star-forming galaxies [arXiv:1611.07038].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GS35257 as a convincing LyC emitter candidate without AGN signs or contaminating neighbors, undetected in Chandra and lying on the AGN/star-forming boundary.

quotes.quote: The first three of our candidates are the most convincing as they show no signs of AGN activity and no nearby, potentially contaminating galaxies. These are GS30668, GS35257, and GS14633. These sources are not detected in the deepest available Chandra GOODS images and associated CANDELS-matched catalogs that include even extremely faint X-ray sources Cappelluti16 . For these three source we can further examine the Mass-Excitation diagram Juneau14 , since reliable [ O 3 ] and H measurements are available. GS30668 and GS14633 are within the star-forming region of the diagram while GS35257 lies on the border separating AGN and star-forming galaxies.

gmw_00001929.name: GS14633
gmw_00001929.overview: GS14633 is a star-forming galaxy that shows no signs of active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1611.07038]. It is identified as a Lyman continuum emitter candidate with an estimated escape fraction of approximately 62 percent [arXiv:1611.07038].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GS14633 as a convincing star-forming LyC emitter candidate, undetected in Chandra, in the star-forming excitation region, with an estimated escape fraction near 62%.

quotes.quote: The first three of our candidates are the most convincing as they show no signs of AGN activity and no nearby, potentially contaminating galaxies. These are GS30668, GS35257, and GS14633. These sources are not detected in the deepest available Chandra GOODS images and associated CANDELS-matched catalogs that include even extremely faint X-ray sources Cappelluti16 . For these three source we can further examine the Mass-Excitation diagram Juneau14 , since reliable [ O 3 ] and H measurements are available. GS30668 and GS14633 are within the star-forming region of the diagram while GS35257 lies on the border separating AGN and star-forming galaxies.
Of our star-forming candidates, [ O 3 ] / [ O 2 ] is available for GS30668 and GS14633, and they have f_ esc of 60_ 38 ^ +40 and 62_ +38 ^ 51 respectively.

gmw_00001930.name: GN21231
gmw_00001930.overview: GN21231 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of 2.004 [arXiv:1504.03329] that is heavily obscured by a large column of dust [arXiv:1504.03329, arXiv:1611.07038]. While its central nucleus is dusty, the rest of the galaxy is not, and its ionizing flux likely originates from extended star-forming regions [arXiv:1611.07038].

sources.summary: XID 243N has a low-count apparently unabsorbed spectrum, but its X-ray luminosity is strongly suppressed relative to MIR emission; the authors favor heavy obscuration.

quotes.quote: XID 243N: The spectrum of this z=2.004 source has only 12 net counts ( 0.5-8  keV) therefore a more complex model than a simple power law cannot be fit to the data. The effective photon index measured from the spectrum is =1.86^ +1.29 _ 0.87 . Fitting the spectrum with an absorbed power law with =1.8 fixed to try to constrain the level of absorption results in an upper limit of <8 10^ 22  cm ^ 2 , which is consistent with the source being unabsorbed. However, the X-ray luminosity of XID 243N is very low compared to the 6  m luminosity measured from its IR SED (see Table tab1 ) and the typical L_ X L_ 6 m relations (e.g., lutz2004,fiore2009,mateos2015 ). The observed X-ray luminosity is consistent with a suppression of a factor of 300 with respect to the intrinsic luminosity measured in the MIR (see Table tab4 ), and still more than a factor of 120 considering the intrinsic scatter of the L_ X L_ 6 m relation ( 0.35  dex; e.g., mateos2015 ).

Another possibility is that this source is intrinsically X-ray weak, as it has been found, for instance, for broad absorption line (BAL) quasars (see e.g., luo2014 ). However, XID 243N has never been identified as a BAL quasar to date. Although the hypothesis of intrinsically weak X-ray emission cannot be ruled out for this quasar, we favour here the heavy-obscuration interpretation (see Sect.

sources.summary: The paper treats GN21231 as a known, heavily obscured AGN where detected LyC flux likely comes from extended star-forming regions rather than the dusty central nucleus.

quotes.quote: GN21231 was identified as an AGN by Kirkpatrick12 and was found to display the distinctive 9.7 m silicate line in its IR spectrum, which indicates a large column of dust along the line of sight. This massive amount of dust is perhaps why GN21231 is heavily obscured and not detected in Chandra images. The dust probed by the 9.7 m line is concentrated in the innermost, central 2pc of the galaxy Kohler10 , however. It is thus fair to conjecture that the ionizing flux from the active nucleus in GN21231 is largely suppressed by the dust torus around it. Thus, any LyC flux we detect probably originates from the star forming parts of the galaxy.

This hypothesis is supported by our FAST SED fit which yields E(B-V) =0 , indicating that while the central nucleus may be dusty, the rest of the galaxy is not. The morphology of the ionizing radiation in the F275W filter, which is extended and not concentrated on a central point-source, is another indication that the LyC flux originates from stars.

gmw_00001931.name: GN19591
gmw_00001931.overview: GN19591 is classified as a likely obscured active galactic nucleus based on its infrared emission [arXiv:1611.07038]. However, its spectral features and extended Lyman continuum morphology suggest that star-forming regions are responsible for its ionizing flux [arXiv:1611.07038].

sources.summary: The paper classifies GN19591 as a likely obscured AGN, but notes star-forming spectral features and extended LyC morphology suggesting its ionizing flux comes from star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: In addition to them, we classify GN19591 as a likely AGN based on a strong Spitzer /MIPS and even a Herschel detection, and the hint of a power law SED across the Spitzer /IRAC bands. However, we note that GN19591 is not detected in the Chandra Deep Field North (2 Msec) images, and has spectral features that could easily belong to a star-forming galaxy (e.g. narrow width of the O 3 line, [ O 3 ] / [ O 2 ] 2.2, [ O 3 ] 5007 / H _ 3.2 ).

Similar to GN21231, GN19591 which is not detected in Chandra 2 Msec images might host a heavily obscured AGN. Based on the extended, resolved LyC radiation in the F275W image, it is likely that star-forming regions are responsible for the ionizing flux, however. The similarity of morphologies in the F336W and F275W images is further evidence of the star-forming regions being correlated with LyC emission.

gmw_00001932.name: GN19913
gmw_00001932.overview: GN19913 is a face-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of approximately z=2.01 [arXiv:astro-ph/0105043, arXiv:1504.06948] that is classified as a merger event with a double nucleus [arXiv:1003.3252]. The system hosts an obscured type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1504.06948] and is associated with a submillimeter source [arXiv:1007.0350].

sources.summary: The paper treats SMMJ123635.59+621424.1 as a merger with a double nucleus, compact bulge-like measurements, unresolved NICMOS structure, faint outer light, and possible spiral structure over about 5 kpc.

quotes.quote: In the case of system SMMJ123635.59+621424.1 the strong disagreement is due to the fact that in the z-band we observe a multicomponent system, made up of a compact bulge with a double nucleus, plus faint spiral arms on larger scales. Since the spiral arms are too faint to be fit with a single S ersic model we have measured the compactness of the bulge. Rather, in the NICMOS H-band the multicomponent structure is not resolved, and fits to the system reveal a much more extended profile.

A peculiar case is represented by the system SMMJ123635.59+621424.1. We classify it as a merger event, as it appears as a double-nucleus system. However, its effective radius and S ersic index are characteristic of those for compact galaxies. It is likely that we are measuring the compactness of the bulge component, given the faintness of the outer light. This systems also shows faint signs of potential spiral structure over a scale of 5 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies CXOHDFN J123635.6 + 621424 as a z=2.011 face-on spiral X-ray/radio source whose optical and X-ray properties indicate AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We present the first published optical spectrum and redshift for CXOHDFN J123635.6 + 621424, a well--observed X--ray source identified with a face--on spiral galaxy at z = 2.011 , fortuitously located in the Inner West HDF Flanking Field. CXOHDFN J123635.6 + 621424 was first detected as a weak radio source (8.15 Jy at 8.5 GHz; 87.8 Jy at 1.4 GHz) in the sensitive HDF radio surveys of richards98, richards00 . The source has a comparatively steep radio spectral index ( S_ ^ ; ^ 8.4 GHz _ 1.4 GHz > 0.87 ), and the radio emission extends across 2 8.

In contradistinction to the foregoing conclusions, both the optical and X--ray properties of CXOHDFN J123635.6 + 621424 indicate the presence of AGN activity. The optical spectrum shows moderate--width ( 1000 km/s), high--ionization emission lines, similar to those of the recently reported quasar II in the Chandra Deep Field South norman01 and typical of high--redshift radio galaxies cf. mccarthy93, stern99 .
Finally, CXO observations of the source indicate a comparatively hard X--ray spectrum --- the definitive signature of an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HDFX 28 as a face-on spiral with an obscured type 2 AGN, then models its line ratios and continuum with starburst, AGN, and shock alternatives.

quotes.quote: In search for type 2 QSRs, HDFX 28 was identified as a face-on spiral galaxy at z=2.011. Initially, it was considered as an extended radio source powered by star formation (Richards 2000), but the hard X-ray source and the spectroscopy demonstrated that this galaxy contains an obscured type 2 AGN (Dawson et al and references therein). The galaxy was observed by ISOCAM and Chandra. Table tab4 shows that a SB dominated model (MDS) roughly reproduces the data. Dawson et al spectrum contains too few narrow lines, [ NII ] 6548, 6548 and to provide a consistent modelling of the low gas.

sources.summary: The paper treats CDFN-190 as an X-ray DOG and Compton-thick candidate, citing its flat spectrum, example X-ray spectrum, submillimeter association, z=2.005 redshift, and FeK line.

quotes.quote: There are four sources which prefer a very flat power-law index ( _1 < 1 at the 90 confidence level) instead of intrinsic absorption: 92, 190, 423 in CDF-N, and 346 in CDF-S. This flat spectrum is consistent with a reflection dominated X-ray emission (e.g. Matt et al. 2004, Akylas Georgantopoulos 2009) and therefore these sources can be considered as Compton-thick candidates. We present an example X-ray spectrum (CDFN-190) in Fig. xspectra .
CDFN-190 which is associated with a sub-mm at a spectroscopic redshift of z=2.005 (Chapman et al. 2005), has a detected FeK _ line with a rest-frame EW of 0.7 keV.

sources.summary: The paper identifies GN19913 as a known AGN whose UV morphology is centrally concentrated, making its ionizing photons likely AGN-origin and unsuitable for stellar LyC escape studies.

quotes.quote: In contrast, the situation in the AGN GN19913 is clearer, and indicates that it is not useful for a study of LyC escape from stars. The UV morphology of GN19913 is concentrated on a central point source consistent with the ionizing photons originating from the AGN.
The two confirmed AGN in our sample show particularly high f_ esc (GN21231 >71 [ 10^ th percentile ] ; GN19913 100 ), with the caveat that we have not included AGN templates in deriving these values.

gmw_00001933.name: IC1182
gmw_00001933.overview: IC1182 is a morphologically peculiar, high-luminosity starburst galaxy located in the Hercules supercluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0410357, arXiv:astro-ph/0402219]. It is an ongoing merger of two disk galaxies, featuring a heavily obscured disk-like structure, central knots, and two slender tidal tails [arXiv:astro-ph/0402219].

sources.summary: The paper presents imaging and spectroscopy of Mkn 298, describing a peculiar merging system whose nucleus has mixed line ratios best modeled as starburst photoionization plus shocks rather than an active nucleus.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopic and imaging data of the peculiar galaxy Mkn 298 are presented in this paper. Narrow-band H and broad-band R images are used to study the star formation rate in the galaxy and its morphology, which is typical of a merging system. Long-slit and integral field spectra are used to assess the kinematics of gas and stars, and the nature of the ionizing source at different distances from the nucleus.

In particular, the nucleus of Mkn 298 is characterized by peculiar line ratios: [ N ii ] 6583 / H is typical of H ii like regions, while [ O i ] 6300 / H could indicate the presence of an active galactic nucleus. We show that models where a shock component is added to photoionization from a starburst allow to reproduce the observed line ratios. Mkn 298 is thus most likely a star forming galaxy, rather than a galaxy hosting an active nucleus.
Mkn 298 (IC 1182) is known as a morphologically peculiar system (Figure fig:bim ) located in the Hercules supercluster.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 1182 as a distorted, high-luminosity starburst with central knots, extended tidal material, and two recognizable disk galaxies in an ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: The analysis of the broad band images reveals a distorted morphology with a large, heavily obscured disk-like structure and several knots in the central region. Galactic material, some of it in the form of two slender tails, is detected well beyond the main body of the galaxy. The second, fainter tail and several knots are reported here for the first time.

The data presented here indicate that IC 1182 is a high luminosity starburst system. Its global properties and peculiarities can be understood as corresponding to two systems that can still be recognized, in the process of merging, with two tidal tails emerging from the central region of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 1182 as a Hercules-cluster merger whose tidal-tail kinematics may involve projection effects, while observed 50-100 km/s gradients suggest local gravity.

quotes.quote: We present here our results for three systems (more in Bournaud et al., 2004) where the presence of massive TDGs had previously been claimed: IC 1182, a merger in the Hercules cluster (see Fig. 2 and van Driel et al., in these proceedings), Arp 105 (NGC 3561, ``The guitar"), an interacting system between a spiral and an elliptical (see Fig. 3) and Arp 242 (NGC 4676, ``The Mice"), an interacting system between two spirals with prominent tidal tails (see Fig. 4, and Hibbard et al., in this volume). The tail of one system, IC 1182, shows a large-scale kinematics suggesting that projection effects may play a role, even though the presence of a genuine accumulation of matter, corresponding to a TDG, cannot be excluded.

Velocity gradients of typically 50--100 km/s were observed in the tails of Arp 105, IC 1182, and NGC 5291. We best explained them by the local effect of gravity. Because of the higher spectral resolution of the FP instrument, the gradients are generally smaller than those initially measured in earlier observations with long slit spectroscopy.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC1182 as an H-alpha-selected A2151 galaxy and AGN candidate, noting diagnostic-line placement, broad Balmer emission, X-ray reporting, and literature arguments against active nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: A total of five galaxies lie clearly above the sequence of Kauffmann et al. -- KUG1602+175 ( 9), NGC6050 ( 21), IC1182 ( 39), PGC057064 ( 29) and KUG1603+179A ( 33) -- and two of them only marginally -- UGC10190 ( 27) and SDSSJ160531.84+174826.1 ( 34) --. Also, broad Balmer emission lines are apparent in IC1182 ( 39) and PGC057064 ( 29). In addition IC1182 ( 39) and SDSSJ160531.84+174826.1 ( 34) are reported to be X-ray sources in NED, which could be indicative of the presence of an AGN.

IC1182 ( 39) was studied in detail by Moles et al. (2004) and they concluded that although the line ratio diagnostics are not conclusive about the nature of IC1182 ( 39), they discard the active nature of the nuclear emission of this galaxy based on other criteria.

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 1182 has an eastern-tail TDG candidate whose kinematics are compatible with projection effects, so the apparent condensation may not be a genuine massive object.

quotes.quote: IC 1182: The presence of a TDG candidate, ce-61, is reported at the extremity of the eastern tail of IC 1182 (Braine et al. 2001; Iglesias-P aramo et al. 2003). The kinematical data for this tail are shown in Fig. PV1182 . The projected velocity first rises away from the IC 1182 center with an amplitude of 130 km s ^ 1 , then decreases by 40--50 km s ^ 1 near its extremity. This kinematical signature corresponds to case (b) in Fig. schema2 , indicating that the end of the tail may be aligned with the line-of-sight.

gmw_00001934.name: PGC057064
gmw_00001934.overview: PGC057064 is an H-alpha-selected galaxy located in the A2151 cluster [arXiv:0907.1370]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus candidate with broad Balmer emission lines, and its disturbed morphology suggests it is undergoing galaxy interactions [arXiv:0907.1370].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC057064 as an H-alpha-selected A2151 galaxy, an AGN candidate with broad Balmer emission, and a possible interacting galaxy due to disturbed morphology and proximity to cluster members.

quotes.quote: A total of five galaxies lie clearly above the sequence of Kauffmann et al. -- KUG1602+175 ( 9), NGC6050 ( 21), IC1182 ( 39), PGC057064 ( 29) and KUG1603+179A ( 33) -- and two of them only marginally -- UGC10190 ( 27) and SDSSJ160531.84+174826.1 ( 34) --. Also, broad Balmer emission lines are apparent in IC1182 ( 39) and PGC057064 ( 29).

Some galaxies can be associated to galaxy interactions because of their disturbed morphologies and their close projected distance to other cluster members: NGC6045 ( 13), SDSSJ160508.81+174545.3 ( 14), NGC6050 ( 21), SDSSJ160305.24+171136.1 ( 4), SDSSJ160304.20+171126.7 ( 5), PGC057064 ( 29), LEDA084724 ( 44), SDSSJ160547.17+173501.6 ( 45).

gmw_00001935.name: LEDA 1543586
gmw_00001935.overview: LEDA 1543586 is a candidate for star formation induced by the ram pressure of the intergalactic medium [arXiv:0907.1370]. The galaxy exhibits asymmetric H-alpha emission, with most emission knots concentrated on the side facing the cluster center [arXiv:0907.1370].

sources.summary: The paper describes LEDA 1543586 as a candidate for ram-pressure-induced star formation, with asymmetric H-alpha emission concentrated toward the cluster center.

quotes.quote: Finally, there is a candidate of star formation induced by the ram pressure exerted by the IGM: LEDA 1543586 ( 15). The H emission of this galaxy is clearly assymetric (see Figure 5) with most knots in the side facing the cluster center and almost no emission in the opposite side. This pattern of H emission has been already observed in cluster galaxies (Gavazzi et al. 2001) and is associated with the ram pressure exerted by the intergalactic medium on the interstellar medium of disk galaxies.

gmw_00001936.name: SDSS J122907.3+020246
gmw_00001936.overview: SDSS J122907.3+020246 was previously identified as a hyper-luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0611755]. However, this classification was a mismatch, as the galaxy lies outside the error ellipse of the infrared source F12265+0219 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611755].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J122907.3+020246 as a problematic HLIRG identification, showing it lies outside the IRAS error ellipse and was mismatched to F12265+0219.

quotes.quote: For example, SDSS J122907.3+020246 was identified as a HLIRG in goto05 . In Figure fig-F12265+0219 , we present the SDSS gri composite image of F12265+0219, which was regarded as the IRAS counterpart of SDSS J122907.3+020246 in goto05 . It is seen that SDSS J122907.3+020246 lies outside the 3 error ellipse and a bright star-like object lies within the ellipse. The star-like object within the ellipse is the famous quasar 3C 273, which is known to be associated with F12265+0219.

gmw_00001937.name: host of GRB 130702A/SN 2013dx
gmw_00001937.overview: The host of GRB 130702A/SN 2013dx is a faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1307.5103] that exhibits active star formation [arXiv:1307.5103]. It belongs to a group of galaxies [arXiv:1502.04883] and is located near a massive, metal-rich primary galaxy [arXiv:1307.5103].

sources.summary: The paper argues SDSS J142914.75+154626.0 is a faint dwarf galaxy at the GRB 130702A explosion site, with star formation and low oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: These authors also noted the existence of a nearby faint =23.01 mag source SDSS J142914.75+154626.0 separated by 0.6 '' from the optical transient, and a bright red ( 18 mag; g- 0.9 mag) galaxy SDSS J142914.57+154619.3, at a separation of 7.6 '' .

The SN-GRB exploded at an offset of 7.6'' from the center of an inclined =18.1 mag red disk-dominated galaxy, and 0.6'' from the center of a much fainter =23 mag object. We obtained Keck-II DEIMOS spectra of the two objects and find a 2 upper limit on their line-of-sight velocity offset of . The substantial offset suggests that the faint source is a separate, dwarf galaxy. The star-formation rate of the dwarf galaxy is , and we place an upper limit on its oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) < dex. The identification of an LGRB in a dwarf satellite of a massive, metal-rich primary galaxy suggests that recent detections of LGRBs spatially coincident with metal-rich galaxies may be, in some cases, superpositions.

sources.summary: The host of GRB 130702A/SN 2013dx is placed in a galaxy group: spectroscopy found nearby galaxies at the same redshift, making this an unusual GRB-SN environment.

quotes.quote: Finally, we studied the SN 2013dx environment through spectroscopy of the closeby galaxies: 9 out of the 14 inspected galaxies lie within 0.03 in redshift from z=0.145 , indicating that the host of GRB 130702A/SN 2013dx belongs to a group of galaxies, an unprecedented finding for a GRB-associated SN and, to our knowledge, for long GRBs in general.

This is clear evidence that SN 2013dx occurred in a group or a small cluster of galaxies. This conclusion is strengthened by the fact that many more galaxies in the SDSS, falling just outside the field of view of our images, have a spectroscopic or photometric redshift consistent with z=0.145 . In detail, since the angular separation among the two farthest galaxies with the same redshift in the field is 3' , the physical extent of the group at z=0.145 is 600 kpc.
